reads: If the RB wheel gets a step up the right sideline, take the shot to the wheel.
If the wheel is carried, climb to the backside post over the top.
If both are covered deep, check the underneath drag for the safe yards.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and run-biased safeties. Coach's Key: the play fake holds the linebackers so the RB wheel can leak up the sideline.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-5

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow shows the back pressing downhill into the interior gap
White blocking stems by the line working their zone steps

reads: If the front-side gap opens, hit it downhill behind the line's zone steps.
If the front side is clogged, plant and cut back to the backside crease.
Stay patient, let the blocks develop, then get vertical.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts. Coach's Key: read the first down lineman, press the line of scrimmage then cut off the zone flow.

name: snapfire_hb_power_o

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow shows the back aiming downhill behind the interior
White blocking stems for the gap scheme up front

reads: Follow the down blocks and let the play develop behind the line.
If the front-side hole shows, get downhill through it.
If it is walled, stay tight to the blocks and grind for what is there.
usage_notes: Beats: lighter boxes. Coach's Key: be patient behind the puller and hit the hole with one cut.

name: snapfire_duo-2

routes.note: Red ball-carrier line shows the back pressing straight downhill into the interior
White blocking stems by the line working vertical double teams

reads: Press the double teams downhill and read the linebacker's fit.
If the front-side displaces, run off the double team vertically.
If the linebacker fills front side, cut back behind the block.
usage_notes: Beats: stout interior fronts. Coach's Key: a downhill, vertical-push run, read the second-level fit and cut off the double team.

name: snapfire_pa_jailbreak_screen

routes.note: Red short arrow shows the screen catch point behind the line with the offensive linemen releasing out front to block (white)
Yellow straight stem up the right sideline to clear the flat

reads: Sell the fake, then get the ball out quick to the screen man behind the line.
Follow the released linemen out in front and turn up off their blocks.
If the screen is read and jumped, throw it away rather than force it.
usage_notes: Beats: heavy pass rush and aggressive ends. Coach's Key: let the rush come upfield, the released linemen become the convoy out front.

name: snapfire_pa_fk_cheat_quick_out

routes.note: Cyan motion line runs flat across underneath from the left
Yellow stem up then breaks out toward the left sideline
Yellow stem releasing and breaking out (deeper of the left pair)
Yellow short stem breaking out to the right just past the sticks
Yellow vertical stem then break out toward the right sideline

reads: After the fake, work the quick out to the play side first.
If the flat defender sits on the out, come back to the underneath crosser.
If both sides are squeezed, check it down and take the easy completion.
usage_notes: Beats: zone that widens slow. Coach's Key: the play fake cheats the linebackers, then the quick outs hit the soft flats.

name: snapfire_y_lead_read_option

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow shows the back's interior aiming point on the read
Yellow arrow alongside the red shows the keep lane off the read key
White blocking stems up front

reads: Read the unblocked end, if he crashes the back, pull and keep.
If the end stays home, hand it off downhill to the back.
Follow the lead blocker through the hole on the keep.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined edge defenders. Coach's Key: make the read defender wrong, then follow the Y lead block.

name: snapfire_pivot_dig

routes.note: Yellow stem releases vertical then breaks in across the middle on a dig
Yellow stem up the right then breaks in across the field
Yellow short stem turning back at the sticks
Red primary read shows the back's flat-to-pivot path out of the backfield
Blue route works back to the left flat

reads: Hit the RB pivot first if the flat defender bails with the digs.
If the underneath is squeezed, climb to the dig crossing the middle.
Use the backside out as your reset if the front side is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: zone that vacates the flat. Coach's Key: the pivot occupies the flat defender so the digs open behind him.

name: snapfire_dig_z_spot

routes.note: yellow, breaks across underneath to the left
red, releases vertical then breaks in across the middle, the primary read
yellow, settles in the right hook zone
yellow, breaks to the sideline
yellow, stems up the seam toward the right deep area

reads: First look the red dig over the middle as the ball comes open.
If the middle is closed, hit the spot sit-down in the right hook window.
Late, check the left drag underneath against man chasers.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage over the middle. Coach's Key: the red dig is the primary, work it off the spot underneath.

name: snapfire_zipper_gl_x_spot

routes.note: yellow, vertical up the left sideline
red, short vertical release then quick break inside, the primary
yellow, hooks back to the middle
yellow, breaks out toward the right sideline
cyan line shows pre-snap motion across the formation to the left flat

reads: Read the red zipper break first off the snap.
Work the spot curl over the middle if the zipper is covered.
Outlet to the motion back swinging to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: short zones with the zipper and spot combo. Coach's Key: cyan motion sets up the flat outlet, the red break is the trigger.

name: snapfire_slot_post

routes.note: yellow, stems up then breaks toward the middle deep
yellow, vertical stem then breaks in across the field
yellow, shallow crosser working left
red, drives vertical up the right sideline as the primary read
yellow, short crosser releasing left

reads: Hit the red vertical down the right side first.
If covered, climb to the slot post/dig working the middle.
Settle for the shallow drag underneath as the checkdown.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high with the post over the top. Coach's Key: the red right-side vertical is the trigger, posts cross behind it.

name: snapfire_zipper_mesh_spot

routes.note: yellow, releases up and bends toward the left deep area
red, runs the mesh across the middle, a primary read
red, the other half of the mesh, crossing underneath
yellow, breaks toward the right sideline
cyan, motions/releases to the left flat

reads: Read the mesh crossers rubbing through the middle first.
Take whichever crosser comes open against the leverage.
If the mesh is walled, work the out to the right or the swing back left.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the mesh rub. Coach's Key: the two red crossers are the engine, let them clear traffic then throw on the move.

name: snapfire_bench_dig_pivot

routes.note: yellow, breaks to the left sideline
yellow, vertical up the left side
red, vertical stem then deep in-break across the middle, the primary read
yellow, breaks to the right sideline
yellow, short pivot release to the right flat

reads: Look the red dig coming open over the middle first.
If the middle is filled, work the bench out to the left.
Check the pivot to the right flat underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the dig in the seam. Coach's Key: red dig is the primary, bench and pivot are the underneath answers.

name: snapfire_orbit_mesh_rail

routes.note: yellow, stems vertical then breaks toward the middle
red, runs across the middle, the primary read
yellow, shallow cross beneath the mesh
yellow, hooks back near the right hashes
cyan, orbit motion across then up the right sideline as the rail

reads: Read the red mesh crosser through the middle first.
Climb to the orbit rail up the right sideline if the crosser is covered.
Settle for the underneath drag or spot if both are taken away.
usage_notes: Beats: man with the mesh rub and the orbit rail behind it. Coach's Key: cyan orbit feeds the rail, the red mesh is the quick trigger.

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_draw

routes.note: red, takes the draw handoff and runs downhill behind the line
white blocking stems showing the draw read, linemen drop then drive
cyan and short stems, receivers stalk/clear for the runner

reads: Let the line invite the rush then follow the red draw lane downhill.
Press the inside crease and read the climbing blocks.
Bounce only if the front-side fills.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive upfield rushes. Coach's Key: this is a draw despite the name, sell pass then run the red lane.

name: snapfire_bench_return

routes.note: yellow, breaks to the left sideline
red, vertical up the left side, the primary/clearout
yellow, breaks in across the middle
yellow, vertical stem then comeback toward the ball
yellow, short crosser working left

reads: Clear the deep defender with the red vertical up the left.
Work the bench out and the dig over the middle.
Come back to the curl/return on the right as the ball settles.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the return/comeback after the vertical clears. Coach's Key: red streak opens the underneath windows, take the bench or return.

name: snapfire_pa_stretch

routes.note: Red line, runs straight up the left sideline as the primary deep shot.
Yellow, releases upfield then breaks out toward the left numbers at the second level.
Yellow, vertical stem then breaks out toward the left at a deeper level.
Blue line shows the back working out to the right flat after the fake.

reads: IF the safety bites on the play-action fake, THEN take the backside Go up the left sideline.
IF the deep shot is covered, THEN work the two right-side out breaking routes crossing back to the left.
IF underneath defenders sink, THEN check the back leaking to the right flat.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high man with a run-fit safety. Coach's Key: sell the stretch fake first, then climb to the backside vertical.

name: snapfire_hb_zone_wk_alert_bubble

routes.note: Yellow line straight up the left sideline as the alert.
Yellow, bubbles back and out from the slot as the quick RPO throw.
Red is the ball carrier path, zone run track to the right side.

reads: IF the box is loaded, THEN throw the bubble to the slot now.
IF the read defender stays wide on the bubble, THEN hand the weak zone run.
IF the corner over the alert WR squats, THEN the backside Go is the deep alert shot.
usage_notes: Beats: stacked boxes that ignore the slot. Coach's Key: read the conflict defender, bubble vs give, with the Go as the alert.

name: snapfire_mtn_flood

routes.note: Yellow route breaking flat across the field toward the left sideline
Yellow route running up and across from the left side
Yellow route stems vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline at intermediate depth
Cyan route releasing wide to the left flat
Blue route working back toward the right sideline

reads: If the flat defender sinks, hit the underneath flat release for easy yards.
If the corner squats, climb to the out or comeback on the right.
If the middle opens, work the crosser coming across the field.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: flood one side with three levels and read the flat defender high to low.

name: snapfire_sft_mtn_pa_y_fork

routes.note: Yellow route across to the left flat
Yellow route stemming up and breaking across from the left
Yellow route up the right side then breaking back inside/up (fork-style release)
Cyan route releasing underneath after the fake

reads: Sell the play action fake first, then climb the dropback.
If the deep defender carries the vertical, dump the underneath flat.
If coverage rolls one way, work the backside crosser.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high looks off play action. Coach's Key: the Y-fork stresses the seam defender, read him then bail to the back.

name: snapfire_sft_mtn_zone_wk

routes.note: White blocks flowing toward the weak (left) side
Red ball-carrier path aiming for the weak-side gap
Cyan block path working to the weak side ahead of the back

reads: Take the snap and aim for the weak-side gap.
If the front flows hard, cut back behind the down blocks.
Follow the lead blocker and get vertical once you clear the line.
usage_notes: Beats: over-pursuing fronts. Coach's Key: zone weak, read the first down lineman and press the hole.

name: snapfire_strong_power

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path: back starts in the pistol, presses downhill and cuts to the right A/B gap behind the line.

reads: IF the right interior gap is clean, THEN hit it downhill behind the blocks.
IF the gap is clogged, THEN press it then bounce off the down block.
usage_notes: Beats: base run fronts. Coach's Key: follow the pulling blocker through the right interior gap, hit it north and south.

name: snapfire_hb_zone

routes.note: Red line: ball-carrier takes the handoff and presses the line behind the right A/B gap, drawn as a short red track angling up to the line of scrimmage.
White blocking marks across the front; line steps in zone.

reads: Aim for the playside gap behind the line block.
If the front flows hard, cut back to the open lane.
Get downhill, this is a one-cut zone run.
usage_notes: Beats: gap-shooting fronts. Coach's Key: simple inside zone, take what the blocks give and stay vertical.

name: snapfire_counter_y

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path: starts deep in the pistol backfield, takes a counter step then cuts back up and to the left into the left interior (A/B) gap, drawn as a short red arrow angling up-left.
Upper backfield dot in the pistol alignment, gives the ball to the back.
White block lines across the line of scrimmage. Interior blockers down-block, with a puller sweeping down and across to the right, the pulling/kick action that defines a counter front.
Two long vertical white lines on the left side: receivers blocking downfield in front of the run, not running routes.
Long vertical white line on the far right: backside receiver blocking downfield.

reads: If the puller kicks out the edge, then bounce the run outside the block.
If the edge squeezes inside, then cut it up vertically through the left interior gap.
If a backside defender chases, then stay tight off the down blocks and get north.
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing or over-pursuing fronts. Coach's Key: it is a counter to the left, follow the pulling blocker and hit the gap downhill.

name: snapfire_orbit_triple_option

routes.note: Red arrow shows the keep lane straight ahead off the mesh
Yellow give path right at the line, the read key
Cyan path loops back behind the QB as the pitch option
Cyan path arcing to the right perimeter
White vertical stem on the far left

reads: IF the dive key crashes the HB, THEN pull and keep on the red lane.
IF the next defender squeezes the QB, THEN pitch to the orbit man cutting outside.
IF the edge stays soft, THEN the HB give on the mesh is live.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined edge defenders. Coach's Key: read it in order, dive then keep then pitch, do not pre-decide.

name: snapfire_hb_dive

routes.note: Red arrow straight downhill through the A gap behind the QB
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front

reads: IF the A gap is open, THEN hit it downhill now.
IF the gap clogs, THEN press the line and cut off the down blocks.
Keep pad level low, this is a fast interior hitter.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft interior fronts. Coach's Key: quick-hitting A gap dive, get vertical fast.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_flat

routes.note: Yellow path up into the interior, the run portion of the RPO
Red line running flat to the left across the field, the alert throw
Short white-tipped stem to the left
Short stem on the right

reads: IF the box is loaded, THEN pull and throw the red alert route to the flat side.
IF the box is light, THEN hand off the inside run.
Read the conflict defender, his move tells you give or throw.
usage_notes: Beats: defenders who chase the run and vacate the flat. Coach's Key: pre-snap box count sets the give-or-throw.

name: snapfire_orbit_hb_split_zone

routes.note: Red carry path downhill behind the line
Cyan loops behind the QB and out to the right edge, split-zone seal / flare
White vertical stem on the far left
White block tics across the front

reads: IF the front flows with the zone, THEN cut off the split-zone block.
IF the backside crashes, THEN the orbit man wraps to seal the edge.
Stay patient, let the zone displace the front before you cut.
usage_notes: Beats: over-pursuing backside ends. Coach's Key: the orbit motion handles the cutback defender, trust the split block.

name: snapfire_te_cross

routes.note: Red route stemming up then crossing back to the left, the primary read
Yellow breaking flat to the left sideline
Yellow breaking out to the left underneath
Yellow crossing from right to left underneath
Yellow breaking up and to the right toward the sideline

reads: IF the middle opens, THEN work the red TE crosser as the primary.
IF coverage carries the cross, THEN check the right corner route up top.
IF underneath defenders sink, THEN dump to the out-breaking flat routes.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and rubbed-off zone defenders. Coach's Key: the TE crosser is the answer, let it clear the trash.

name: snapfire_jet_qb_zone

routes.note: Takes the zone path behind the line off the jet action
Motions across the formation, a decoy that pulls the edge defender
Covered drive, uncovered combo and climb to the linebacker
Wall off the perimeter defenders

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Empty QB-run look, the jet motion stretches the edge and counts a hat for the box
**1️⃣ Jet pulls the edge:** the QB keeps on the zone path inside
**Back's read:** bang the A gap, bend the B gap, bounce outside if the front overflows
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Light boxes and edge defenders who chase the jet motion
**Coach's Key:** Needs a mobile QB, this is a quarterback-carrier run

name: snapfire_jet_qb_zone_wk

routes.note: Runs the zone path to the weak side off the jet action
Motions across to hold the play-side edge
Covered drive, uncovered combo and climb
Wall the perimeter

reads: **Pre-Snap:** The weak-side mirror of Jet QB Zone, call it to run away from the defense's strength
**1️⃣ Jet holds the edge:** keep the QB on the weak-side zone path
**Back's read:** bang, bend, bounce off the line's movement
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Defenses that set strength to the jet motion, you run away from it
**Coach's Key:** Mobile QB required

name: snapfire_hb_counter_u

routes.note: Two white vertical blocking stems walling off defenders downfield
White down-blocking angle to the right with the line stepping that way
Red primary, takes the handoff and cuts back to the left behind the pull

reads: Open the give to the right to sell flow, then counter back left
Follow the pulling blocker through the hole
If the front overflows, press it back inside off the down blocks
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Fast-flowing fronts that chase initial action
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, the misdirection sets up the cutback

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_base

routes.note: White vertical blocking stem up the left side
Cyan short motion then a flat sealing path across
Red primary, downhill run angling up and to the right behind the lead

reads: Take the handoff and follow the lead blocker downhill
Read the playside block and press the open gap
Cut off the seal if the front squeezes the hole
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Light boxes that give up the playside gap
**Coach's Key:** This is a base power run, hit it downhill and read your lead

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_wheel

routes.note: Cyan motion across the formation then turning up the sideline on a wheel
Blue route breaking back up and out to the sideline
Yellow stem up the left side
Red route working down the left edge as a deep target

reads: Watch the wheel develop off the motion up the sideline
If the flat defender carries the wheel, the underneath blue route opens
Take the backside vertical or comeback if the wheel side is capped
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Defenders who squat on the flat and get beat up the sideline
**Coach's Key:** Let the wheel clear, throw it over the trailing defender

name: snapfire_speed_option-2

routes.note: Two white vertical blocking stems walling off defenders
Red primary, attacks the left edge reading the pitch key
Yellow pitch path running outside and to the left in pitch relationship

reads: Attack the edge defender on the option
If he plays the QB, pitch it to the yellow back
If he widens to the pitch, keep it and turn upfield
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Slow or undisciplined edge defenders forced to choose
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, get downhill and make the key wrong

name: snapfire_spacing_x_wheel

routes.note: Red primary read, short stem then turns up the field along the left numbers
Yellow, climbs vertically up the left side
Yellow, breaks out toward the left sideline underneath
Yellow, short hook settling over the ball
Yellow, runs the full width of the field across the intermediate level

reads: Work the red wheel first: if the defender squats on the underneath spacing, the wheel runs by him up the sideline.
If the wheel is carried, drop your eyes to the spacing triangle (flat, curl) and take the open horizontal window.
If everything underneath is covered, the backside over route is your late crossing answer.
usage_notes: Beats: zone and squat coverage. Coach's Key: the spacing routes hold underneath defenders flat-footed so the wheel can climb behind them.

name: snapfire_te_slot_cross

routes.note: Yellow stem up then hooks back to the left sideline.
Yellow shallow drag working left underneath.
Yellow short route breaking back to the left across the formation.
Red primary read, stems up then crosses the field to the left at depth.
Yellow vertical stem breaking back toward the middle.

reads: Work the red TE crosser across the middle as the primary.
If the crosser is bracketed, drop to the shallow drag underneath.
The comeback and back-side breaker are the outlets if the middle is flooded.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with a rub. Coach's Key: the TE crosser off the shallow drag stresses defenders crossing through traffic.

name: snapfire_dbl_ins

routes.note: Yellow stem upfield then hooks back to the left.
Yellow drives up then breaks in to the left across the middle.
Red primary read, shallow route working left underneath across the formation.
Yellow stem up then breaks back inside upfield.
Yellow drives up then breaks in to the left across the middle.

reads: Start on the red shallow drag working left underneath.
Climb to the dig routes breaking in over the middle if the drag is covered.
Take the deepest open in-breaker, the double ins stack the middle of the field.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: stacking in-breakers at two levels gives a high-low read across the middle.

name: snapfire_go_s_y_shake

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline.
Red vertical upfield, the marked primary deep shot.
Yellow stems up then breaks back to the left across the middle, the shake route.
Yellow vertical upfield (seam).
Yellow vertical up the right sideline.

reads: Eyes on the red streak from the slot as the primary deep shot.
If the deep ball is covered, work the Y shake breaking back across the middle.
The outside streaks clear the sidelines and isolate the shake underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high man. Coach's Key: four verticals occupy the deep defenders while the Y shake works the vacated middle.

name: snapfire_mesh_spot

routes.note: Yellow; runs straight vertical up the left sideline to clear the side
Yellow; shallow crossing route running across the formation at the line
Yellow; settles into the spot/hook over the middle then breaks back out
Red primary read; releases to the right and works out toward the flat
Yellow; runs vertical up the right sideline

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Classic mesh-spot: a shallow crosser under a spot settle with the back to the flat
**1️⃣ Primary:** Hit the back in the flat on the right; it is the marked read vs man or spilling defenders
**2️⃣ Spot:** Drop to the spot/curl over the middle vs zone underneath
**3️⃣ Mesh:** The shallow crosser is your on-the-move man-beater if the flat is squatted
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage with the mesh, zone with the spot. **Coach's Key:** Flat is the primary, work the spot vs zone.

name: snapfire_deep_crossers

routes.note: Yellow; releases up then crosses to the right at depth
Red primary read; runs the opposite deep crosser back to the left at depth
Yellow; pushes vertical then breaks toward the left sideline
Yellow; pushes vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline
Blue; works a shallow angle out to the right flat as the underneath option

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Two deep crossers tie together over the middle at the same depth
**1️⃣ Primary:** Read the marked crosser; throw it on the open window as it clears the middle
**2️⃣ Mirror:** If the first crosser is bracketed, climb to the opposite crosser working the other way
**3️⃣ Check-down:** Use the flat/out underneath if the deep crossers are walled off
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone over the middle and man rub. **Coach's Key:** Read the crossers high to low and let them clear before you throw.

name: snapfire_cheat_slash_wheel_cross

routes.note: Yellow; runs straight vertical up the left sideline
Cyan motion across the formation then turns straight upfield
Yellow; works up then breaks in across the middle at intermediate depth
Yellow; releases up the right sideline on a wheel then turns up
Yellow; works out toward the right sideline underneath

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Use the cyan motion to read leverage and confirm man or zone
**1️⃣ Wheel:** Hit the wheel up the right sideline when the flat defender carries inside or man chases the motion
**2️⃣ Cross:** Work the dig/cross over the middle as the intermediate window opens
**3️⃣ Verticals:** The go and motion vertical stretch the top if the underneath defenders sink
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage off the motion and wheel. **Coach's Key:** Motion to ID the look, then take the wheel or the cross.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-2

routes.note: Cyan; comes in motion across the backfield and takes the short touch pass on the move toward the right
White block; blocks the perimeter for the jet runner
White block; blocks down for the jet
White block; seals the edge defender on the right for the jet to turn up

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Time the jet motion so the runner is moving at the catch point
**1️⃣ Touch pass:** Flip the short touch pass to the jet man on the move; he is the only ball-carrier
**2️⃣ Edge:** Read the perimeter blocks and let the jet runner press the edge then turn upfield
usage_notes: **Beats:** Slow-flowing edge defenders and soft contain. **Coach's Key:** It is a touch pass, not a route concept, the jet man takes it on the move behind the stalk blocks.

name: snapfire_te_corner

routes.note: Yellow; runs straight vertical up the left sideline
Yellow; shallow route working in across the formation
Red primary read; pushes vertical then breaks out to the deep corner
Yellow; releases up and bends out toward the right on a wheel/out
Yellow; pushes upfield then breaks out toward the right sideline

reads: **Pre-Snap:** The TE corner is the featured route; check the safety leverage to that side
**1️⃣ Primary:** Throw the TE corner into the boundary window vs single-high or beaten leverage
**2️⃣ High-low:** Pair the corner over the underneath out/wheel to stress the flat defender
**3️⃣ Backside:** The drag and backside go give you a man-beater and a clear-out if the corner is doubled
usage_notes: **Beats:** Single-high and squat-flat coverage. **Coach's Key:** TE corner is the read, work it high-low with the underneath out.

name: snapfire_posts

routes.note: Yellow breaking back toward the left flat
Red, drives up the field
Yellow releasing toward the left sideline
Yellow zig stem working back inside deep
Yellow climbing up and inside deep
Yellow short release out of the backfield

reads: Read the safety leverage in the middle of the field.
If the safety stays deep, work the underneath crossers.
If the safety drives down, take the post behind him.
Check the back down if the rush gets home.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high safety looks. Coach's Key: high-low the safety with the deep post over the crossers. ART VERIFIED: PASS label confirmed; left-side drags/outs, red vertical stem, right-side zig/dig and a deep vertical up top, plus RB release all drawn. Confidence honestly LOW (route mix is busy and the 'Posts' name only loosely matches), kept as authored.

name: snapfire_hb_quick_base-2

routes.note: White vertical stems, block their men down the field
Red ball-carrier path taking the handoff and pressing behind the line

reads: Snap and read your base blocks up front.
If the front blocks down clean, hit the interior gap fast.
If the gap clogs, bounce off the block and stay north-south.
Get downhill, this is a quick-hitter.
usage_notes: Beats: light or spread-out fronts. Coach's Key: base blocking, take the first clean gap and go downhill. ART VERIFIED: RUN label confirmed; two white downfield blocks on the left, red HB ball-carrier path present. Note: the red path angles inside/short, original 'presses to the right' wording softened to 'behind the line' to stay honest to the art.

name: snapfire_fk_toss_slide

routes.note: yellow breaks out toward the left sideline
red primary, works back across underneath after the fake
yellow climbs across the middle to the deep left
yellow up and back to the right sideline
yellow sells the toss then leaks up the right sideline

reads: Sell the toss fake then reset eyes to the crossing primary.
If the cross is covered, climb to the deep crosser over the top.
If the defense flows hard to the fake, hit the HB leaking up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-flow defenses biting on the toss action. Coach's Key: the fake holds the linebackers, the crosser comes open behind them.

name: snapfire_hb_mtn_dig_spot

routes.note: cyan motion and shallow path working across underneath from the left
yellow stems vertical then breaks in at intermediate depth
yellow breaks back to the right at medium depth
red primary read, vertical up the right side
yellow releases and works to the right flat

reads: Primary is the vertical up the right side, take the shot if single-high.
If the vertical is capped, settle on the dig over the middle.
If underneath defenders carry the dig, dump the shallow or the HB flat.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the shallow rub and zone with the dig sitting down. Coach's Key: vertical first, dig second, shallow as the outlet.

name: snapfire_speed_option_wk

routes.note: red path attacking the weak side edge, reads the pitch key
yellow trails wide as the pitch man on the option
white blocks, the playside line works to the second level
white vertical, blocks the backside corner

reads: Attack the weak side edge and read the pitch key (force defender).
If the key crashes the QB, pitch it to the back outside.
If the key stays wide to play the pitch, keep it upfield.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined edge defenders to the weak side. Coach's Key: make the force man wrong, pitch off his shoulder.

name: snapfire_whip_dig

routes.note: Yellow runs out and underneath then whips back inside on the left
Yellow short pivot underneath off the left edge of the trips
Red shallow crosser running left to right: primary read
Yellow stems vertical then breaks back across on the deep dig
Yellow short vertical then settles back toward the ball on the right

reads: Hit the red crosser first if it comes open across the middle.
If the underneath defenders jump the cross, work the whip routes settling underneath.
Late shot is the deep dig coming back across the field.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and over-pursuing underneath defenders. Coach's Key: the whips break back the opposite way to lose man defenders.

name: snapfire_mtn_y_poco

routes.note: Cyan motion across the formation, then breaks up the seam toward the post/corner
Yellow stems up then breaks out to the left sideline at depth
Yellow breaks out to the left at a shallower depth
Yellow breaks in toward the middle from the right
Yellow vertical up the right side

reads: Use the motion to ID man or zone, then take the Y up the seam if the middle is open.
If the safety sits on the Y, work the deep out to the left sideline.
Check the in route over the middle against soft inside leverage.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverage by stressing the seam off motion. Coach's Key: the Y motion reads the defense before the snap, then attacks the post/corner window.

name: snapfire_mtn_corners

routes.note: Both break to the corners off the verticals
Cyan motion then up the seam
Clears the middle to open the corners
Outlet underneath

reads: Read the safeties: two high opens a corner outside the half safety
Throw the corner away from the rotation
Take the checkdown if both corners are capped
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Two-high shells (the corners sit outside the safeties)
**Coach’s Key:** Put air under the corner, lead it to the sideline

name: snapfire_te_whip

routes.note: Yellow shallow crossing in from the left sideline
Yellow stems up the field and bends in toward the deep middle
Yellow short break, whips underneath out to the left
Yellow stems vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline at depth
Red underneath route working across to the right: primary read

reads: Hit the red whip route first as it works open underneath.
If the underneath is covered, climb to the post over the middle.
Work the deep out to the right sideline as the late progression.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage on the TE with the whip break. Coach's Key: the whip snaps back against trailing man, take the quick window now.

name: snapfire_escort_hb_split_zone

routes.note: White, line steps in zone toward the play side
Cyan, crosses the formation to kick out / wham the backside edge
Red, takes the handoff and reads the backside cutback off the escort block

reads: IF the backside edge crashes, THEN the escort kicks him and the back cuts behind it.
IF the front flows hard play side, THEN bend it back into the split zone lane.
Stay patient and follow the zone blocks before pressing the cut.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Fast-flowing front sevens. **Coach's Key:** The escort (split-zone) block opens the backside cutback; read the kicked-out edge.

name: snapfire_x_spot

routes.note: Yellow, breaks flat to the left sideline as the backside isolation
Red primary, settles into the spot window underneath
Yellow, stems up and breaks to the deep right corner over the spot
Yellow, releases and swings wide to the right flat

reads: Read the triangle: corner over the spot over the flat to the right.
IF the flat defender drops to the corner, THEN throw the spot sitting underneath.
IF the flat defender jumps the spot, THEN throw the corner behind him.
IF nothing develops, THEN check the backside out to the X.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone coverage with the spot-corner-flat triangle. **Coach's Key:** Read the flat defender; spot is the primary, corner is the shot.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-6

routes.note: White, full line zone steps in unison toward the play side
Red, takes the handoff aiming inside and reads the first down lineman

reads: Aim for the inside gap and read the playside down lineman.
IF the front gets reached, THEN press the front-side lane.
IF the defender crosses your face, THEN cut back into the open zone behind the block.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Spread-out fronts and light boxes. **Coach's Key:** Pure inside zone, press the line and take the cut the blocks give you.

name: snapfire_te_corner-2

routes.note: Out of the bunch, the corner off the rub
Red primary, the deep shot up the middle
Verticals to clear and an out underneath off the bunch rub
Blue release to the right flat as the checkdown

reads: The bunch creates a natural rub for the TE corner
Take the red vertical if the middle is open
Work the corner off the rub, then the RB flat
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Man coverage (the bunch rub springs the corner)
**Coach’s Key:** Let the rub happen, the TE corner comes clean

name: snapfire_curl_flat_corner

routes.note: Red primary, vertical stem then curls back at the sticks
Yellow, breaks underneath to the left flat
Yellow, breaks flat to the left sideline over the flat route
Yellow, swings out of the backfield and up the right sideline

reads: Read the curl-flat high-low on the flat defender.
IF the flat defender sinks to the curl, THEN throw the flat underneath.
IF the flat defender jumps the flat, THEN hit the curl sitting behind him.
IF the coverage rotates, THEN climb to the HB on the right.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone with the curl-flat stretch. **Coach's Key:** Classic curl-flat read; the curl is the primary versus soft corners.

name: snapfire_power_read-2

routes.note: Read the end man on the line; keep through the B gap behind the wrap when he widens
The give; hits the edge with blockers in front when the read key crashes
Pull through the hole to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules with a double team up to the backside linebacker
Stalk the perimeter for the sweep

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A loaded box is fine here: the end man on the line goes unblocked and becomes the read key, so the extra defender is in conflict
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** give the sweep, the back wins the edge with blockers in front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside or upfield:** keep it, QB power downhill through the B gap behind the wrapping guard
**🚨 No pass option:** this is a run-run option (also called inverted veer), the only decision is give or keep
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense (the read key plays the QB, crashes inside, and the sweep runs free)
**Coach's Key:** Hold the keep button (X on PlayStation, A on Xbox) when the read key stands up; do nothing and the give happens on its own

name: snapfire_spot_slot_out

routes.note: Yellow straight vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow stem up the seam then break out toward the right sideline at depth.
Yellow short stem then break flat to the right sideline, the underneath out.
Red. Stems up then works back out toward the right sideline, marked as the primary read.

reads: First look the primary out on the right side, throw it on the break.
If the flat defender jumps it, climb to the corner/out breaking behind him.
If both are covered outside, come back to the left Go on the backside.
usage_notes: Beats: zone and soft corners. Coach's Key: high-low the right sideline, take the open level off the flat defender.

name: snapfire_strong_flood

routes.note: Primary, the high route of the flood to the right
Intermediate level of the flood
Low route of the flood, swings to the right flat
Backside crossing route
Backside crossing route working across

reads: IF the flat defender carries the corner, THEN drop it to the flat underneath.
IF the flat defender jumps the flat, THEN take the corner over the top.
IF the right side is bracketed, THEN come back to the backside in routes.
usage_notes: Beats: Zone coverage, the three-level flood puts a defender in a high-low bind on the right. Coach's Key: Flood the right with three levels, read the flat defender high-low. Verified: art labeled PASS, red deep-corner to the right + intermediate out + flat + two backside in routes all drawn.

name: snapfire_mtn_curls

routes.note: Primary, comes back to the ball on the left
Settles in the open zone
Motions and runs a shallow crosser, the underneath outlet
Breaks to the right sideline
Swings out as a checkdown

reads: IF the curl defender sinks deep, THEN hit the curl in front of him.
IF the underneath jumps the curl, THEN take the drag running across.
IF nothing settles, THEN check the back.
usage_notes: Beats: Soft zone coverage, the curls sit in the windows while the drag clears underneath. Coach's Key: Find the open curl window, the drag is the moving relief. Verified: art labeled PASS, red curl + yellow curl + cyan drag + out + RB outlet all drawn.

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_read

routes.note: Primary, works across the middle off the play action
Clears the deep zone on the left
Breaks to the right deep corner

routes.route: Swing / flat right after the fake (cyan)
routes.note: Releases to the right flat following the play fake
Sells the run fake then sets to pass

reads: IF the play fake pulls the linebackers up, THEN throw the crossing dig behind them.
IF the safety jumps the cross, THEN take the corner or the streak over the top.
IF the pocket collapses, THEN check the back in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: Aggressive run-fitting linebackers, the play action pulls them up and opens the crosser. Coach's Key: Sell the run first, the dig comes open behind the second level. Verified: art labeled PASS, red crossing dig + yellow streak + corner + cyan RB swing + white PA protection all drawn.

name: snapfire_45_quick_base

routes.note: Angles to the left A gap on a fast-hitting track
Down and base blocks to seal the interior

reads: IF the interior gap is sealed, THEN hit it downhill fast.
IF the gap is muddied, THEN cut off the base block to daylight.
Quick hitter, get north now.
usage_notes: Beats: Slow-reacting interior fronts, the quick base hits before the defense flows. Coach's Key: Fast track to the left A gap, no hesitation. Verified: art labeled RUN, red quick-dive to the left A gap + white blocking drawn, both listed routes present.

name: snapfire_read_option

routes.note: Ball carrier path up the middle to the right side
QB pulls and bounces left off the read
Edge read defender left unblocked

reads: IF the read defender crashes the dive, THEN pull and keep to the left edge.
IF the read defender stays on the QB, THEN give the dive.
Make the edge defender wrong, get downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: Crashing edge defenders, the read keeps the backside end honest. Coach's Key: Read the unblocked edge, give or keep off his crash. Verified: art labeled RUN, red dive + yellow QB keep-left + white blocking all drawn.

name: snapfire_45_quick_base-2

routes.note: Red arrow showing the ball carrier hitting straight through the interior gap
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White vertical stem on the single side

reads: Take the snap and aim for the interior gap on the red track.
Read the down blocks up front and press the line of scrimmage.
Cut off the first defender that shows and get north.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft interior. Coach's Key: quick-hitting downhill run, no hesitation at the mesh. VERIFIED: art shows RUN label, red interior run arrow, and three white stalk-block stems (left outside, left slot, right backside). All four routes present, type matches.

name: snapfire_texans_dig

routes.note: Red primary, releases vertical then breaks hard across the middle (the dig)
Yellow route stemming up then breaking to the sideline
Blue route releasing out of the backfield to the flat
In-breaking route up the seam then breaking in over the middle (drawn blue in art, not yellow as authored noted, but the in-breaking route itself is present)
Yellow route stretching the sideline deep

reads: Open to the dig from the left, that is the primary.
If the middle is closed, come off to the right slot in-breaker.
Check the back in the flat if the coverage sinks deep.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and soft middle zones. Coach's Key: the red dig is the throw, work it off the safety. VERIFIED: art shows PASS label, red deep dig left, yellow out from left slot, blue flat from RB, blue in-breaker from right slot, yellow vertical right. All five routes present, type matches. Only a color-note inaccuracy on the right-slot route (blue vs yellow), not an invented route.

name: snapfire_x_close_pa_slants

routes.note: Red primary, the deep in-breaker behind the linebackers off play-action
Yellow in-breakers coming back across at two levels
Blue release to the flat after the fake
Sell the run, then protect

reads: Sell the play-action to hold the linebackers
Throw the red deep dig in the window the fake opened
Work the second in-breaker or the checkdown if covered
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Run-keying linebackers (the fake holds them, the digs come behind)
**Coach’s Key:** Ride the fake, the red dig is the throw

name: snapfire_duo-3

routes.note: Red ball-carrier line straight downhill behind the line, the primary run track.
White blocking lines across the front, vertical double teams up to the second level.

reads: Take the snap and press the line of scrimmage downhill on the red track.
Read the double teams and the linebacker flow, cut off the displaced defender.
Stay north-south and fall forward, this is a gap-scheme grinder.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and undersized fronts. Coach's Key: vertical double teams, let the back read the linebacker off the combo. Verified: RUN label matches, red downhill track + white double-team blocks drawn.

name: snapfire_hb_direct_snap

routes.note: Red ball-carrier line taking the ball straight ahead into the interior.
Green/dark lead line in front of the back, kicking out the front-side defender.

reads: Snap goes direct to the back, hit the interior gap immediately on the red track.
Follow the lead block to seal the front-side defender.
Get downhill and finish, this is a quick-hitting interior run.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses keying the QB and slow to fit the box. Coach's Key: direct snap removes the mesh, hit it now behind the lead. Verified: RUN label matches, red interior run track + green lead block drawn.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_out-2

routes.note: Yellow drive then break out to the left flat
Yellow run track, the give read on the zone run
Red alert out breaking to the right sideline, the throw option

reads: IF the box is light, THEN hand off on the inside zone.
IF the box is loaded, THEN pull and throw the alert out to the right.
IF the flat defender sits, THEN take the give instead.
usage_notes: Beats: loaded boxes and soft outside leverage. Coach's Key: read the box count first, alert out is the pre-snap answer versus a stacked front.

name: snapfire_cheat_slash_wheel_in

routes.note: Yellow breaking back out to the left sideline
Yellow shallow working across to the right
Cyan motion into a vertical/wheel stem straight up the middle/left
Yellow up then breaking back out to the right sideline
Yellow release out of the backfield, slashing toward the line

reads: IF the motion wheel runs free vertically, THEN take the shot.
IF the wheel is carried, THEN work the in/drag crossing underneath.
IF coverage sinks deep, THEN check the back-side comebacks.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage that loses the motion. Coach's Key: motion the Y, then read the wheel over the crosser. Art is busy, lines overlap.

name: snapfire_mtn_te_middle_screen

routes.note: Yellow up the left sideline
Short yellow hitch settling near the line
Yellow up the right sideline
Gray/white screen, TE settles over the middle with linemen releasing
Cyan swing out to the left flat
Red shallow working to the right underneath

reads: IF the rush clears upfield, THEN deliver the TE middle screen behind the blockers.
IF the middle is congested, THEN check the RB swing to the left.
IF the screen is read, THEN throw it away or take the underneath cross.
usage_notes: Beats: heavy interior pressure. Coach's Key: let the rush come, then drop it to the TE over the middle.

name: snapfire_redzone_scissors

routes.note: Red = primary read. Drives up from inside and breaks across and up toward the right corner, the scissors over route.
Yellow. Stems vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline, crossing under the red route (scissors).
Yellow. Stems up toward the 20 then breaks to the left sideline.
Yellow. Short vertical then breaks back inside across the middle.

reads: Read the red primary first. The two right-side routes cross (scissors), so take whichever clears.
If the corner sits on the out, throw the red post behind him.
If the safety jumps the post, come back to the right out underneath.
Left side out and dig are the backside answers vs rotation.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and man coverage in the red zone. Coach's Key: the crossing right-side releases force a defender to choose, throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_power_read-3

routes.note: Read the end man on the line; keep through the B gap behind the wrap when he widens
The give; hits the edge with blockers in front when the read key crashes
Pull through the hole to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules with a double team up to the backside linebacker
Stalk the perimeter for the sweep

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A loaded box is fine here: the end man on the line goes unblocked and becomes the read key, so the extra defender is in conflict
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** give the sweep, the back wins the edge with blockers in front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside or upfield:** keep it, QB power downhill through the B gap behind the wrapping guard
**🚨 No pass option:** this is a run-run option (also called inverted veer), the only decision is give or keep
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense (the read key plays the QB, crashes inside, and the sweep runs free)
**Coach's Key:** Hold the keep button (X on PlayStation, A on Xbox) when the read key stands up; do nothing and the give happens on its own

name: snapfire_rpo_glance_post

routes.note: Yellow drive then break out to the left flat
Yellow run track, the give read
Red slant/glance bending to the post over the middle, the pull-and-throw answer

reads: IF the box is light, THEN hand off the inside zone.
IF the box is loaded or the safety sits low, THEN pull and throw the glance post.
IF the post window closes, THEN give the ball.
usage_notes: Beats: stacked boxes and aggressive safeties. Coach's Key: read the box, the glance post hits behind the crashing defenders.

name: snapfire_double_slant

routes.note: Red slant breaking inside to the middle, the primary read
Yellow slant breaking inside underneath
Yellow up the right sideline
Yellow breaking out to the right sideline at the second level

reads: IF the outside leverage is soft, THEN hit the red slant first.
IF the defender jumps the slant, THEN throw the second slant underneath.
IF the middle is crowded, THEN work the out to the right.
usage_notes: Beats: off-coverage and zone. Coach's Key: quick game, read the outside slant to the inside slant on rhythm.

name: snapfire_escape

routes.note: Yellow shallow working across to the right
Yellow up the seam from the left
Red breaking out to the right sideline, the primary read
Yellow breaking out to the right sideline deeper
Yellow release out of the backfield toward the right

reads: IF the right out is open against the leverage, THEN take the primary.
IF the flat is covered, THEN climb to the seam up the middle.
IF coverage sinks, THEN check the back-side drag underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: zone and soft flats to the right. Coach's Key: read the out high to low, the drag is the escape valve.

name: snapfire_empty_mtn_hb_swing

routes.note: Yellow short release breaking back inside over the middle right
Yellow shallow crossing to the right sideline
Cyan swing from empty across the formation to the left flat

reads: IF the swing has grass to the left flat, THEN take it and run.
IF the flat defender jumps it, THEN come back to the Y whip over the middle.
IF both are covered, THEN climb to the shallow crosser right.
usage_notes: Beats: zone that vacates the flat from an empty look. Coach's Key: empty spreads the defense, then swing the back to space.

name: snapfire_y_sail

routes.note: Yellow up then break out to the left sideline
Short yellow settling/flat over the middle left
Red up then breaking out toward the right corner, the sail and primary read
Yellow up the right sideline clearing the corner
Yellow release out of the backfield to the right flat

reads: IF the corner carries the vertical, THEN throw the Y sail underneath into the void.
IF the flat defender widens, THEN drop to the RB flat.
IF the right is bracketed, THEN work the back-side out on the left.
usage_notes: Beats: Cover 3 and zone flats. Coach's Key: high-low the corner, the sail sits between the corner and flat defenders.

name: snapfire_mtn_inside_zone

routes.note: White vertical block stems showing the line stepping playside on inside zone.
Cyan ball-carrier path taking the handoff and pressing the interior, bending back to the left.
Red short angle blocking up into the second level at the point of attack.
Cyan tail from the right showing the back's alignment and flow into the run.

reads: IF the front flows playside, THEN the back cuts back behind the red lead block.
IF the interior holds, THEN press downhill through the A or B gap.
IF the edge crashes, THEN bend it back to the open cyan lane.
IF the second level fills, THEN stay tight off the line's hip.
usage_notes: Beats: an overflowing front. Coach's Key: classic inside zone, read the first down lineman and let the cutback develop.

name: snapfire_pin_pull_toss

routes.note: White block stems showing playside down blocks and a backside pull tracking flat to the right.
Red ball-carrier path taking the toss and bending out toward the right edge behind the pullers.

reads: IF the pullers seal the edge, THEN bounce it outside and get vertical.
IF the playside down blocks pin inside, THEN press the alley behind them.
IF the edge sets hard outside, THEN cut it up inside the pull.
IF the corner crashes, THEN string it to the sideline behind your blockers.
usage_notes: Beats: a soft or wide edge. Coach's Key: pin the inside, pull around it, follow the blocks to the corner.

name: snapfire_mtn_duo

routes.note: White block stems and the cyan combo bracket showing the playside double team working to the linebacker.
Red vertical ball-carrier track pressing straight downhill behind the double teams.
White stems climbing up to the second level on the right.

reads: IF the double team displaces the down lineman, THEN run off the doubled block's vertical push.
IF the linebacker fills hard, THEN press it and read his color.
IF the front spills, THEN stay tight and ride the double team.
IF the second level scrapes over, THEN cut off the climbing block.
usage_notes: Beats: a two-gapping front. Coach's Key: duo is a downhill double-team run, press it and read the backer off the combo.

name: snapfire_fk_toss_lead_swing_cross

routes.note: Yellow line runs straight up the field from the left sideline.

Primary read. Releases up to about the 20, then breaks hard back across the field to the left, finishing with the arrow upfield. The red primary-read line traces this same crosser into the open window on the left.
Yellow vertical up the right side near the right hash.
Yellow line loops down out of the backfield and releases to the right flat (the Swing).
White line shows the lead block path to the right side, selling the toss fake before protection.
White block assignments at the line of scrimmage.

reads: First sell the toss fake to the right, then settle to throw.
If the deep crosser (red read) clears into the open middle, hit him as he comes across to the left.
If the cross is covered, check the swing back to the right flat for the easy completion.
Take the left streak as the shot if the deep defender bites on the fake.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and aggressive run defenders. Coach's Key: the toss fake holds the second level so the deep crosser (red) can run open behind it; the swing is your safety valve.

name: snapfire_mtn_mesh_rail

routes.note: Cyan line running flat across the field left to right, half of the mesh.
Red crosser working flat across at the same depth, the mesh partner and primary read.
Yellow line running flat across the middle at intermediate depth.
Yellow stem breaking out and up toward the right sideline.
Yellow path up the right sideline (the rail) climbing vertically.

reads: IF man coverage chases the mesh, THEN let the crossers rub and throw on the run.
IF zone sits underneath, THEN the rail up the sideline opens behind it.
IF the flat defender widens, THEN hit the dig over the middle.
IF nothing crosses open, THEN climb to the corner or rail.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the mesh rub. Coach's Key: two shallow crossers form the mesh, the rail is your shot off the pick.

name: snapfire_verticals-2

routes.note: Yellow stem upfield then a hard flat break to the left sideline.
Red line straight up the field. Primary read.
Yellow vertical up the right sideline.
Yellow vertical stem that bends toward the right sideline near the numbers.
Yellow short route breaking back to the left across the field underneath.
White center dot, sets to throw.

reads: IF the middle of the field is open, THEN rip the red seam straight up the gut.
IF the safety sits on the seam, THEN climb to the right streak or the left out off the sideline.
IF coverage carries everything deep, THEN check the underneath drag for the easy completion.
IF pressure comes, THEN take the drag or the shortest open vertical now.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and soft zone. Coach's Key: the red seam is the answer versus a vacated middle, the underneath drag is the pressure outlet.

name: snapfire_stick_spacing_switch

routes.note: Yellow stem breaking out to the left sideline
Red short route breaking inside then back out
Yellow short hook settling over the ball
Yellow stem crossing release then up the field on the right
Yellow stem crossing under then up the right sideline

reads: If the switch verticals win leverage, take the seam up the right.
If underneath defenders widen, hit the red pivot in the window.
If the right is covered, work the spacing sit over the middle.
Backside, the deep out is the single-coverage answer.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: the switch release crosses the defenders up, the pivot and spacing sit are the quick spacing answers.

name: snapfire_empty_stick_spacing_switch

routes.note: Yellow stem breaking out to the left sideline
Red short route breaking inside then back out
Yellow short hook settling over the ball
Yellow stem crossing release then up the field on the right
Yellow stem crossing under then up the right sideline

reads: Empty look, so confirm the count and find the open grass fast.
If the switch verticals clear, take the seam up the right.
If defenders widen underneath, hit the red pivot in the window.
Backside, the deep out is the quick single-coverage answer.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: empty version of the switch concept, no back to check so get the ball out on rhythm.

name: snapfire_mtn_rpo_zone_alert

routes.note: Cyan line motioning right to left across the formation
Red primary route working up through the middle
Yellow stem up the field on the right
Yellow run track into the line
Cyan settling into the flat as the run-side option

reads: Read the box: if the front is light, give the inside zone run.
If the alert defender crashes the run, pull and throw the red route up the middle.
If the flat is open off the motion, take the quick cyan throw.
Check the right alert vertical against soft coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined second-level defenders. Coach's Key: run-pass conflict the box defender, give on the zone or pull to the alert.

name: snapfire_mtn_stick

routes.note: Yellow route stemming up the field then breaking back out to the left sideline
Red route running flat across the field to the left as the primary read
Yellow route stemming vertical then breaking out toward the right sideline
Cyan motion route releasing out to the right flat

reads: If the underneath defender widens, hit the red drag working across the middle.
If the drag is carried, climb to the yellow out-and-up on the left sideline.
If the coverage squeezes inside, take the yellow corner to the right.
Dump the cyan swing to the back if the flat opens off the motion.
usage_notes: Beats: zone underneath. Coach's Key: drive the red drag across, climb to the out-and-up or the right corner if it gets carried, swing valve to the back.

name: snapfire_hb_power-2

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow cutting up through the interior gap to the right
White blocking with vertical lead blocks set up ahead of the back

reads: IF the front down-blocks and the pulling lane opens, THEN press the inside gap behind the blocks.
IF the playside crowds, THEN follow the lead block and cut off it.
IF the backside chases, THEN stay tight to the wash and get vertical.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft interior. Coach's Key: hit the gap behind the lead blocks, downhill on the red track.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-3

routes.note: Cyan path shows the receiver running jet motion flat across the formation and continuing toward the right sideline to catch the touch pass.
White paths show blockers staying in to wall up the front for the jet.

reads: IF the edge is clear, THEN flip the touch pass to the jet man on the move.
IF the defense flows hard with the motion, THEN it can cut back behind the blocks.
IF the edge defender sits, THEN get the ball out quick before he closes.
usage_notes: Beats: slow edge defenders and over-flowing fronts. Coach's Key: time the touch pass to hit the jet in stride.

name: snapfire_y_cross-2

routes.note: Yellow route breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow vertical up the left.
Red primary route running a deep crosser from right to left across the middle.
Yellow route stems up and curls back to the inside.
Yellow route works out and climbs up the right sideline.

reads: IF the deep cross clears the underneath defenders, THEN throw it on the move.
IF a safety drives the crosser, THEN climb to the vertical behind it.
IF coverage rolls deep, THEN check the curl or the out underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and zones that pass the crosser late. Coach's Key: the Y cross is the primary, verticals clear the box.

name: snapfire_inside_cross

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow route working shallow across the middle from left.
Yellow route stems up and breaks in across the middle (the inside crosser).
Yellow route stems vertical then breaks back inside.
Red route up the right sideline breaking back down to the sideline.

reads: IF the crossers stack and run free, THEN take the open man on the move.
IF underneath defenders widen, THEN throw the shallow drag.
IF the corner sits, THEN come back to the comeback on the right.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with the crossing mesh, drag underneath. Coach's Key: high-low the middle with the two crossers.

name: snapfire_inverted_veer

routes.note: Yellow route shows the back running flat across behind the line on the sweep path.
Short red arrow at the mesh shows the QB read keeping it inside off the edge defender.
White paths show the line blocking the front for the veer.

reads: IF the read defender crashes the back, THEN the QB keeps it inside.
IF the read defender stays home, THEN give the back the outside path.
IF the edge spills, THEN get vertical now off the cut.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive edge defenders. Coach's Key: read the unblocked edge, give or keep off his leverage.

name: snapfire_angle_y_sail

routes.note: Yellow route breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow vertical up the left.
Yellow route releases out then angles back inside (the angle route from the inside).
Red primary route stems up and breaks out to the right corner (the sail).
Yellow vertical up the right sideline.

reads: IF the sail clears the flat defender, THEN drive it to the sideline.
IF the corner carries the sail, THEN come back to the angle inside.
IF the deep defenders hold, THEN check the underneath out.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 3 and soft-corner zones with the sail-flat stretch. Coach's Key: sail is the strike, angle is the underneath answer.

name: snapfire_empty_mtn_fl_screen

routes.note: Cyan path shows the receiver releasing and working back behind the line for the FL screen to the right side.
White paths show linemen releasing downfield to set up the screen wall, plus a short red blocking arrow at the point of attack.
Cyan path on the left shows a receiver clearing out wide to pull coverage away from the screen.

reads: IF the screen blockers get out clean, THEN throw it and let them lead.
IF the defense sniffs the screen, THEN pull it down and protect the ball.
IF the edge stays wide, THEN hit the screen now and follow the wall.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive pass rush and blitz looks. Coach's Key: sell the drop, then dump it to the screen and let the blockers run.

name: snapfire_dagger-2

routes.note: Red line, vertical then breaks hard in to the right intermediate, primary
Yellow, vertical clearing the middle
Yellow, up then deep to the right corner
Yellow, short in-breaker back across to the left
Blue line, out to the right

reads: If the seam clears the safety, the dig sits open over the middle.
If the linebacker drops under the dig, take the shallow drag.
If man, the vertical seam can win.
usage_notes: Beats: Cover 3 and zone. Coach's Key: the seam clears the hole for the dig behind it.

name: snapfire_mtn_y_post

routes.note: Yellow, vertical up the left sideline
Yellow, short in-breaker to the right
Cyan, motions across underneath to the right
Red line, vertical then deep middle left, primary
Cyan and yellow, vertical up the right
Yellow, breaks out toward the right sideline intermediate

reads: If single-high, the Y post attacks the deep middle.
If the safety stays over the post, work the drag.
If man, take the backside vertical.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high. Coach's Key: the TE post is the shot, the drag is the man-beater outlet.

name: snapfire_te_in

routes.note: Yellow, vertical then comes back outside left
Yellow, straight vertical
Yellow, short in-breaker to the right
Red line, vertical then breaks hard in to the left intermediate, primary
Yellow, vertical up the right sideline

reads: If the middle linebacker widens, the TE dig comes open over the ball.
If the safety drops on the dig, take the underneath in-breaker.
If man outside, the comeback left or vertical right is the answer.
usage_notes: Beats: zone. Coach's Key: the TE deep in is the target, the shallow in is the high-low underneath.

name: snapfire_pa_fork

routes.note: Yellow, vertical up the left then back out to the left sideline
Yellow, short angle out to the left flat
Red line, works across and breaks out to the deep left
Yellow, vertical up the right then back toward the sideline
White block marks at the line, play fake protection

reads: Sell the play fake, then climb to the deep out.
If the corner sits on the out, work the comeback on the backside.
If pressure, dump to the flat underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run defenses biting on the fake. Coach's Key: play-action, the deep outs flood one side.

name: snapfire_redzone_crossers

routes.note: Red primary read, releases across the field underneath
Yellow, climbs and crosses to the left at depth
Yellow, stems up then breaks out to the right sideline
Cyan motion underneath pre-snap, settles working to the left
Yellow, shallow release across the formation to the left

reads: IF man coverage chases, THEN take the shallow crosser running away from leverage.
IF zone sits underneath, THEN climb to the deep crosser behind it.
IF the corner squats, THEN work the out route to the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: man and zone in tight red-zone windows. Coach's Key: stacked crossers create a natural rub, hit whichever crosser comes clean.

name: snapfire_mtn_dbl_post_reverse_dragon

routes.note: Red primary read, climbs vertically up the right then bends back inside toward the deep middle
Yellow, second post climbing up the center-right and breaking in toward the middle at depth, the other half of the double post
Yellow, short route breaking out toward the right sideline near the line of scrimmage
Yellow, short vertical stem then breaks hard out to the left sideline
Cyan pre-snap motion working across toward the right, settling as the underneath dragon/drag element

reads: IF the single-high safety leans to the yellow post, THEN throw the red post breaking the other way.
IF both posts are bracketed deep, THEN check the underneath motion man settling across the middle.
IF the corner squats on the short side, THEN take the out route to the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverage. Coach's Key: the double post puts two vertical breakers on the deep middle with the red post as the money read, the cyan motion adds an underneath dragon outlet. Overlapping red and yellow lines on the right make exact route depth lower certainty.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_out-3

routes.note: Red, vertical stem then breaks out to the left sideline as the alert pass option
Yellow, run path through the right A/B gap, the run side of the run-pass option
Yellow, short out-breaking route to the right as the RPO read
White, blocking down for the inside run

reads: IF the alert defender on the isolated receiver is off, THEN throw the out before the snap commit.
IF the box is light, THEN hand it off on the inside run track.
IF the read defender crashes the run, THEN pull and throw the quick out.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft alert coverage. Coach's Key: pre-snap alert to the isolated out, otherwise read the box for give or quick throw.

name: snapfire_se_corner

routes.note: red primary read, stems up and works across to the deep left side of the field
yellow, shallow drag working from left to right across the middle
yellow, short out to the left flat underneath the drag
yellow, breaks back toward the middle then up, working the right side
yellow, releases out of the backfield to the right toward the flat

reads: If the red deep route gets a step on the corner or safety, let it rip.
If the deep shot is covered, work the drag crossing the middle.
If underneath defenders sit, check the RB swing to the right flat.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and pressed man on the primary. Coach's Key: favorite play in this set, the red route is the money read with a drag and RB swing as outlets.

name: snapfire_levels_y_corner

routes.note: yellow, drives vertical then breaks in across the field at the higher level
yellow, shorter in-breaking route at a lower level (the levels stack)
yellow, third in-breaking route running underneath to build the levels look
red primary read, stems vertical then breaks to the corner up the right sideline
yellow, releases to the right then bends up the right side

reads: If the safety squeezes the levels in-routes, take the Y corner deep up the right sideline.
If the corner stays over the top, hit the high in-route as it crosses.
If the middle is sat on, work the shorter in-route or the RB to the right.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with the levels concept high-low. Coach's Key: red Y corner is the primary, the stacked in-routes give a clean levels read if the corner is taken away.

name: snapfire_pa_jailbreak_screen-2

routes.note: Red short screen path to the left flat behind the line
White block stems releasing out to the left to set up in front of the screen
White receiver releasing short toward the screen side

reads: Pre-Snap: sell the play-action fake to hold the front
1: get the ball out quick to the red screen target on the left
2: let the released linemen get out front before you commit upfield
Bail: if defenders sniff the screen, throw it away, do not force it
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive pass rushes and over-pursuing fronts. Coach's Key: the linemen jailbreak release to lead, deliver the screen on time and run behind the convoy.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-7

routes.note: White block stems stepping play-side off the line
Two white vertical block stems out on the left
Red path from the backfield curving up into the play-side A gap

reads: Pre-Snap: this is zone, read the first down lineman play-side
1: press the red path into the A gap and read the block
2: bang it if the gap shows, bend it back if the front flows over
Stay disciplined: one cut and get vertical
usage_notes: Beats: flowing/over-pursuing fronts that open cutback lanes. Coach's Key: classic inside zone, press the hole, read the down lineman, one cut north.

name: snapfire_z_close_pa_flood

routes.note: Red route working up the right then bending, the marked deep read
Yellow route releasing toward the right sideline underneath
Yellow route from the left working up and across
Yellow vertical up the far-left sideline

reads: Pre-Snap: sell the play-action, this is a three-level flood to the right
1: eyes to the deep red route, take the shot if it is one-on-one
2: if the deep is covered, drop to the flat route underneath
Backside: the left routes are the late clear-out and reset
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverages that get stretched vertically to one side. Coach's Key: high-low the flood to the right off play-action, deep first then the flat.

name: snapfire_qb_zone

routes.note: White block stems stepping play-side off the line
Two white vertical block stems out on the left
White block stem arcing up and out to the right edge
Red path pressing up into the right interior gap

reads: Pre-Snap: this is the QB on the zone run, the HB is the lead
1: press the red path into the play-side gap behind the HB lead
2: read the edge, bang it inside or follow the arc
Get vertical: do not dance, take what the front gives
usage_notes: Beats: fronts that do not box the quarterback. Coach's Key: QB carries the zone with the HB arcing as the lead, read the edge and press downhill.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_bubble-3

routes.note: Red bubble path out to the left flat, the alert throw
White block stem out front for the bubble
Yellow short inside route off the run action
White block stems straight ahead selling the run

reads: Pre-Snap: count the box and check the leverage on the left bubble
1: if the alert numbers favor the bubble, throw the red screen to the left
2: if the box is light, give it on the run action inside
Conflict defender decides: bubble out, give inside
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that cannot fit the run and cover the bubble. Coach's Key: read the conflict defender, the left bubble is the alert, the inside run is the give.

name: snapfire_jet_hb_duo

routes.note: Long cyan arrow sweeping all the way across the formation
White block stems driving straight ahead off the line
Short red path straight up the middle behind the line

reads: Pre-Snap: send the jet motion across to pull the edge and second level
1: this is the HB on duo, give it downhill on the red path
2: read the linebacker off the double teams, cut off the push
The jet is the decoy: stay disciplined inside
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that chase jet motion and vacate the interior. Coach's Key: jet motion is the eye candy, the HB runs duo downhill behind the doubles.

name: snapfire_power_read-4

routes.note: Read the end man on the line; keep through the B gap behind the wrap when he widens
The give; hits the edge with blockers in front when the read key crashes
Pull through the hole to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules with a double team up to the backside linebacker
Stalk the perimeter for the sweep

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A loaded box is fine here: the end man on the line goes unblocked and becomes the read key, so the extra defender is in conflict
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** give the sweep, the back wins the edge with blockers in front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside or upfield:** keep it, QB power downhill through the B gap behind the wrapping guard
**🚨 No pass option:** this is a run-run option (also called inverted veer), the only decision is give or keep
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense (the read key plays the QB, crashes inside, and the sweep runs free)
**Coach's Key:** Hold the keep button (X on PlayStation, A on Xbox) when the read key stands up; do nothing and the give happens on its own

name: snapfire_pa_read-3

routes.note: Long yellow route stems vertical then breaks back across the middle
Yellow route bending across the field to the right intermediate window
Short yellow release out of the backfield toward the left flat
Red primary read up the right side breaking back to the sideline
Blue fake handoff action before the dropback

reads: IF the play-action holds the defense, THEN work the red primary on the right side first.
IF the right is covered, THEN come back to the crosser through the middle.
IF coverage sinks deep, THEN check it down to the back in the flat.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Aggressive run-fit defenses that bite on play-action. **Coach's Key:** Sell the fake, read the red comeback, then work the crossers and the back as outlets.

name: snapfire_rpo_peek_slant

routes.note: Short yellow route breaking down and out to the flat underneath
Yellow route working from the right across toward the deep middle
Red primary slant breaking inside and up across the middle

reads: IF the box is loaded, THEN peek the red slant as the quick throw.
IF the conflict defender widens, THEN give the inside run.
IF the slant is covered, THEN work the underneath flat.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Defenses that crash the run and leave the slant window open. **Coach's Key:** Peek the red slant first, give the run when the key vacates.

name: snapfire_inside_cross-2

routes.note: Yellow route stems vertical from the left then bends across the field
Yellow route up and breaking hard inside across the top
Yellow route breaking out toward the right sideline
Red primary crossing route working underneath to the left
Blue release out of the backfield turning up the field

reads: IF the underneath is open, THEN hit the red crosser on the move.
IF defenders chase the cross, THEN climb to the deep in or post.
IF coverage is deep, THEN check the back on the blue release.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage that gets rubbed by the crossers. **Coach's Key:** Lead the red crosser into space, work the deep digs behind it.

name: snapfire_mtn_stick_y_nod

routes.note: Yellow. Short stem then breaks out to the left sideline.
Red primary read. Pushes vertical then hooks back at a short depth.
Yellow. Short break out toward the right at the sticks.
Yellow. Gets vertical then bends out and up toward the deep right.
Cyan motion into a swing release out to the right flat.

reads: Read the red hook over the middle as the primary.
If the hook is covered, work the stick out at the sticks on the right.
Use the swing back out wide if the underneath defenders squeeze inside.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with a stick-flat conflict and a hook sitting in the window. Coach's Key: the cyan motion swing flares the back out as the safety valve. Art verified: PASS label, red pushes up then hooks back, cyan back swings to the right flat, deep corner/nod drawn on the right.

name: snapfire_slot_shot_y_swirl

routes.note: Yellow. Short stem then breaks square to the middle.
Red primary read. Releases up and bends to the deep middle-right for the shot.
Blue underneath route running left to right just past the line.
Yellow. Pushes vertical then curls back to the left (the swirl).

reads: Take the red deep shot first if the safety is beaten or single-high.
If the post is covered, work the swirl curl on the right.
Check the underneath drag as the safe completion.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverage with a deep shot off the slot. Coach's Key: the red post is the money route, everything else is the answer if the top is taken away. Art verified: PASS label, red bends deep middle-right, blue underneath drag crosses left to right, right-side yellow curls back left as the swirl.

name: snapfire_post_wheel_texas

routes.note: Yellow. Works up the left sideline turning vertical.
