Yellow. Short release out toward the left at the line.
Yellow. Climbs then breaks back across to the left.
Yellow. Short crosser working to the left underneath.
Red primary read. Releases out toward the right then angles back inside (the Texas route).

reads: Read the red back angle route out of the backfield first.
If the back is covered, climb to the dig working across the middle.
Take the wheel up the left sideline against man if the defender trails.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the back angle and a wheel up the sideline. Coach's Key: the red Texas route is the primary (deep routes run off the top of the screen, confirm depth). Art verified: PASS label, red back angle out then in is drawn near the line, far-left yellow runs the wheel up the sideline, mid yellow dig points back left. Confidence honestly low because the deep routes exit the top of the frame.

name: snapfire_mtn_verticals_y_cross

routes.note: Red read. Runs straight up the left sideline.
Yellow. Releases vertical up the field.
Yellow. Up the seam.
Yellow. Breaks across the field to the right.
Cyan motion into a swing out to the right.

reads: Read the vertical shots first against single coverage.
If the verticals are bracketed, work the Y-cross coming across the field.
Take the swing flat as the checkdown.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high with verticals plus a crosser. Coach's Key: the image is small and low resolution, so trust the vertical-and-cross concept over fine route depth. Art verified: PASS label, red vertical up the left sideline, multiple yellow verticals plus a crosser, cyan swing to the right. Confidence honestly low because the art is small and low resolution.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_y_flat-2

routes.note: Yellow route stems straight up about 5 yards then breaks at a 45 degree angle up and across to the right.
Yellow short route from the inner alignment working up and back to the inside flat, the named Y read.
Red ball-carrier path from the mesh point running to the right along the line, the give option of the RPO. White Xs show blockers on the play side.
White blocking caps across the interior, blocking up the run action.

reads: Off the snap, read the box. If the numbers favor the run, give it on the red path to the right.
If the box is loaded against the run, pull and look the Y flat as the quick read.
If the flat is squeezed, the backside split end dig works across into the window the underneath defender vacated.
usage_notes: Beats: loaded boxes by pulling for the quick Y flat, and light boxes by giving the run to the right. Coach's Key: this is an RPO, the run read is the red path right while the Y flat is the named throw. Art verified: RPO label, red run path to the right, yellow Y flat working inside, backside yellow dig from the far left receiver. 0 calls logged so treat as untested in practice.

name: snapfire_01_trap-2

routes.note: Same-side downhill through the A gap; stay patient and cut off the trap block
Backside guard pulls to kick out the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard
Leaves the trap target alone and climbs straight to the Mike
Backside gap rules; vs odd fronts expect a double team on the nose up to a backer
Stalk the perimeter; nobody runs a route on this play

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Find the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard; he is the trap target and the lane opens inside his vacated spot
**1️⃣ Mesh:** Straight handoff, same-side run; defenses expect shotgun runs to cross the formation, so users often fly the wrong way
**2️⃣ RB track:** Downhill through the A gap behind the down blocks; vs odd fronts the trap block on a wide 4i makes the hole huge
**🚨 Check out of it:** vs double mug or six-plus defenders stacked on the line, audible out; trap struggles vs a loaded line of scrimmage
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Odd fronts (3-3-5, Mint, Penny, 3-2-6, dime 2-3 odd), pinched lines, users jumping the inside zone stereotype
**Coach's Key:** Trap is an alternate way to run power; same gap rules, but the play-side guard free releases and the puller traps the first lineman outside him

name: snapfire_hb_orbit_spacing

routes.note: Cyan. Orbits behind the line and releases wide across the formation.
Yellow. Breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow. Short stem then settles, the spacing hook.
Red. Sits in the short middle, the spacing read.
Yellow. Drives up then works back outside to settle.

reads: IF the underneath zones are spread, THEN hit the inside curl sitting in the void.
IF the hook defender drives the sit, THEN work to the outside spacing curl.
IF coverage collapses inside, THEN check to the back on the orbit swing.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: spacing curls flood the underneath, the orbit back is the safety valve to space. Verified: art labeled PASS; cyan orbit, yellow left flat, yellow sit/curl, red short curl, and right yellow curl all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_flat_wheel

routes.note: Red ball marker. Run track works to the right behind the line.
Yellow. Short flat route to the left, the quick pull read.
Yellow. Pushes up and crosses deep over the middle.
Yellow. Stems flat then turns up the right sideline.

reads: IF the run box is light, THEN give the inside run.
IF the conflict defender drops, THEN pull and hit the flat to the left.
IF the corner sinks on the flat, THEN climb to the wheel up the right sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: a defender stuck between run fit and the flat. Coach's Key: flat now, wheel up the sideline as the next level. Verified: art labeled RPO; red run track, left yellow flat, yellow deep over/dig, and yellow wheel up the right sideline all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_mtn_x_post

routes.note: Red stems vertical then breaks to the post over the middle, primary read
Yellow breaks back across to the left sideline
Yellow stems up and breaks out toward the left
Yellow stems vertical then breaks out to the right
Cyan releases out to the right flat then up

reads: IF the safety stays wide, THEN throw the post into the middle.
IF the middle is closed, THEN work the deep out to the right.
IF pressure comes, THEN check the back releasing to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high man. Coach's Key: the X post is the shot, the out routes are the timing answers.

name: snapfire_h_shallow_cross

routes.note: Yellow shallow working in from the left
Yellow crossing underneath left to right
Yellow stems up then breaks in across the middle
Red streak up the right sideline, the primary clearout read
Yellow short release out to the right flat

reads: IF the vertical clears a defender out, THEN hit the dig behind it.
IF the underneath defenders sink, THEN take the shallow cross on the move.
IF nothing opens, THEN check it down to the back.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the shallow rub. Coach's Key: cross clears the box, dig sits in the vacated window.

name: snapfire_pa_read-4

routes.note: Yellow streak straight up the left sideline
Yellow stems then climbs across to the middle
Yellow short out toward the right
Red stems vertical then breaks out and up, the primary read
White play fake then check release; line shows PA blocking

reads: IF the play fake pulls the linebackers up, THEN hit the crosser behind them.
IF the corner bites the out, THEN take the primary up the sideline.
IF coverage holds deep, THEN work the underneath out for the gain.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-support defenses. Coach's Key: sell the fake, then read the high-low on the right.

name: snapfire_mesh-3

routes.note: Yellow stems up then breaks out to the left sideline
Yellow shallow crossing left to right
Yellow shallow crossing right to left, the mesh point
Red breaks out toward the right sideline
Yellow swings out of the backfield and up the right sideline

reads: IF man coverage, THEN throw the crosser running away from the trailing defender.
IF zone, THEN sit the mesh man in the open window.
IF the underneath is jammed, THEN take the back on the wheel up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the rub at the mesh point. Coach's Key: let the crossers pick, hit whoever comes clean.

name: snapfire_post_flood_salem

routes.note: Red streak up the left sideline, clearout
Yellow breaks back across toward the left sideline
Yellow stems up then breaks out to the left
Yellow short hitch breaking back inside on the right
Yellow stems vertical then breaks across to the middle

reads: IF the vertical clears the corner, THEN read the flood high to low on the left.
IF the flat defender widens, THEN throw the out behind the underneath.
IF the middle opens, THEN hit the dig coming across.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with a three-level flood. Coach's Key: stretch one defender vertically, throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_flood_hb_flat

routes.note: Yellow vertical stem running straight up the left sideline
Second yellow vertical climbing up the field on the left, stacking the deep zone
Red line working across to the left at intermediate depth then turning up, marked as the key read
Yellow route coming back inside from the right toward the middle underneath
Backfield dot with a yellow release out into the left flat underneath

If the deep stuff is taken away, check it down to the HB out in the left flat for the easy gain

Yellow bubble screen path releasing back and to the right
White stalk blocks set up in front of the bubble

reads: Read the conflict defender over the slot before handing off
If he sits in the box, throw the bubble to the right out in space
If he widens to the bubble, give the red buck run inside
usage_notes: Beats: defenders who cannot cover the bubble and fit the run at the same time. Coach's Key: read the box-edge defender, take what he gives.

name: snapfire_qb_zone-2

routes.note: Quarterback keeps and runs the inside zone behind the offensive line; no routes drawn
White: blocks the perimeter defender on the left
White: blocks the perimeter defender on the right
White: line steps and blocks zone for the QB carry

reads: This is a designed quarterback run: take the snap and hit the inside zone lane behind the blocks.
Read the flow of the line and press the first open interior gap, then get vertical.
Finish forward; the receivers are stalk blocking the edges to spring the run.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and defenses that do not account for the QB as a runner. Coach's Key: it is a run, follow the zone blocks and hit the crease downhill.

name: snapfire_four_verticals-3

routes.note: Yellow: runs straight up the left sideline
Red primary read: runs the inside seam vertically and bends toward the post
Blue: releases short and works to the flat underneath as the outlet
Yellow: runs the vertical seam up the right hash
Yellow: runs straight up the right sideline

reads: Read the safeties: against single high, take the red seam bending into the open half.
Against two-high, work the seams into the holes between the hash and the numbers.
If the deep coverage holds, dump the blue checkdown to the back underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverage where a seam splits the safety. Coach's Key: bend the seams off the safety leverage, the checkdown bails you out if it is covered.

name: snapfire_h_mtn_mesh

routes.note: Yellow: dips in then wheels up the left sideline
Red primary read: shallow crosser, half of the mesh
Yellow: short stem and settles over the middle
Cyan/yellow: shallow crosser the opposite way, the other half of the mesh, with motion
Yellow: stems up the right then breaks out toward the corner

reads: Read the mesh first and take the crosser that comes clean off the muddle of the mesh.
If both shallow routes are covered, hit the sit route in the soft spot over the ball.
Backside wheel or corner is the shot when the coverage squeezes the crossers.
usage_notes: Beats: man (rub at the mesh) and zone (sit route finds the hole). Coach's Key: motion reads the coverage, then throw the open crosser. Favorite tag in the art.

name: snapfire_power_read-5

routes.note: Read the end man on the line; keep through the B gap behind the wrap when he widens
The give; hits the edge with blockers in front when the read key crashes
Pull through the hole to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules with a double team up to the backside linebacker
Stalk the perimeter for the sweep

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A loaded box is fine here: the end man on the line goes unblocked and becomes the read key, so the extra defender is in conflict
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** give the sweep, the back wins the edge with blockers in front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside or upfield:** keep it, QB power downhill through the B gap behind the wrapping guard
**🚨 No pass option:** this is a run-run option (also called inverted veer), the only decision is give or keep
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense (the read key plays the QB, crashes inside, and the sweep runs free)
**Coach's Key:** Hold the keep button (X on PlayStation, A on Xbox) when the read key stands up; do nothing and the give happens on its own

name: snapfire_verts_x_drag

routes.note: Blue motion-then-route: comes underneath on the shallow drag across the formation
Red primary read: stems up then breaks inside across the middle on the dig
Yellow: runs the vertical seam up the right hash
Yellow: runs vertically up the right side
Yellow: runs straight up the right sideline

reads: Verticals stretch the top of the coverage; read the red dig breaking in behind the linebackers.
If the deep middle squeezes the dig, take the X drag working across underneath.
Against deep coverage that drops, the seams can win up the hashes off the safety leverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone where the dig settles behind the underneath defenders. Coach's Key: the verts clear the deep zones, the dig and drag fill the vacated middle.

name: snapfire_salem

routes.note: Yellow: stems vertically up the left then breaks back toward the sideline at the top
Yellow: angles out and down toward the left sideline at a shallow depth
Red primary read: stems up then breaks inside across the middle
Yellow: short stem then settles back at the sticks
Red: pushes vertically up the right then breaks back toward the sideline

reads: Work the right side first: read the red dig breaking in over the middle as the primary.
If the dig is covered, sink to the curl settling underneath at the sticks.
Backside, the deep out or the comeback is the answer against tight coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with the dig-curl combo flooding the intermediate middle. Coach's Key: high-low the hook defender with the dig over the curl.

name: snapfire_dig_n_go

routes.note: Yellow: breaks out toward the left sideline at a shallow depth
Yellow: stems inside then breaks back up vertically (the dig-and-go double move)
Yellow: runs the vertical seam up the right hash
Yellow: short stem then breaks out underneath toward the right
Red primary read: pushes vertically then breaks back toward the right sideline

reads: The dig-and-go double move is the shot; sell the inside break then take it up if the defender jumps it.
Read the red comeback on the backside as the rhythm throw if the double move is not there.
Underneath, the out and whip routes give you the quick outlet versus pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive defenders who drive on the dig. Coach's Key: the dig sets up the go, bait him inside then beat him over the top.

name: snapfire_corner_strike-2

routes.note: Yellow: breaks out toward the left sideline at a shallow depth
Yellow: stems up then breaks out and up toward the left corner
Yellow: stems up then breaks inside across the middle on the dig
Yellow: releases out shallow toward the right
Red primary read: stems up the right then breaks out and up to the corner (the strike)

reads: The right-side corner is the strike: read the red corner route breaking away from the safety.
If the corner is bracketed, work the dig coming across the middle underneath it.
Check the flat or out routes for the quick outlet if the deep shots are covered.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high or sideline leverage where the corner can win to the boundary. Coach's Key: hit the corner in the hole between the corner and safety.

name: snapfire_wheel_mills

routes.note: Yellow. Drives upfield then breaks hard in across the middle.

Yellow vertical clearing down the right sideline.
Yellow. Pushes deep then breaks in behind the vertical, the Mills element.

reads: First look is the HB wheel up the left sideline against man or a vacated flat.
If the wheel is carried, climb to the dig coming in behind it over the middle.
The bunch vertical clears the deep defender to open the in-breaker underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and single-high. Coach's Key: the wheel and the dig work the same grass, take whichever the safety leaves.

name: snapfire_duo-4

routes.note: White vertical blocking lines, double teams climbing to the second level.
Red ball-carrier line straight downhill behind the center, reading the double teams.
White vertical lines on both sides, receivers blocking the edge.

reads: Press the line of scrimmage straight downhill behind the double teams.
Read the vertical push, cut off whichever double team climbs to the linebacker.
Stay patient and let the blocks displace the front before pressing the crease.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts. Coach's Key: it is a downhill gap-and-go, ride the double teams and cut off the displaced backer.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_bubble

routes.note: Yellow / red read side. Pushes up then breaks flat to the left as the alert.
Short yellow track through the middle, the give read with the red QB line to the left.
Yellow. Inside bunch receiver bubbles out and back toward the right sideline for the screen.

reads: Read the bubble flat defender on the bunch side.
If that defender stays inside or commits to the box, throw the bubble to the bunch right now.
If he widens to cover the bubble, hand it off and run it inside.
usage_notes: Beats: boxes that overload the run fit. Coach's Key: the bubble punishes any defender who jumps the give.

name: snapfire_counter

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow taking the handoff and cutting back to the right behind the blocks.
Cyan path running flat from the left across the formation, then turning up to the right off the edge.
White block stems with backside action pulling to lead the counter.

reads: IF the front flows to the motion, THEN counter back against the grain behind the pullers.
IF the pullers log the edge, THEN bend it up inside.
IF the edge stays disciplined, THEN press north into the interior gap.
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing fronts that overcommit to motion. Coach's Key: use the cyan motion to misdirect, then run the counter back behind the pull. Art verified: RUN label, red HB carrier cuts right behind the blocks, cyan motion crosses the formation.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-8

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow taking the handoff and pressing the interior, cutting down through the right-center gap.
White stems flowing playside on the zone.
White vertical stems clearing out downfield on the left.

reads: IF the playside gap opens, THEN press and get north quickly.
IF the front gets washed playside, THEN cut back behind the flow.
IF the second level fills the cutback, THEN bang it straight up the called gap.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined gap fits. Coach's Key: press the zone, read the first down lineman, hit the cutback if the front overflows. Art verified: RUN label, red HB carrier presses the interior and cuts through the right-center gap behind white zone blocks.

name: snapfire_shock

routes.note: Red read. Runs flat across to the left underneath. Drawn in red as the primary.
Yellow. Short stem then turns back at a shallow depth on the left.
Yellow. Releases straight up the field on the left side.
Yellow. Pushes up then curls back over the middle-right.
Yellow. Works toward the right sideline then turns vertical up the boundary.

reads: IF zone widens underneath, THEN hit the red drag crossing into the open window.
IF the drag is carried, THEN climb to the curls sitting at the sticks.
IF the coverage squeezes the curls, THEN take the vertical shot up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with a drag underneath and curls at the chains. Coach's Key: the red drag is the primary, the curls are the rhythm answer. Art verified: PASS label, red runs the flat drag left, yellow curls and verticals fill the rest of the field.

name: snapfire_fade_out

routes.note: Red primary read. Breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow. Releases up the left sideline.
Yellow. Stems upfield and leans slightly inside.
Yellow. Runs straight up the field.
Yellow. Runs straight up the field.

reads: Pre-snap: count the deep safeties to confirm vertical leverage.
IF the left out (red) is open underneath, THEN take the quick throw to the sideline.
IF the defense squats on the out, THEN hit the fade up the left sideline over the top.
IF the left is bracketed, THEN work the three right-side verticals one-on-one.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high coverage. Coach's Key: the out is the quick game, the fade and right-side verts are the shot if they jump the flat.

name: snapfire_bench_corner

routes.note: Red line: releases vertical off the left stack then breaks deep up the field as the primary read.
Yellow line: pushes upfield to about the 30 then breaks flat out to the left sideline.
Yellow line: vertical release straight up the right sideline.
Yellow line: short release then breaks in across the field to the left.
Yellow line: short release then breaks in across the field to the left, stacked under the other in-breaker.

reads: First look is the red corner route off the left stack. If the deep defender sits or trails, take the corner.
If the corner is bracketed, work back to the bench out on the left as the sideline outlet.
If the defense rolls to the left, hit the in-breaking drags coming across from the right.
Late, the right go route clears the top and can come open against single high.
usage_notes: Beats: man and soft outside leverage. Coach's Key: the red corner is the primary, the bench out and crossing drags are the timing answers underneath.

name: snapfire_fk_cheat_outside_zone

If the edge is sealed, bounce it outside off the stretch blocks.

name: snapfire_z_dagger

Yellow vertical stem up the seam to clear the middle for the dig
Yellow stem up then short break toward the left sideline

If the safety sinks on the dig, the seam can run by him up the field.
If the middle is walled, drop it down to the swing in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high zone. Coach's Key: the vertical clears the hook defender so the dig settles in the void behind him.

name: snapfire_wr_under

Yellow vertical stem up the field from the left side
Yellow short crosser coming underneath back to the left
Yellow stem up then breaks out to the right sideline

reads: Read the deep in across the middle as the primary.
If the middle is taken, work the drag coming underneath.
Backside out is the reset against squeezing coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: the under drag and the dig high-low the middle linebacker.

name: snapfire_slot_cross

Yellow deep crosser climbing from the left across the field
Yellow stem up then breaks in across the middle
Yellow flat route working in underneath from the left
Yellow short crosser coming back underneath toward the left

If the top is covered, work the crosser climbing across the field.
Underneath drags are the outlet against deep zone.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the crossers, zone with the verticals. Coach's Key: the crossing routes rub defenders and open the middle.

name: snapfire_fork_z_dig

routes.note: Red primary read runs straight up the left sideline
Yellow route climbs from the left across to the right
Yellow stem up then breaks in across the middle
Yellow path working out toward the right sideline
Yellow checkdown releasing toward the flat

reads: Work the deep dig over the middle as the main answer.
If the dig is bracketed, climb to the crosser running across.
Take the vertical or the checkdown if the middle is loaded.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: the crosser and the dig fork the second level, pick the open side.

name: snapfire_shallow_cross

routes.note: Red primary read runs the shallow crosser flat across the field from left
Yellow stem up then short break out to the left
Yellow stem up then breaks in across the middle
Yellow stem turning back over the middle
Yellow vertical stem up the field on the right
Blue checkdown swinging out to the left flat

reads: Follow the shallow crosser across the field as your first read.
If the underneath is jumped, climb to the dig over the top.
Take the swing to the flat if everything inside is walled.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the shallow rub. Coach's Key: lead the crosser away from trailing man defenders for run after catch.

name: snapfire_drive

routes.note: Red primary read stems up then breaks in across the middle
Yellow vertical stem up the field on the right
Yellow shallow crosser running across underneath to the right
Yellow checkdown swinging out to the left flat

reads: Run the dig and the shallow drive as a high-low on the middle defender.
If the linebacker sinks to the dig, throw the shallow underneath.
If he jumps the shallow, hit the dig behind him over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: classic drive concept, high-low the hook defender with the dig over the shallow.

name: snapfire_01_trap-3

routes.note: Same-side downhill through the A gap; stay patient and cut off the trap block
Backside guard pulls to kick out the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard
Leaves the trap target alone and climbs straight to the Mike
Backside gap rules; vs odd fronts expect a double team on the nose up to a backer
Stalk the perimeter; nobody runs a route on this play

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Find the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard; he is the trap target and the lane opens inside his vacated spot
**1️⃣ Mesh:** Straight handoff, same-side run; defenses expect shotgun runs to cross the formation, so users often fly the wrong way
**2️⃣ RB track:** Downhill through the A gap behind the down blocks; vs odd fronts the trap block on a wide 4i makes the hole huge
**🚨 Check out of it:** vs double mug or six-plus defenders stacked on the line, audible out; trap struggles vs a loaded line of scrimmage
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Odd fronts (3-3-5, Mint, Penny, 3-2-6, dime 2-3 odd), pinched lines, users jumping the inside zone stereotype
**Coach's Key:** Trap is an alternate way to run power; same gap rules, but the play-side guard free releases and the puller traps the first lineman outside him

name: snapfire_mtn_rpo_read_flat

routes.note: cyan, runs the inside zone path as the run portion of the RPO
yellow short arrow to the flat, the pass key off the read defender
white stems, line zones for the run
cyan stems, receivers block or clear on the perimeter

reads: Read the conflict defender at the snap.
If he sits or crashes the box, throw the yellow flat now.
If he widens to the flat, hand off the inside zone.
usage_notes: Beats: a second-level defender who cannot cover the flat and fill the run. Coach's Key: read-key decides give or throw, do not predetermine.

name: snapfire_rz_mtn_spot

routes.note: yellow, short break to the left sideline
yellow, vertical then breaks toward the left deep corner
cyan stem then hooks up, sits in the middle hook window
yellow, vertical then breaks toward the right deep corner
cyan, releases underneath across the formation

reads: In the red zone, read the spot curl sitting in the middle first.
Work the corner breaks on either side against single coverage.
Dump to the flat if the hook defenders squeeze the spot.
usage_notes: Beats: zone in the red zone with the spot-corner high-low. Coach's Key: spot sits, corners stress the deep edges, take the open level.

name: snapfire_speed_option-3

routes.note: red, attacks the edge and reads the pitch key
runs parallel as the pitch option on the perimeter
white vertical stems, receivers block the perimeter defenders

reads: Attack the edge and read the pitch-key defender.
Keep it inside if the key takes the pitch man.
Pitch to the trailing back if the key squeezes the quarterback.
usage_notes: Beats: an unblocked edge defender on the perimeter. Coach's Key: speed option, keep or pitch off the read, do not block the pitch key.

name: snapfire_bench

routes.note: yellow, breaks to the left sideline
red, vertical up the left side, the clearout/primary
yellow, vertical up the right side
yellow, breaks to the right sideline
blue, releases to the flat as the checkdown

reads: Read the bench out-breaking routes against the corner leverage.
Use the red vertical to clear the deep defender off the bench.
Check down to the back in the flat if the outs are covered.
usage_notes: Beats: off/zone corners with the high-low bench concept. Coach's Key: verticals clear, throw the bench out underneath, back is the outlet.

name: snapfire_spot_x_option

routes.note: red, vertical stem then breaks in across the middle, option route the primary
yellow, stems up then breaks toward the left deep corner
yellow, hooks back into the right hook window
yellow, breaks toward the right deep corner
yellow, short release to the right flat

reads: Read the X option route over the middle off coverage leverage.
If the middle defender sits, break the X out, if he widens, break it in.
Work the spot-corner combo on the right as the secondary read.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the X option finding the soft spot. Coach's Key: the red X option is the read, let it settle in the open window.

name: snapfire_spacing-2

routes.note: yellow, shallow crosser working to the left
yellow, short vertical then hooks back into the left hook window
red, hooks back into the middle as the primary spacing sit
yellow, short hook into the right hook window
yellow, breaks to the right sideline

reads: Pick the open curl/snag window against the zone spacing.
Read the red middle curl first, then work to the nearest open hook.
Take the out or drag if the hook defenders crowd the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage by flooding the underneath windows. Coach's Key: spacing concept, throw to grass, the red curl is the anchor.

name: snapfire_y_cross-3

routes.note: yellow, breaks to the left sideline
yellow, vertical up the left side
red, crosses the field from right to left on a deep over, the primary read
yellow, short stem then bends up toward the right corner
yellow, releases to the right flat then turns up the sideline

reads: Track the red Y deep cross across the field as the primary.
Throw it on the move once it clears the underneath defenders.
If the cross is bracketed, climb to the corner/wheel on the right or check the streak.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the deep over splitting the levels. Coach's Key: the red Y cross is the answer, let it run open across the field.

name: snapfire_mtn_drive_wheel

routes.note: red, vertical stem then deep in-break across the middle, the primary
yellow, shallow crosser underneath the dig
yellow, vertical up the right side
yellow, releases then turns up the right sideline as the wheel
cyan, releases to the left flat as the checkdown

reads: Read the red drive/dig coming open over the middle first.
Climb to the wheel up the right sideline against man.
Take the shallow drag or the back in the flat as the outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: man with the drive dig and wheel combo. Coach's Key: drive dig is the primary, wheel is the man-beater behind it.

name: snapfire_salem-2

routes.note: Red primary read, vertical stem then breaks in hard across the middle from the right.
Yellow, drives upfield then breaks back out to the left sideline.
Yellow short hook turning back at the left hash.
Yellow, small hook curl settling in front near the right hash.
Yellow, releases and angles out underneath to the left.

reads: IF the middle of the field is open, THEN hit the right dig coming across as the primary.
IF the dig is bracketed, THEN work to the left comeback on the outside.
IF underneath defenders widen, THEN check the settled curls or the back in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with a high-low on the hook defenders. Coach's Key: primary is the crossing dig, with curls and the back as the dump underneath.

name: snapfire_hb_blunt_dive

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path, short downhill dive into the A/B gap just right of center.
White vertical stems, line blocks straight ahead for the interior dive.
White lines on the perimeter, receivers stalk block their men.

reads: IF the interior gap shows daylight, THEN hit the dive straight downhill.
IF the front squeezes, THEN press the heels of the line and stay tight to the block.
IF the perimeter blocks hold, THEN keep north and south for the easy gain.
usage_notes: Beats: light or spread-out boxes. Coach's Key: quick-hitting interior dive, get downhill now, no dancing.

name: snapfire_zone_split_lead

routes.note: White blocks across the front
Red ball-carrier path pressing the interior gap
White lead path through the hole ahead of the back

reads: Follow the lead blocker into the hole.
If the front clogs the playside, cut back to daylight.
Stay tight to the blocks and finish downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: lighter boxes. Coach's Key: split-flow zone with a lead blocker, hit it downhill behind the block.

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_counter

routes.note: Red primary read running across and up toward the right at intermediate-deep level
Yellow route up the right sideline vertical
Cyan route releasing to the flat after the counter fake
White block paths showing counter-action protection

reads: Sell the counter run action, then set up to throw.
If the deep defender bites on the vertical, hit the primary crosser underneath it.
If coverage caps the crosser, check the back in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-support and single-high. Coach's Key: counter fake holds the linebackers, read the primary across the field.

name: snapfire_sft_pa_double_cross

routes.note: Red primary read crossing the field at deep-intermediate level toward the right
Yellow route crossing the opposite way, forming the double-cross with the primary
Yellow route working flat across to the left
Cyan route releasing underneath after the fake

reads: Carry out the play action, then read the crossers high to low.
If the deep defender widens, the primary over route opens up the middle.
If both crossers are covered, settle for the back underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the rub from crossing routes. Coach's Key: double crossers stress the middle, take the first one that clears traffic.

name: snapfire_read_option-2

routes.note: Red arrow straight ahead off the snap, QB reads the edge and can keep it.
Cyan/green path to the right, the back runs laterally to the right as the give option.

reads: IF the edge defender crashes on the back, THEN the QB keeps it straight upfield (red path).
IF the edge stays home on the QB, THEN hand it to the back running right (green path).
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive edge defenders. Coach's Key: read the unblocked end, take what he gives you.

name: snapfire_mtn_duo-2

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path runs to the right toward the line of scrimmage, pressing the interior.
Cyan paths sweep to the right across the formation, blockers working playside.

reads: IF the interior double team creates a crease, THEN press it and cut off the climbing blocker.
IF the front spills outside, THEN bend the run back behind the down blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: one-gap fronts. Coach's Key: it is a downhill duo, read the double team to the linebacker level and stay patient.

name: snapfire_wheel_switch-2

routes.note: Yellow route breaks flat across the field to the left.
Yellow route releases inside then turns vertically up the left sideline.
Yellow route bends out and runs vertical up the right boundary.
Yellow route stems up and breaks back across toward the middle deep.
Red mark: back releases and hooks toward the right flat, drawn in red as the primary/checkdown.

reads: Hit the wheel up the sideline if the corner sits or jumps the underneath.
If the deep coverage carries the wheels, work the crossing/drag underneath.
If nothing opens, check the back releasing to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and flat-sitting zones. Coach's Key: switch release clears the wheel deep down the sideline.

name: snapfire_bench_switch

routes.note: Yellow route runs flat to the left.
Yellow route releases up and outside on the left.
Yellow route runs up then breaks square to the right sideline.
Red route up the right side breaking to the boundary, drawn in red as the primary read.
Cyan (blue) mark over the back indicates motion or release out of the backfield.

reads: Look first to the bench route breaking to the right sideline.
If the corner squats on the out, climb to the switch vertical behind it.
If the boundary is covered, work the backside drag across the field.
usage_notes: Beats: off-coverage and soft corners. Coach's Key: bench out at the sticks is the primary, switch release stresses the deep corner.

name: snapfire_jet_pa_boot

routes.note: Cyan rollout path to the right off the play-action fake
Red route stemming deep then breaking back left, the primary boot read
Blue flat-crossing line underneath to the right
Cyan flat path across, sells the jet action
Short white release off the front

reads: IF the boot side flat is open, THEN hit the crosser coming with you.
IF underneath is covered, THEN climb to the red deep route.
IF the edge pressures, THEN tuck and run off the boot.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-flow linebackers and edge crash. Coach's Key: sell the jet, get on the perimeter, work high to low.

name: snapfire_hb_counter

routes.note: Red carry path angling back to the right behind the line, counter footwork
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front

reads: IF the pullers clear, THEN follow them through the hole.
IF the front over-pursues the false flow, THEN cut behind the down blocks.
Be patient on the counter step, let the blocks set up.
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing fronts that chase the initial step. Coach's Key: false-flow then follow the pull, do not bounce too early.

name: snapfire_strong_power-2

routes.note: Red carry path downhill and to the right behind the down blocks
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front to the strong side

reads: IF the kick-out seals the edge, THEN run downhill off it.
IF the puller leads through, THEN follow him into the hole.
Get vertical once you clear the down blocks, do not dance.
usage_notes: Beats: light strong-side edges. Coach's Key: gap-scheme power to the strength, hit it behind the pull.

name: snapfire_jet_lead_read_option

routes.note: Red arrow shows the QB keep lane straight ahead off the read
Yellow give path at the mesh point, the read key
Cyan flat path crossing the whole formation as the sweep / pitch option
White vertical stem on the far left

reads: IF the read key crashes inside, THEN keep on the red lane.
IF the key sits, THEN give the inside lead run.
IF the edge widens with the jet, THEN the interior give is clean.
usage_notes: Beats: edges that cannot handle jet motion plus the read. Coach's Key: jet motion stresses the perimeter, then read the give-or-keep.

name: snapfire_stick-2

routes.note: Red flat route running across to the left, the backside answer
Yellow breaking to the left sideline
Yellow stem climbing up the left hash
Yellow short hook settling then squaring, the stick concept
Yellow path bending up the right sideline

reads: IF the flat defender widens, THEN hit the stick sitting inside.
IF the flat defender squeezes, THEN throw the out / flat outside him.
IF nothing underneath, THEN climb to the backside drag.
usage_notes: Beats: zone flat defenders, classic stick-flat high-low. Coach's Key: read the flat defender, stick or flat off his leverage.

name: snapfire_jet_qb_counter

routes.note: Counter-steps off the jet, follows the pullers downhill
Flows one way to pull the defense, the counter hits back the other way
One kicks the edge, one wraps to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules, wall off the lane

reads: **Pre-Snap:** The jet motion sets the flow, the counter goes back against it
**1️⃣ Defense chases the jet:** the QB counters behind the pullers into the vacated side
**🚨 Patience:** let the pullers clear before pressing the hole
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Fast-flowing, jet-chasing defenses (the counter punishes over-pursuit)
**Coach's Key:** Mobile QB, time it behind the kick and wrap

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-4

routes.note: Catches a quick flip on the perimeter at full speed off the motion
Stalk and crack the perimeter defenders to spring the catch
Brief protection, the ball comes out instantly
Touch it to the jet man before the edge can react

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A glorified outside-zone to the edge, get the fast man the ball in space
**1️⃣ Edge stays inside:** flip it to the jet on the perimeter with blockers in front
**🚨 If the edge widens with the motion:** pull it down, the space is gone
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Pinched fronts and slow edge defenders, it attacks the perimeter they vacate
**Coach's Key:** Get the ball out instantly, this is a perimeter run by another name

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_slot_option

routes.note: Yellow stem off the line up to about 8 yards, then breaks back across to the right (the upper-left yellow arrow)
Cyan motion from the right wing that loops down and around, then runs the long horizontal crossing route all the way across the field
Yellow route from the right wing climbing up and out to the deep upper-right corner toward the numbers
Blue route releasing off the line then breaking back inside short, sitting underneath as the secondary look

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Sell the play-action, the motion sets the slot crossing the field
**1️⃣ Crosser (cyan):** if he comes open running across underneath the coverage, that is the throw
**2️⃣ Option/sit (blue):** if the crosser is walled off, work the short inside sit underneath
**🚨 Deep shot or backside out:** climb to the deep yellow route up the right side or check the backside out if the underneath is capped
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Coverage that bites on the play-action and loses the crossing slot off the motion
**Coach's Key:** Sell the run fake first, the motion crosser is the timing throw, the deep yellow and short blue are the secondary reads

name: snapfire_qb_wrap

routes.note: Two white vertical blocking stems walling off defenders
Yellow pulling path leading up through the middle to seal the gap
Red primary, follows the wrap blocker downhill through the middle

reads: Read the mesh, then keep it on the wrap
Follow the pulling blocker through the interior gap
Press it downhill behind the down blocks and find daylight
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Boxes that lose the extra hat when the QB becomes a runner
**Coach's Key:** This is a QB run, follow the wrap and get vertical

name: snapfire_rpo_read_bubble-2

routes.note: Red read/handoff mesh path working to the left as the give option
Yellow bubble path arcing out to the right as the throw option

reads: Read the conflict defender at the snap
If he sits inside on the run, throw the yellow bubble to the right
If he widens to the bubble, hand off the red inside run
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Defenders who cannot cover the bubble and fit the run at once
**Coach's Key:** Pure RPO, let the read key declare then take what he gives

name: snapfire_mtn_te_lead_read_opt

routes.note: White vertical blocking stem up the left side
Cyan lead path arcing across to seal the playside, supported by white block stems
Yellow lead path through the middle in front of the keep
Red primary, reads the key and keeps it up the middle behind the lead

reads: Ride the mesh and read the unblocked key
If he crashes, keep it on the red path behind the lead
If he stays home, give it on the read and let the TE lead the run
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Edge keys that crash the dive, the keep follows the lead block
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, read the key and let the TE lead open the lane

name: snapfire_mtn_flood_h_sneak

routes.note: Red primary read. Short vertical stem off the bunch then breaks hard left and runs deep across the field, ending with the arrow up at the left 20. This is the backside answer and the first place to look.
Yellow. Releases and climbs straight up the left sideline as the clear-out over the top.
Yellow. Releases from the bunch and runs up the left-middle, bending slightly, holding the safety.
Cyan motion. Loops out of the bunch and works a shallow crossing route, the arrow pointing back to the right at the 30 as the underneath level.
Yellow. Vertical stem then breaks out to the right at intermediate depth, the arrow curling back, the high level of the flood.
Yellow. The H-Sneak release. Slips out of the backfield and runs flat toward the right sideline as the low, quick flood option.

reads: First eyes go to the red dig coming back across to the left: if the middle of the field is open and the safety widens with the verticals, throw the over.
If the dig is walled off, flip to the right-side flood and read it high to low: take the out / corner over any flat defender.
If the flat defender carries the corner, drop down to the H-back in the flat for the easy completion.
Late, the shallow drag is your outlet underneath if everything else is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high coverage. Coach's Key: the verticals clear the top while the right-side out and flat stretch the flat defender vertically, so let the red backside over come open first, then high-low the flood.

name: snapfire_corner_strike-3

routes.note: Yellow short route breaking out to the left sideline.
Yellow stems up then breaks out toward the left corner.
Yellow drives upfield then breaks in to the left across the middle.
Red primary read, stems inside then breaks out to the right corner.
Yellow breaking back out toward the right sideline.

reads: Primary is the red corner route from the right slot toward the sideline.
If the corner is covered, work the dig breaking in over the middle.
Use the underneath outs to the sidelines as the quick outlets versus pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 2 and squat corners. Coach's Key: the corner route attacks the hole behind the flat defender and in front of the safety.

name: snapfire_curls_y_shake

routes.note: Yellow short route breaking out to the left sideline.
Yellow stems up then hooks back, a curl settling over the ball.
Yellow drives upfield then breaks back in to the left, the Y shake.
Red primary read, stems up then hooks back, a curl, marked as the read.
Yellow breaking out to the right sideline.

reads: Work the red curl on the right side as the primary, sit it in the open zone.
If the curl is covered, climb to the Y shake breaking across the middle.
The outside outs are the quick answers to the flats versus blitz.
usage_notes: Beats: off coverage and soft zones. Coach's Key: curls at the sticks with the Y shake gives an underneath-to-intermediate progression.

name: snapfire_y_corner-2

routes.note: Yellow short route breaking out to the left sideline.
Yellow stems up then hooks back to the left.
Yellow shallow route working left across the formation underneath.
Red primary read, stems up the middle then breaks out to the right corner deep.
Yellow shallow route working back to the left across the formation.

reads: Primary is the red Y corner route breaking deep to the sideline.
If the corner is bracketed, drop to the shallow drag working underneath.
Use the back-side curl and out as the outlets versus rotated coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 2 and quarters. Coach's Key: the Y corner from the slot finds the void behind the flat defender between the corner and safety.

name: snapfire_duo-5

routes.note: Red; takes the handoff and runs downhill behind the double teams, reading the linebacker level to cut
White blocks; line works vertical double teams up to the linebackers (the gap scheme power-run footwork)
White blocks; outside receivers block their men on the perimeter, nobody runs a route

reads: **Pre-Snap:** This is a downhill gap run; identify the double teams and the linebacker you are reading
**1️⃣ Mesh:** Take the straight handoff and press downhill behind the double teams
**2️⃣ Cut:** Read the linebacker level off the double teams and cut to the side he vacates
**🚨 Loaded box:** Be ready to check out vs a stacked or extra-hat box where the duo gets clogged
usage_notes: **Beats:** Even fronts where double teams can climb. **Coach's Key:** Duo is a vertical-push gap run, ride the double teams and cut off the linebacker.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_out-4

routes.note: White; short out route breaking toward the left sideline
Yellow; runs a short inside angle as the read attached to the run
Red primary read; stems up then breaks out toward the right sideline, this is the alert throw
White; line shows run blocking for the option give

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Read the box and the leverage on the alert out to the right
**1️⃣ Alert:** Throw the marked out route on the right when the corner is off or the numbers favor the pass
**2️⃣ Give:** Hand off on the run track if the box is light and the defenders widen to the out
**3️⃣ Peek:** The backside slant gives you a quick inside answer vs squatting flat defenders
usage_notes: **Beats:** Off coverage and conflicted run defenders. **Coach's Key:** Alert the out first, fall back to the run/slant off the box read.

name: snapfire_pa_go_slot_cross

routes.note: Yellow; runs straight vertical up the left sideline
Yellow; works up then bends across to the deep middle behind the go
Red; runs vertical up the right sideline as the deep clear/read
Yellow; runs across the field underneath to the right after the play fake

reads: **Pre-Snap:** This is play-action; sell the fake to hold the linebackers before you climb
**1️⃣ Verticals:** Read the go routes off the safety; take the one-on-one up the sideline
**2️⃣ Slot cross:** Work the slot crossing the deep middle behind the verticals as the play-action window opens
**3️⃣ Drag:** Drop to the underneath crosser if the deep routes are covered
usage_notes: **Beats:** Aggressive run-fitting defenses biting the fake. **Coach's Key:** Sell the run fake, then read verticals to the slot cross.

name: snapfire_rpo_peek_slant-2

routes.note: White; short out-breaking route to the left sideline
Red marked slant; runs a quick inside slant across the middle, this is the peek read
Yellow; works out toward the right sideline at a short depth as the attached option
White; line shows run blocking for the give

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Identify the slant defender; he tells you whether to peek the throw or give the run
**1️⃣ Peek slant:** Throw the marked slant on the left when the underneath defender sits or widens
**2️⃣ Give:** Hand off on the run track if the slant window is closed and the box is light
**3️⃣ Backside:** The right-side out is your quick answer vs a squatting flat defender
usage_notes: **Beats:** Conflicted run defenders and soft inside leverage. **Coach's Key:** Peek the slant first, give the run when the slant is covered.

name: snapfire_cheat_spot_y_quick

routes.note: Yellow; runs straight vertical up the left sideline
Cyan motion across the formation then continues on a shallow drag to the left
Yellow; works out of the backfield, bends out then up the right on a wheel
Yellow; pushes up then breaks out toward the right corner
Yellow; runs vertical up the right sideline

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Use the cyan motion to read man or zone and create the shallow drag
**1️⃣ Quick drag:** Hit the motion man on the shallow drag on the move vs man coverage
**2️⃣ Spot/out:** Work the right-side out/corner into the void if the flat defender chases the drag
**3️⃣ Verticals:** The two go routes hold the top and open the underneath quick game
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage off the motion drag. **Coach's Key:** Motion to ID, then take the quick drag or the right-side spot/out.

name: snapfire_qb_power

routes.note: White blocking scheme across the front
Red ball-carrier path keeping it and running behind the blocks to the right

reads: Snap and follow the down blocks to the play side.
If the edge seals inside, get downhill behind your blockers.
If the edge spills you, bounce it outside and stay north-south.
Lower the pads and finish forward.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes that cannot match the extra hat. Coach's Key: the QB is the runner, follow the power blocks and finish downhill. ART VERIFIED: RUN label confirmed; white blocking across the front and red QB ball-carrier path behind blocks to the right all present.

name: snapfire_smash

routes.note: Red route climbing up the field and bending at the top, the primary read
Yellow short stem settling underneath
Yellow driving straight up the field
Yellow stemming up then breaking out toward the right sideline up top
Yellow releasing wide and turning up the right sideline

reads: This is a smash concept, read the corner over the hitch on the throwing side.
If the corner sinks to the deep route, drop the hitch underneath him.
If the flat defender jumps the hitch, throw the corner behind him.
Work the red primary down the field if the coverage is light deep.
usage_notes: Beats: Cover 2 and soft-corner zones with the high-low corner/hitch combo. Coach's Key: the corner-over-hitch is the answer, let the flat defender pick your throw.

name: snapfire_return_rpo_alert_flat

routes.note: yellow breaks down and out toward the right, the alert flat throw
yellow short corner stem off the bunch
white vertical, stalk blocks the backside
white blocks, run action with the cyan return path underneath
cyan ball-carrier track on the run option, works back across the formation

reads: Read the flat defender on the alert side.
If the flat defender widens to cover the flat, give the return run.
If the flat defender sits in the box, throw the alert flat now.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that overcommit one side, the return run counters their flow. Coach's Key: the flat throw is the conflict on the alert defender.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-9

routes.note: red ball-carrier path, presses the front then cuts off the zone blocks
white blocks, the line works the inside zone scheme playside
white vertical blocks, stalk the perimeter defenders

reads: Press the inside zone track and read the first down lineman playside.
If the front gives a cut-back, plant and get vertical in the back gap.
If the playside opens, stay on the zone track and hit it downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts with one-gap defenders. Coach's Key: press the line of scrimmage, let the cut declare itself off the double teams.

name: snapfire_dagger-3

routes.note: yellow vertical clearing up the left sideline
yellow pushes vertical up the seam to clear the middle
red primary read, comes underneath the clear-out and breaks in across the middle
yellow short hook to the right side

reads: The vertical seam clears the middle defender out.
Throw the dig behind the cleared-out zone, this is the primary.
If the dig is carried, work back to the short curl on the right.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high zone, the seam runs off the safety and opens the dig window. Coach's Key: dig behind the clear-out is the throw.

name: snapfire_duo-6

routes.note: red ball-carrier path, runs vertical behind the double teams reading the linebacker
white blocks, the line doubles to the second level on duo
white vertical blocks on the perimeter

reads: Run the duo track tight behind the double teams.
Read the playside linebacker, cut off whichever way he flows.
If the double teams move bodies, get vertical downhill behind them.
usage_notes: Beats: heavy fronts, the double teams move the down linemen and climb to the backers. Coach's Key: it is a vertical run, the back reads the second-level defender.

name: snapfire_pa_copo_cross_dig

routes.note: Red route from the top crossing down toward the middle left: primary read
Yellow stems up then breaks in across the field
Yellow deep crosser breaking in to the left from the right
Yellow stems vertical then settles back on the right
Blue underneath route breaking across to the left after the play fake

reads: Sell the play-action fake to hold the linebackers.
Read the crossers high-low: take the red cross if it clears the second level.
If the dig is bracketed, dump it to the back on the underneath drag.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-fitting linebackers and Cover 3 with the crossing dig. Coach's Key: the fake pulls the box, then flood the intermediate middle.

name: snapfire_y_option_wheel

routes.note: Yellow swings from inside and turns up the left sideline
Yellow climbs vertical and bends toward the deep middle
Red route stems up then breaks out: option route read off coverage, primary
Yellow stems then breaks out toward the right sideline at depth
Yellow vertical up the right sideline

reads: Read the Y option first: sit it down versus zone, break it out versus man.
If the option is covered, climb to the post up the seam.
Take the wheel up the left sideline as the deep shot if the corner bites inside.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the option break, and Cover 3 with the seam. Coach's Key: let the Y option win on leverage, then hit the wheel over the top.

name: snapfire_mesh_y_out

routes.note: Yellow shallow crossing in from the left, part of the mesh
Yellow shallow crossing the opposite way, completing the mesh
Yellow stems up then breaks out to the sideline at intermediate depth
Yellow short break out underneath to the right
Red vertical straight up the right sideline: primary read

reads: Take the backside go on the red route if the corner is off or squatting.
Work the mesh underneath against zone, pick the crosser away from the linebacker.
Hit the Y out breaking to the sideline as the intermediate option.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the mesh rub and single coverage outside. Coach's Key: the two drags pick the defenders, then settle the out into the open void.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-10

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path: takes the handoff and presses the inside A/B gap
White blocking bars: line zone-blocks in unison and works to the second level

reads: Press the inside zone aiming point and read the first down lineman.
If the front gets washed inside, cut it back to the open gap.
Stay patient behind the zone blocks and get downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts and gap-sound defenses with the cutback. Coach's Key: read the play side first, then trust the cutback when it washes.

name: snapfire_hb_power-3

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path: takes the handoff and cuts up behind the pulling blocker
White blocking bars: line down-blocks and a guard pulls to kick out the edge

reads: Read the down blocks and follow the puller through the hole.
If the edge is kicked out, get vertical inside the kick block.
Press the second level and stay behind your pulling lineman.
usage_notes: Beats: lighter boxes and one-gap fronts. Coach's Key: down block, kick out, and run the back downhill behind the puller.

name: snapfire_qb_wrap-2

routes.note: White, line down-blocks and a lineman pulls to wrap up to the play side
White, fills or fakes through the backfield mesh
Red, QB keeps and follows the wrapping puller into the hole

reads: IF the puller wraps clean, THEN follow him straight downhill into the gap.
IF the edge stays wide, THEN press inside off the down blocks.
This is a designed QB keeper, attack downhill behind the wrap.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Defenses that ignore the QB run threat. **Coach's Key:** QB follows the wrapping lineman; gap-scheme power with the quarterback as the runner.

name: snapfire_whips

routes.note: Red primary, stems inside then whips back out to the sideline
Yellow, shallow route working across to the left
Yellow, breaks inward across the middle from the right
Yellow, releases late and angles out toward the right sideline

reads: IF the defender overplays the inside stem, THEN the whip breaks back out and is open (the red).
IF man coverage trails, THEN let the whip and crossers create natural rubs.
IF the underneath is jammed, THEN work the in-breaker across the middle.
Use the HB angle as the checkdown versus pressure.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage; the whip eats up trailing defenders. **Coach's Key:** Sell the inside stem, the whip back to the sideline is the answer.

name: snapfire_inside_cross-3

routes.note: Yellow, climbs vertical then breaks across on a deep over route
Red, shallow drag working across the middle left to right
Blue, runs straight up the seam in the middle
Yellow, breaks out toward the right at intermediate depth

reads: Work the crossing routes high-low, the shallow under the deep over.
IF the underneath is open, THEN hit the shallow crosser on the move (the red).
IF a linebacker carries the shallow, THEN climb to the deep over behind him.
Use the seam to hold the middle safety so the crossers clear.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man and zone with the high-low crossers. **Coach's Key:** Shallow-deep cross combo; throw the crosser the defense passes off late.

name: snapfire_inside_switch

routes.note: Red primary, releases inside-out on the switch and breaks to the deep post
Yellow, short switch release then settles underneath
Yellow, runs straight up the seam in the middle
Yellow, breaks flat to the left sideline
Yellow, releases from the backfield and runs up the right hash

reads: The switch release crosses the two bunch defenders to create separation.
IF single-high, THEN take the post breaking across the middle (the red).
IF the safety sits on the post, THEN work the underneath switch route.
Use the seam and HB up the right to stress the deep coverage.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage off the switch rub and single-high safeties. **Coach's Key:** The switch frees the post; take the deep shot when the middle opens.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_bubble-4

routes.note: Red, the run portion, HB takes the inside zone / give track
Yellow, the alert, bunch receiver bubbles back to receive the now screen
White, line blocks the run action while the bubble is alerted

reads: Pre-snap: count the box versus the numbers over the bunch.
IF the box is loaded or the flat defender crashes, THEN throw the bubble to the bunch.
IF the bubble is covered and the box is light, THEN hand off on the inside run.
This is a true read, conflict the alley defender and take what he gives.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Defenses that cannot cover the bubble and fit the run with the same defender. **Coach's Key:** Read the overhang, give versus light boxes, bubble when he sits in the box.

name: snapfire_slot_option

routes.note: Yellow comes back hard to the left across the field at intermediate depth.
Yellow short stem then breaks to the left toward the sideline underneath.
Yellow stem upfield then hooks back, a short sit-down option route over the middle.
Red track from the backfield up the middle, the primary marker showing the QB read lane.
Yellow vertical stem then break back out to the right sideline.

reads: Work the option route over the middle first, throw it where he settles versus the leverage.
If the defender sits on the option, the crosser is coming across behind it.
If the pocket breaks down, the red lane shows the QB pull-up read up the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: man and zone, it is an option-route concept. Coach's Key: let the inside option settle in the soft spot, art is slightly blurry so confirm the middle break live.

name: snapfire_hb_circle

routes.note: Yellow vertical stem then break flat to the left sideline.
Yellow short stem then break back out to the right (whip) underneath.
Yellow stem up then a long break to the left across the field at depth.
Yellow short stem then break flat out to the right sideline.
Red straight vertical up the right sideline, marked as the primary.

reads: First eyes the right vertical for a one-on-one shot up the sideline.
If the top is capped, work the dig coming across the middle.
If underneath is what they give, take the open out route on either flat.
usage_notes: Beats: zone underneath with the flats and dig. Coach's Key: verticals clear the top, the dig and outs flood the levels below.

name: snapfire_y_lead_read_option-2

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow forward and slightly right out of the backfield, the give/keep read run.
Green/teal short arrow ahead of the back, the lead blocker inserting up into the hole.
White vertical stalk/block lines from the split receivers, no routes drawn.

reads: Read the unblocked end, give to the back or keep based on his crash.
On the give, run off the Y lead block inserting into the hole.
If the edge squeezes, follow the lead up inside and finish downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive edge defenders. Coach's Key: it is a lead read, the Y blocks the play side while the back keys the read defender.

name: snapfire_rpo_peek_slant-3

routes.note: The peek read, breaks in over the middle
Clears the area for the slant window
The run portion of the RPO
Blocks the inside zone run

reads: IF the box is light or the linebacker triggers, THEN peek the slant.
IF the slant window is covered, THEN hand off the inside zone.
IF the defender sits in the throwing lane, THEN give the run.
usage_notes: Beats: Linebackers caught between run fit and slant window. Coach's Key: Peek the slant first, fall back to the zone run if it is covered. Verified: art labeled RPO, red slant + yellow clearout + yellow zone-run path + run blocking all drawn.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-5

routes.route: Jet across then up the right sideline (cyan)
routes.note: Motions left to right, the touch pass target turning vertical
Seals for the jet receiver
Blocks out front for the jet

reads: IF the edge is unset and the receiver clears, THEN flip the touch pass to the jet.
IF the defense strings it out, THEN let the receiver turn it up behind the blocks.
Quick touch throw, get it out on the move.
usage_notes: Beats: Slow edge defenders, the jet motion gets the ball to space fast. Coach's Key: This is a quick lateral touch pass to the jet, blocks lead in front. Verified: art labeled PASS, cyan jet motion left-to-right turning up the right sideline + white blocking all drawn.

name: snapfire_stick_slot_option

routes.note: Primary, sits down at the sticks on the right
Second level of the stick read
Works underneath across
Stick-flat high-low partner
Backside clearout
Backside settle route

reads: IF the flat defender widens to the back, THEN hit the stick sitting inside.
IF the flat defender squeezes the stick, THEN drop it to the flat.
IF the right side is covered, THEN work the backside curl or drag.
usage_notes: Beats: Zone underneath, the stick-flat option puts the flat defender in a bind. Coach's Key: Read the flat defender, stick over him or flat under him. Verified: art labeled PASS, two red routes on the right (stick/hook + out behind) + yellow drag + RB flat + backside clearout/curl all drawn.

name: snapfire_four_verticals-4

routes.note: Outside vertical on the left
Primary, reads the middle of the field deep
Inside vertical on the right
Outside vertical on the right
Outlet if the verticals are covered

reads: IF single-high, THEN bend the seam away from the deep safety.
IF two-high, THEN attack the seam between the safeties or hit the outside go.
IF the coverage caps it all, THEN check the back underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: Single-high coverage, the inside seams split the lone safety. Coach's Key: Read the deep middle, the slot seam benders win against one-high. Verified: art labeled PASS, red seam-bender primary + yellow outside/inside verticals + blue back route all drawn.

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_verticals

routes.note: Outside vertical on the left
Inside vertical on the left
Motions and releases vertically, the moving target
Outside vertical on the right

routes.route: Play action block at the mesh (white)
routes.note: Sells the run fake then protects

reads: IF the play fake holds the safety, THEN throw the seam behind him.
IF the coverage stays deep, THEN read the moving slot up the middle.
IF nothing opens vertically, THEN take the matchup on the outside go.
usage_notes: Beats: Run-honoring safeties, the play action buys the seams a step on the deep middle. Coach's Key: Sell the run, the motion seam is the moving deep-middle read. Verified: art labeled PASS, yellow outside/inside verticals + cyan motion seam up the middle + white PA mesh block all drawn.

name: snapfire_redzone_scissors-2

routes.note: Primary, the high crossing leg of the scissors to the corner
Crosses under the corner route, the other scissors leg
Underneath outlet
Backside vertical breaking out
Backside crossing route

reads: IF the corner defender carries the post, THEN throw the corner over the top.
IF the corner sinks to the corner route, THEN hit the post crossing inside.
IF the safety brackets the scissors, THEN check the back in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: Man and quarters in tight space, the crossing post and corner create a natural rub in the red zone. Coach's Key: The scissors cross holds the corner, throw opposite his leverage. Verified: art labeled PASS, two crossing red routes on the right (corner + post) + yellow RB flat + backside corner/dig all drawn.

name: snapfire_slants_middle

routes.note: Red primary, quick break inside across the middle
Yellow route angling in toward the middle
Yellow route crossing in to the right across the middle
Yellow route breaking inside over the middle
Short yellow release out of the backfield

reads: Hit the red slant from the left on rhythm.
If it is covered, read the crossing slants filling the middle.
Dump to the back underneath if the middle clogs up.
usage_notes: Beats: off coverage and zone shells. Coach's Key: throw the red slant now, do not wait for the cross. VERIFIED: art shows PASS label, red slant left outside, yellow in-breakers from left slot and right side, crossing drag, and short yellow RB checkdown. Five routes (1 red + 4 yellow) consistent with art, type matches.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-6

routes.note: Cyan path across the formation and out toward the right edge, the touch-pass target and ball carrier
White vertical stem clearing the left side
White vertical stem clearing the right edge for the runner

reads: Catch the snap and flip the touch pass to the jet man crossing.
Let him get edge leverage on the cyan track to the right.
Outside receivers clear and wall off the perimeter.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-flowing edges. Coach's Key: it is a forward touch pass on the sweep, get it out fast (labeled PASS). VERIFIED: art shows PASS label, cyan jet/touch-pass path sweeping right, and two white vertical stems (left outside, right outside). All three routes present, type matches.

name: snapfire_pa_zone_read

routes.note: Red arrow off the mesh showing the QB pulling and reading after the run fake
Yellow route stemming up the field and bending in
Yellow route breaking back to the left across the middle
Yellow route working up and over toward the middle deep
Yellow route stretching the right sideline deep

reads: Sell the zone run fake to hold the second level.
Pull off the mesh on the red read and find the open level.
Work the deep yellow routes if the defenders bite up.
Take the crossing drag underneath if everything is capped.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run-keying defenses. Coach's Key: the play fake is the setup, deep shots open behind it. VERIFIED: art shows PASS label, red QB keep/read arrow off mesh, and four yellow routes (left deep vertical bending in, left-slot drag breaking back left, right-slot deep over, right outside deep vertical). All five routes present, type matches. Confidence honestly marked LOW given the dense overlapping art.

name: snapfire_mtn_curl_n_go

routes.note: Yellow route running flat across underneath to the backside.
Yellow route stemming up then hooking back, the curl portion.
Yellow route hitching at depth then turning vertical, the curl-and-go double move.
Yellow vertical stem turning back, working the boundary.
Cyan motion line crossing the formation and bending up the far sideline.
Red vertical line up the backside, the alert deep shot.

reads: Watch the curl-and-go: if the defender bites on the hitch, take the go over the top.
If they play soft, settle the underneath curls for the steady completion.
Use the cyan motion route as the moving target if the curls are bracketed.
The backside vertical on the red line is the deep alert versus single high.
usage_notes: Beats: defenders who jump the curl. Coach's Key: the curl-and-go is the trap, the curls are the safe outlet. Verified: PASS label matches, all six routes drawn (yellow backside drag, yellow curl, yellow curl-and-go, yellow comeback, cyan motion bender, red backside vertical).

name: snapfire_rpo_x_close_duo_bubble

routes.note: Short yellow vertical pop route off the snap, the pass option behind the run.
White run blocking shown for the duo run action behind the line.
Red route working out, settling, then breaking back, the backside read.

reads: Read the run conflict defender on the duo action.
If he fits the run, throw the quick pop route behind him.
If he drops to the pop, hand off the duo run.
The backside route on the red line is the late alternate window.
usage_notes: Beats: linebackers caught between the duo run fit and the pop pass. Coach's Key: it is duo first, pop the seam when they crash. Verified: RPO label matches, short yellow pop + white duo blocking + red whip/return all drawn.

name: snapfire_four_verticals-5

routes.note: Yellow streak straight up the left sideline.
Yellow route stemming vertical then bending toward the left numbers.
Yellow vertical up the inside seam.
Yellow streak up the right sideline.
Blue route releasing up the middle, the checkdown or fifth vertical.
Red vertical line, the primary alert on the backside seam.

reads: Read the single-high or two-high shell pre-snap.
Versus single high, hit the seam that splits the safety on the red alert.
Versus two high, work the outside go routes one-on-one or the hash benders.
Take the underneath blue checkdown if everything vertical is bracketed.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and soft zone coverage. Coach's Key: the inside seams stress the safety, win deep before the checkdown. Verified: PASS label matches, all six routes drawn (two yellow go's, yellow bender, yellow inside seam, blue checkdown, red backside vertical).

name: snapfire_empty_mtn_spot_replace

routes.note: Red short stem hooking back inside on the left, the primary read
Yellow up then breaking in across the middle to the left
Yellow short hook settling in the spot zone over the middle right
Yellow up then breaking in across the middle to the left
Cyan releases flaring wide to both flats underneath

reads: IF the spot/settle is open in the void, THEN take it on rhythm.
IF the zone defender sits on the spot, THEN replace him with the dig behind it.
IF the middle is full, THEN check the flat releases underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with spot-replace concept. Coach's Key: read the settle defender, the dig replaces the vacated window.

name: snapfire_rpo_peek_spot

routes.note: Yellow breaking flat to the left sideline
Yellow short out to the left
Yellow run track, the give read
Red short hook/snag over the middle right, the peek read
Yellow shallow working back across to the right

reads: IF the box is light, THEN hand off the inside zone.
IF the box is loaded, THEN peek the spot/snag over the middle.
IF the spot defender drops, THEN give the ball.
usage_notes: Beats: loaded boxes and short-zone droppers. Coach's Key: read the box, the peek snag is the quick pull-and-throw answer.

name: snapfire_mtn_snag_curls

routes.note: Yellow. Vertical release straight up the left side.
Yellow. Second vertical up the left side, paired with the outside go.
Cyan motion path out of the backfield swinging to the left flat.
Red. Short route that hooks back inside over the middle, the snag sit-down.
Yellow. Runs across the field from right toward the right-to-deep, an over route at the 30.

reads: Read the snag curl over the middle first, sit it in the soft spot of the zone.
If the hook is covered, the back swinging to the left flat is the quick outlet.
Up top, the two left verticals clear space and punish single coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage underneath. Coach's Key: the snag plus flat swing stretches the flat defender, throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_cheat_flat_x_post

routes.note: Yellow stem up then bending to the post over the middle
Short yellow out to the left flat
Cyan motion into a shallow flat working left
Blue breaking in across the middle
Blue working in across the middle
Blue up the right sideline

reads: IF the safety sits, THEN work the X post into the deep middle.
IF the flat defender widens with the motion, THEN hit the flat underneath.
IF the middle is open, THEN take the dig crossers.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high looks with the post behind the flat. Coach's Key: motion sets the flat, read the post off the deep safety. Art has overlapping blue lines, low confidence on exact depths.

name: snapfire_y_stick_dig

routes.note: Yellow breaking out to the left sideline
Yellow up the seam from the left
Yellow short stick settling and turning over the middle
Red dig breaking in across the middle to the left, the primary read
Yellow shallow working to the right flat

reads: IF the linebackers widen, THEN hit the dig crossing the middle.
IF the dig is covered, THEN drop to the Y stick underneath.
IF the flat opens, THEN take the shallow to the right.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the dig behind the linebackers. Coach's Key: read the hook defender, dig over stick on the high-low.

name: snapfire_mesh_spot-2

routes.note: Yellow shallow crossing left to right across the middle
Yellow shallow crossing right to left, forming the mesh
Yellow short stem settling then breaking up toward the corner in the spot
Red up the right sideline clearing space

reads: IF the mesh crossers run free, THEN throw the first one to clear underneath.
IF the underneath is rallied, THEN settle the spot route in the void.
IF coverage squeezes inside, THEN take the vertical clearout one-on-one.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage on the mesh rub and zone in the spot. Coach's Key: read mesh to spot, let the crossers pick off the underneath defenders.

name: snapfire_reload_mesh_spot

routes.note: Yellow up then breaking back out to the left sideline
Yellow shallow crossing left to right
Yellow shallow crossing right to left, forming the mesh
Yellow short stem settling then breaking up in the spot
Cyan up the right sideline
Cyan release out of the backfield to the right flat

reads: IF the mesh crossers come open, THEN hit the first crosser through the rub.
IF the underneath defenders sink, THEN settle the spot route in the void.
IF everything inside is covered, THEN check the swing or the back-side comeback.
usage_notes: Beats: man rub coverage and zone in the spot window. Coach's Key: read mesh to spot, reload protection keeps the back in then releases.

name: snapfire_reload_hb_sweep

routes.note: White stems, line blocks for the sweep to the right
Cyan/blue arc, ball-carrier takes the handoff and sweeps wide to the right

reads: IF the edge sets the perimeter, THEN press it wide and turn the corner.
IF the defense flows hard outside, THEN cut up inside the blocks.
