IF the edge is sealed, THEN get north and beat the pursuit to the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: slow edge defenders and over-pursuing fronts. Coach's Key: get the back on the perimeter behind the reload blocks, then turn upfield.

name: snapfire_read_option-3

routes.note: White stems, line zone blocks the interior
Yellow give path, the running back's mesh lane
Red arrow, QB reads the unblocked end man and keeps if he crashes

reads: IF the read key crashes on the back, THEN keep it and get to the edge.
IF the read key stays home on the QB, THEN give to the back inside.
IF the edge over-pursues, THEN cut it back up the seam.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive ends and run-blitz looks. Coach's Key: read the unblocked edge, hold the keep when he sits, give when he chases.

name: snapfire_fk_jet_y_wheel

routes.note: Yellow up then settling up the left hash
Cyan jet motion across underneath after the fake, flaring to the right flat
Yellow short stem then wheeling up the right sideline
Red up then bending over the top on the right, the primary vertical

reads: IF the jet fake holds the flat defender, THEN throw the Y wheel up the right sideline.
IF the wheel is carried, THEN take the red vertical/post over the top.
IF coverage sinks deep, THEN check the jet swing underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage that bites the jet fake. Coach's Key: sell the jet, then hit the wheel behind the cleared-out flat.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-7

routes.note: Cyan jet motion across the formation, receiving the quick touch pass on the move to the right edge
White short release setting up to block in front of the jet
White stems releasing to wall off the perimeter for the jet runner

reads: IF the edge is unset, THEN flip the touch pass to the jet man and let him run.
IF the perimeter is blocked up, THEN follow the lead blocks to the sideline.
IF the edge crashes, THEN the jet bends it back inside the blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: soft edges and slow-flowing defenses. Coach's Key: time the touch pass with the jet motion and get him the ball on the perimeter in stride.

name: snapfire_hb_mtn_mesh_flat

routes.note: Red crosser running flat across left to right, the mesh and primary read.
Yellow short stem with a hook, sitting down over the middle.
Yellow line breaking flat across to the right at intermediate depth.
Yellow stem then breaking out and up toward the right sideline.
Cyan path out of the backfield to the right flat.

reads: IF man chases the mesh, THEN throw the crosser on the move.
IF the flat defender jumps the back, THEN the corner opens over the top.
IF zone sinks, THEN settle the curl in the window.
IF the underneath is clogged, THEN check the HB to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage, with the HB flat as a zone outlet. Coach's Key: the red mesh crosser is your first look, the flat is the safety valve.

name: snapfire_quick_hb_mtn_spacing

routes.note: Yellow stem then breaking flat out to the left sideline at mid depth (spacing).
Yellow vertical to a hook, sitting in the spacing window up the left side.
Red short stem hooking back over the middle, the primary spacing read.
Yellow climbing straight up the field to clear out.
Yellow stem then breaking out and up toward the right sideline.
Cyan path out of the backfield to the right flat.

reads: IF the underneath defenders spread, THEN take the open spacing hook on the snap.
IF the hook defender sits, THEN work to the out on either flank.
IF zone widens, THEN settle the inside curl in the void.
IF nothing sits open, THEN check the HB to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with spacing windows. Coach's Key: quick game, the red curl is the rhythm throw, spread the field and take what sits.

name: snapfire_mtn_y_corner_z_choice

routes.note: Yellow line working flat out to the left sideline at the line level.
Yellow stem then breaking flat in across the middle toward the left.
Cyan stem climbing vertical then breaking out toward the left at depth, the Y corner.
Yellow stem up then breaking out and up toward the right sideline, the Z choice read.
Yellow climbing up and bending inside over the top on the right.

reads: IF the Z choice defender sits underneath, THEN climb past him to the corner.
IF the safety carries the Y corner, THEN the right-side post opens up the middle.
IF the underneath floods, THEN hit the in-breaker across the second level.
IF the boundary squeezes, THEN reset to the backside out.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high with the Z choice option. Coach's Key: the Z reads leverage, the Y corner stresses the safety on the other side.

name: snapfire_power_o

routes.note: White block stems showing playside down blocks and the backside guard pulling across to kick out / lead.
Red ball-carrier path taking the handoff and pressing the playside gap behind the puller.

reads: IF the kick-out seals the edge, THEN press the gap inside the pulling guard.
IF the down blocks displace the front, THEN run off the double-team's push.
IF the linebacker fills the hole, THEN follow the puller and read his block.
IF the edge spills, THEN bounce it back outside the pull.
usage_notes: Beats: a gap-control front. Coach's Key: down block, pull the backside guard, the back follows the puller through the hole.

name: snapfire_mtn_rpo_alert_swing

routes.note: White block stems showing the line working the run side, the give portion of the RPO.
Yellow ball-carrier track pressing the interior gap on the run option.
Cyan path looping out of the formation and bending up the right sideline, the alert swing throw.
White short stem on the right showing the alert throw target / block.

reads: IF the alert defender sits on the box, THEN throw the swing to the perimeter.
IF the conflict defender widens with the swing, THEN give the run inside.
IF the numbers favor the box, THEN pull and hit the swing in space.
IF the edge stays home, THEN hand it off and run downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: a box defender in conflict. Coach's Key: RPO, read the alert key, give the run or throw the swing based on his leverage.

name: snapfire_qb_zone_lt

routes.note: Red run arrow stepping up through the interior on the zone path.
White blocking lines, the front zone steps to seal the play side.
Long white line releasing upfield to block the deep defender on the backside.
White line angling down and to the right, driving the play-side defender to open the lane.
White short block stem holding the edge defender.

reads: IF the play-side lane opens off the zone blocks, THEN press it and hit the crease.
IF the front side clogs, THEN cut back behind the blocks.
IF the edge sets soft, THEN bounce it outside off the stalk blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts and slow-flowing linebackers. Coach's Key: 6 calls at 8.5 avg, ride the white zone blocks and read the first down lineman play side.

name: snapfire_sft_bounce_mesh_drive

routes.note: Cyan route bouncing out to the right flat
Yellow shallow with arrows both ways across the middle
Red route working in behind the shallow
Yellow stem up and toward the right sideline

reads: If the mesh rubs a defender free, hit the shallow crosser on the move.
If a linebacker jumps the shallow, throw the red drive dig behind it.
If the back leaks free, take the cyan bounce to the flat.
Deep clear is the corner up the right side.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: the mesh and drive create natural rubs, bounce the back out as the safety valve.

name: snapfire_empty_spot_wheel

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline
Yellow stem that hooks back up over the middle left
Yellow short hook settling near the line
Red route bending up the right sideline

reads: If the flat defender chases the spot, the wheel runs free up the right sideline.
If coverage carries the wheel, settle the spot sit underneath.
If single high shows, take the backside go up the left.
Curl is the checkdown over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: spot-wheel combo high-lows the flat defender, hit the wheel when he sits on the spot.

name: snapfire_mtn_cross_post

routes.note: Cyan line motioning in from the left then to the flat
Red route working in across the middle
Yellow route crossing the field to form an X downfield
Yellow route crossing the opposite way to complete the X
Yellow stem breaking out to the right
Cyan settling into the flat after motion

reads: If the crossers clear the middle, hit the red post in behind them.
If the deep crossers win leverage, take the one running away from coverage.
If the underneath flat is open off motion, take the quick cyan throw.
Out route is the backside sideline answer.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: the crossing routes form an X to stress the middle, read the post off how the safeties carry the crossers.

name: snapfire_mtn_fade_smash

routes.note: Red primary read stemming up then breaking down and out toward the left corner
Yellow short route stemming up underneath the red on the left
Yellow route pushing vertical then breaking in/out toward the right
Yellow route running straight up the field
Cyan motion route releasing out to the right flat

reads: If the corner sits on the underneath hitch, throw the red fade over the top on the left.
If the corner bails deep, drop it to the yellow hitch underneath.
If the middle clears, work the yellow dig in behind the coverage.
Check the cyan swing to the back if the right flat is open.
usage_notes: Beats: a single-high corner on the smash side. Coach's Key: high-low the corner with the red fade over the yellow hitch, then the dig and swing as outlets.

name: snapfire_dagger_sit

routes.note: Red route stemming up then breaking in across the field to the right
Yellow short stem breaking out to the left sideline
Yellow route running straight up the field
Blue short route settling down in the middle
Yellow route breaking out toward the right sideline

reads: IF the seam clears the underneath defenders, THEN throw the red dig behind them.
IF the middle defender carries the vertical, THEN drop it to the blue sit hook underneath.
IF the coverage squeezes inside, THEN check the yellow out routes on either side.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: the vertical clears the window, then the dig sits in behind it with the hook as the dump.

name: snapfire_hb_quick_base-3

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow shows the back taking the quick handoff and hitting downhill into the interior behind the base blocks.
White paths show the line base-blocking the front. No receivers released.

reads: IF the interior lane shows, THEN hit it fast off the quick handoff.
IF the front clogs, THEN bounce off the base blocks.
IF nothing opens, THEN get downhill and finish forward.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and quick-hitting needs. Coach's Key: it is a fast handoff, hit the first lane and go.

name: snapfire_mesh-4

routes.note: Yellow route stems up and breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow shallow crossing route from left to right across the middle.
Yellow shallow crossing route from right to left, forming the mesh point with the other shallow, with a small sit/settle indicated.
Red primary route up the right sideline breaking out toward the corner.
Yellow route releasing out of the backfield and turning up the right sideline on the wheel.

reads: IF the mesh crossers run free, THEN take the open shallow on the move.
IF a defender sits in the mesh, THEN the receiver settles in the window.
IF coverage drives the shallows, THEN throw the corner or wheel deep.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the rub at the mesh point. Coach's Key: mesh first, corner and wheel are the deep shots over it.

name: snapfire_qb_zone-3

routes.note: White path shows the QB keeping the ball and following the zone blocks downhill through the interior.
Short path at the mesh shows the back carrying out the exchange action.
White paths show the line zone-blocking the front for the QB run.

reads: IF the front-side lane opens, THEN the QB presses it behind the zone blocks.
IF the line washes the front, THEN cut back to the backside.
IF nothing is there, THEN protect the ball and get what is blocked.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes that do not account for the QB run. Coach's Key: it is the QB carrying zone, read the blocks like a back.

name: snapfire_y_corner-3

routes.note: Yellow route stems up and breaks in across the middle.
Yellow route stems up and breaks in across the middle, stacking with the X dig.
Red primary route stems vertical and breaks out to the right corner (the Y corner).
Yellow route working shallow across underneath from right to left.
Yellow route breaks flat to the right sideline.

reads: IF the corner route clears the flat defender, THEN drive it to the sideline.
IF the safety drives the corner, THEN work back to the digs inside.
IF underneath opens, THEN check the shallow drag.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high looks and soft corners. Coach's Key: the Y corner is the target, digs and drag fill underneath.

name: snapfire_x_follow

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow route working shallow across the middle from left to right.
Yellow second shallow crossing route following the first across the middle (the follow concept).
Yellow route stems up and breaks in across the middle.
Red route up the right sideline breaking back down toward the sideline.

reads: IF the two shallow drags stack, THEN take the trailing follow route in the open window.
IF defenders jump the first drag, THEN the follow comes open behind it.
IF the middle is crowded, THEN come back to the comeback on the right.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the rub off the stacked drags. Coach's Key: follow route trails the first shallow into open grass.

name: snapfire_mtn_fk_screen_wheel

routes.note: Yellow. Releases vertical off the LOS, then breaks at a sharp angle to the upper right and runs across the field deep.
Yellow. Angled stem climbing to the upper right with an arrowhead, clearing the deep middle.
Red = primary read and ball carrier. Sets up just past the LOS to the right, then turns and runs vertical up the right sideline. This is the real target behind the screen fake.
Cyan = pre-snap motion. Comes across underneath toward the left, selling the screen action; the wheel runner works off this fake.
White. Five linemen across the LOS, center is the square. A white block arrow pulls diagonally back to the right to sell the screen and wall off for the wheel.

reads: First, sell the screen with the motion and the pulling block to the right.
If the flat defender jumps the screen fake, hit the wheel (red) climbing up the right sideline.
If the wheel is bracketed, work back to the deep crosser coming across from the left.
If everything deep is covered, take the angle route up the right hash.
usage_notes: Beats: defenders who bite on screen action and corners who sit flat. Coach's Key: the red wheel up the right sideline is the real shot, the screen look is just the bait.

name: snapfire_levels_sail

routes.note: Yellow, short in-breaker to the right
Red line, vertical then breaks in to the right higher, primary
Yellow, short in-breaker just under the red dig
Yellow, vertical then deep right corner
Yellow, intermediate break out toward the right sideline

reads: Read levels high to low, the deeper dig comes first.
If the linebacker jumps the high dig, drop to the shallow in.
If the dig defenders squeeze inside, take the sail.
usage_notes: Beats: zone. Coach's Key: levels stacks two digs, the sail floods the right sideline.

name: snapfire_read_option-4

routes.note: White, line steps to block the front
Red line, QB reads the backside end and keeps or hands
Yellow, dives into the mesh point on the inside zone path

reads: Read the unblocked backside defender at the mesh.
If he crashes on the back, pull and keep off the edge.
If he stays home on the QB, hand off the inside zone.
usage_notes: Beats: undisciplined edge defenders. Coach's Key: classic zone read, let the defense declare.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-11

routes.note: White, line zone steps to the right
Red line, takes the handoff and presses the inside zone gap, the ball-carrier

reads: Press the front-side A or B gap and read the down blocks.
If clogged, cut back to the backside.
Get north and south once the crease shows.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes. Coach's Key: downhill inside zone, read the first down lineman.

name: snapfire_mtn_hitch_n_go

routes.note: Red line, vertical up the left sideline, primary
Yellow, vertical up the left
Yellow, short hitch then breaks back vertical
Cyan, motions across underneath to the right
Yellow, releases out toward the right flat
Cyan and yellow, vertical up the right sideline

reads: Sell the hitch to bait the corner up, then take the go behind him.
If the corner stays deep, settle on the hitch.
If man, the drag underneath is the quick answer.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive press and squat corners. Coach's Key: double-move shot, the hitch-and-go punishes corners who jump the stop.

name: snapfire_rpo_zone_alert_omaha

routes.note: Yellow, short bubble breaking out to the left, the alert throw
White, line zone steps for the run action
Yellow, takes the mesh on the inside zone path
Red line, vertical release then breaks out to the right, the read alert side

reads: Read the conflict defender over the run box.
If the box is loaded, pull and throw the bubble.
If the box stays light, hand off the inside zone.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that overload the box. Coach's Key: RPO, zone run with a bubble alert.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-12

routes.note: Red ball-carrier track, aiming inside through the interior gap
White, full line zone blocking with vertical climb to the second level

reads: IF the front flows, THEN press the inside gap and read the first down lineman.
IF the playside crowds, THEN cut back behind the zone flow.
IF the second level fills late, THEN get vertical through the crease.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts and gap-sound defenses. Coach's Key: classic inside zone, hit the bang gap and trust the cutback.

name: snapfire_branch_return

routes.note: Yellow, climbs straight up then breaks out at the top
Yellow, pushes up then returns back to the line, the branch/return route
Yellow, breaks inside across the middle
Red, takes the top off on the right
Cyan blue route, works out and up toward the left sideline

reads: IF the corner bails deep, THEN take the return route settling underneath.
IF coverage squats underneath, THEN climb to the vertical behind it.
IF the middle opens, THEN work the dig across the field.
usage_notes: Beats: off coverage and soft zones. Coach's Key: the return route gives a quick reset target while the verticals hold the top. Overlapping yellow lines in the middle make individual assignments lower certainty.

name: snapfire_hb_power-4

routes.note: Red ball-carrier track, aiming inside to the right behind a pulling lineman
White, down blocks playside with a backside guard pulling to lead through the hole

reads: IF the playside down blocks seal, THEN follow the puller through the gap.
IF the edge sets hard, THEN bounce off the puller's block.
IF the linebacker fills, THEN press the kickout and cut up.
usage_notes: Beats: gap-sound fronts. Coach's Key: down block, kick out, pull the guard and run downhill behind him.

name: snapfire_pa_crossers

routes.note: yellow, releases from the left and crosses deep to the right
yellow, second crosser climbing deep across the formation
red primary read, ball-carrier red arrow driving up to the deep middle-right
yellow, stems vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline
white block fake then short release out of the backfield

reads: After the play fake, read the red crosser into the deep middle as the primary.
If the deep middle is closed, take the trailing crosser working across.
If coverage rotates over the top, check the right out underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive run defenses biting on the play fake. Coach's Key: sell the run, the red deep crosser is the shot, with crossers and the out as the progression.

name: snapfire_rpo_zone_y_peek

routes.note: red ball-carrier path, takes the handoff and runs the zone track then cuts back to the left (red arrow bends back)
yellow, short quick route out of the backfield area to give the QB a quick peek throw off the run action

reads: Read the run key first, if the box gives the zone run, hand it off and let the back cut to daylight.
If the conflict defender crashes the run, peek the Y on the quick route for the throw.
Stay disciplined, take the give unless the peek read pulls down hard.
usage_notes: Beats: defenders who over-commit to the zone run. Coach's Key: this is a zone run with a built-in Y peek, take the give by default and only throw the peek when the read defender crashes.

name: snapfire_jet_qb_counter-2

routes.note: Cyan path sweeping under the line from left to right
White block stems with a lineman angling play-side as the puller
White vertical block stem on the far left
Red path pressing up into the play-side gap behind the pull

reads: Pre-Snap: jet motion sets the flow one way, counter goes back the other
1: QB keeps and runs the red counter path behind the puller
2: read the kickout and the wrap, hit the gap that opens
Sell the jet: let the motion pull the defense before you cut back
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing defenses that overrun the jet action. Coach's Key: jet flow plus counter misdirection, QB follows the puller into the back-side gap.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-8

routes.route: Jet across then up the right edge
routes.note: Long cyan path sweeping across and turning upfield on the right sideline; the touch-pass target
White block stems straight ahead off the line
White block stem releasing play-side to lead for the catch

reads: Pre-Snap: time the jet motion so the touch pass hits him in stride
1: deliver the quick touch pass to the jet man as he crosses
2: let him turn upfield on the right edge behind the lead block
Quick game: get it out fast, this is an extended handoff
usage_notes: Beats: slow-reacting edge defenders and overloaded interiors. Coach's Key: the touch pass is a forward handoff, hit the jet man and let him run the right edge.

name: snapfire_four_verticals-6

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the far-left sideline
Red vertical bending up the middle-left, the marked read
Yellow vertical bending up the middle-right
Yellow vertical up the far-right sideline
Blue route working across the middle as a checkdown
Long blue arrow out to the right flat as the outlet

reads: Pre-Snap: read the safeties, one high or two high sets the answer
1: hit the red inside seam against single-high if it splits the safety
2: against two-high, work the outside streaks on the hash
Outlet: come down to the blue cross or the HB swing if the verticals are covered
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverages on the inside seams and two-high on the outside verticals. Coach's Key: read the high safeties, the inside red seam is the first answer, checkdowns stay underneath.

name: snapfire_qb_power-2

routes.note: White block stems down-blocking off the line
White stem angling across to the play-side gap
White stem arcing up play-side to lead
Two white vertical block stems out on the left
Red path up into the play-side B gap behind the puller

reads: Pre-Snap: identify the play-side gap and the pull track
1: QB keeps and follows the red path behind the puller and HB lead
2: read the kickout, bang it inside or bounce off the wrap
Downhill: get vertical behind the double team and the pull
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and defenses that do not account for the QB run. Coach's Key: gap power with a puller and a lead, QB hits the B gap behind the wrap.

name: snapfire_z_close_duo

routes.note: White block stems driving straight ahead off the line
White vertical block stem on the far left
Short red path straight downhill behind the line

reads: Pre-Snap: the Z is closed tight, this is interior duo
1: give it downhill on the red path behind the double teams
2: read the linebacker off the doubles, cut off the vertical push
Stay patient: let the doubles climb, then go north
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and one-gap interior fronts. Coach's Key: closed Z adds a blocker, run duo downhill and read the linebacker off the double teams.

name: snapfire_jet_sprint_hb_slide

routes.note: Cyan path runs left to right behind the line and finishes past the right side along the line of scrimmage. This is the pre-snap jet motion / jet fake that sets up the pass.
Red is the primary read. Pushes vertical from the right-center, then breaks hard back to the left across the middle at roughly 12 yards.
Releases vertical up the left numbers, then bends in toward the middle of the field at depth (roughly 18-20 yards).
Climbs vertical up the right side, then breaks left at the top and works across the field deep.
Releases out of the backfield on a short slide underneath into the right flat. This is the checkdown the play is named for.

reads: Sell the jet motion first: if the defense chases the cyan motion man, the backside throws open.
Work the red primary read across the middle: if the dig comes open in the window, deliver it.
If the middle is covered, climb to the deep crosser or the left dig over the top.
If everything downfield is taken away, check it down to the HB on the slide in the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage that follows the jet motion and zone that sinks with the verticals. Coach's Key: the jet motion is window dressing for the dropback. Trust the red dig as your first look and use the HB slide as the safe outlet underneath.

name: snapfire_flood_post

routes.note: Yellow route stems up then breaks inside across the middle
Short yellow route up and hooking back to settle
Yellow route up then breaking out toward the right sideline at depth
Yellow route releasing and breaking out to the right flooding the right side
Red primary route from the right breaking deep across to the post

reads: IF the safety widens with the flood, THEN hit the red post over the top.
IF the post is bracketed, THEN work the flood routes high to low on the right.
IF underneath sits, THEN take the curl as the outlet.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Coverages that rotate to the flood and leave the post one on one. **Coach's Key:** Read the post first, then flood the right sideline high to low.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-13

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path angles from the backfield into the interior behind the line

reads: IF the front flows, THEN press the red path and read the first down lineman.
IF the playside gap opens, THEN hit it north.
IF the front overcommits inside, THEN cut back to the soft gap.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Aggressive flowing fronts that overrun the gap. **Coach's Key:** Press the red path, read the block, and cut off the first soft gap.

name: snapfire_te_under

routes.note: Stems vertical to about the 20 then breaks hard out toward the left sideline
Releases up then breaks out toward the left sideline at a shorter depth than the outside man
Red primary read; releases up a few yards off the line then runs flat underneath all the way across left to right, arrow finishing to the right
Stems vertical to about the 20 then breaks hard back inside to the left across the field
Straight vertical up the right sideline

reads: IF the underneath middle is clear, THEN hit the red TE on the under cross as it works across.
IF a defender carries the crosser, THEN climb to one of the deep out / dig breaks at the 20.
IF the secondary squeezes the intermediate routes, THEN take the far-right go up the sideline.
IF nothing opens, THEN check the crosser back down as the outlet on the move.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone coverage that opens the underneath window for the crosser. **Coach's Key:** The red TE under is your first look, let it clear the box then layer the deep outs and the right vertical behind it.

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_slide

routes.note: Cyan route running flat across the field from the left
Yellow route up then breaking out and up toward the right
Red primary route working up the field and settling to the right
Blue play-action fake with a slide-protect path before the throw
White pass-protection assignment in the backfield

reads: IF the play-action holds linebackers, THEN work the red primary settling to the right.
IF the right is covered, THEN come back to the cyan slide crossing underneath.
IF pressure comes, THEN slide protect and check it down.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Defenses that bite on play-action and chase the slide. **Coach's Key:** Sell the fake, read the red, then work the cyan slide underneath.

name: snapfire_te_attack

routes.note: Yellow route up the left side then breaking back down to the sideline
Yellow route up then breaking out toward the left sideline
Yellow route releasing straight up the seam through the middle
Yellow route up the right side then breaking back to the sideline
Red primary route working from the left underneath up across to the right
Blue release out of the backfield turning up the field

reads: IF the middle opens, THEN attack the TE seam up the hash.
IF the seam is bracketed, THEN work the red crosser into space.
IF deep is covered, THEN settle the comebacks outside or check the back.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Single-high coverage that leaves the seam open for the TE. **Coach's Key:** Attack the TE up the seam first, then crosser and comebacks as answers.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-14

routes.note: White. Both flexed left receivers stalk block their defenders.
White. Line steps and blocks the interior zone.
Red ball-carrier. Takes the handoff and presses the inside zone gap, cutting off the down blocks.

reads: Press the line of scrimmage and read the first down block.
If the interior gap opens, hit it downhill.
If the inside is clogged, cut back behind the zone flow.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts with a downhill zone run. Coach's Key: this is a RUN despite the name, the red back is the ball-carrier reading the zone blocks. Art verified: RUN label, red HB ball-carrier track at the mesh, white stalk blocks on the two flexed left receivers, white interior zone blocks.

name: snapfire_mtn_slot_fade_y_cross

routes.note: Yellow. Short stem then breaks out to the left.
Red primary read. Releases vertical up the field for the fade.
Yellow. Short stem then breaks out to the right.
Yellow. Stems vertical then bends across to the deep right (the Y-cross).
Cyan motion into a swing out to the right flat.

reads: Take the red fade first against single coverage up the seam.
If the fade is covered, work the Y-cross bending across the field.
Use the swing flat as the outlet if the underneath is open.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with a slot fade and a crosser behind it. Coach's Key: the red fade is the primary, the cyan motion swing gives a quick outlet. Art verified: PASS label, red fade runs vertical, cyan back swings to the right flat, yellow Y-cross bends to the deep right.

name: snapfire_dagger_h_choice

routes.note: Yellow. Short break to the middle underneath.
Red primary read. Climbs vertical then breaks square across the middle (the dagger dig).
Yellow. Runs straight up the seam to clear the middle.
Yellow. Stems vertical then breaks back across to the right.
Yellow. Gets vertical then bends out and up toward the deep right.
Yellow. Releases and runs a choice route reading the leverage of the defender.

reads: Read the red dig behind the vertical clearout as the primary.
If the dig is covered, work the corner up the right sideline.
Take the back's choice route or an underneath drag as the outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high zone with the dagger dig opening behind the seam clearout. Coach's Key: the vertical seam pulls the safety so the red dig sits in the window. Art verified: PASS label, red dig climbs then breaks square across the middle, yellow seam clearout up the middle, far-right corner and a backfield choice release are both drawn.

name: snapfire_hb_slip_screen

routes.note: Yellow. Breaks out toward the left sideline to clear the area.
Yellow. Runs vertical up the field to clear coverage.
Yellow. Runs vertical to pull defenders downfield.
Yellow. Runs vertical up the field.
White / grey. Linemen release to set up the screen blocks out in front.
Red ball-carrier. Slips out behind the line and works to the right behind the releasing blockers.

reads: Let the rush come, then deliver the ball to the red back on the slip out.
Read the lead blockers releasing in front of the back.
If the defense sniffs the screen, the vertical clearouts can come open up the field.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive pass rush and blitz looks by letting the rush up the field. Coach's Key: this is a screen, the red back is the target with the linemen leading the block. Art verified: PASS label, red HB slips out to the right behind the grey releasing linemen, yellow vertical clearouts up the field, left WR breaks out to clear.

name: snapfire_rpo_bubble_y_pop-2

routes.note: Yellow straight vertical stem up the middle of the field, the Pop.
Yellow short bubble release back and out to the left.
Yellow bubble with a stalk in front of it out to the right.
Red ball path running to the left, the give option of the RPO.

reads: Off the snap, read the box. If numbers favor the run, give it on the red path to the left.
If the box is stacked, pull and look the Pop seam up the middle when the safety sits.
If the seam is covered, dump the Bubble to the perimeter where the defender vacated.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes for the give and condensed coverage for the Pop. Coach's Key: 73 calls at 9.6 avg means the give is live, the Pop and Bubble keep second-level defenders honest.

name: snapfire_duo-7

routes.note: White blocking assignments, double teams working up to the linebackers.
Red ball-carrier path pressing straight downhill through the interior.

reads: Take the handoff and press the playside double team straight ahead.
Read the displaced linebacker off the double, then cut off whichever blocker climbs to him.
Stay patient, let the combo blocks vertical-push before declaring the lane.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts and one-gap aggressive linebackers. Coach's Key: this is a vertical double-team run, not a designed cutback, hit it downhill and let the back read the dislodged linebacker.

name: snapfire_chip_poco_x_dig

routes.note: Red. Primary, drives deep and breaks toward the upper right.
Yellow. Pushes up and breaks over the middle to the right.
Blue. Works underneath back across to the left.
Blue. Works underneath in from the right.
Blue. Chips at the line then releases up and digs across the middle.

reads: IF the top safety stays inside, THEN take the deep post / corner to the right.
IF the deep route is bracketed, THEN come down to the dig over the middle.
IF the middle is clogged, THEN work the underneath drags running away from leverage.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: the chip and drags clear traffic while the deep break and the X dig attack high-low over the middle. Verified: art labeled PASS; red deep break, yellow up-and-over, two blue drags, and blue RB chip-then-up all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_hb_orbit_mesh_spot

routes.note: Cyan. Orbits out of the backfield and bends up the right sideline.
Yellow. Comes underneath across to the left, mesh element.
Red. Settles in the short middle, the spot read.
Yellow. Stems up then breaks out and over the top.

reads: IF the underneath defender chases the cross, THEN hit the spot sitting behind it.
IF the spot is covered, THEN work the shallow cross running to open grass.
IF the flat clears out, THEN take the orbit back up the sideline on the wheel.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: mesh and spot create a triangle underneath, the orbit wheel stretches the sideline. Verified: art labeled PASS; cyan orbit wheel up the right sideline, yellow left shallow cross, red spot/sit, and yellow up-and-over all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_inside_zone_split

routes.note: Red. Takes the handoff and cuts up inside off the zone blocks.
White. Line blocks zone, working playside.
White. Split wide, blocks the perimeter.
White. Split wide, blocks the perimeter.

reads: IF the front flows playside, THEN cut back against the grain.
IF the backside stays honest, THEN press the playside zone hole.
IF the receivers seal their men, THEN bend it outside for extra yards.
usage_notes: Beats: light or spread-out boxes. Coach's Key: read the first down lineman, bang it up or cut it back off the zone blocks. Verified: art labeled RUN; red run track, white zone-block lines, and two white split-wide stalk blocks all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_z_shallow_cross

routes.note: Yellow stems vertical then breaks in across the middle
Yellow stems up then breaks in across the field
Red shallow crossing left to right underneath, the cross
Yellow releases vertical up the field on the right
Yellow climbs from the right side across to the middle

reads: IF defenders carry the digs, THEN hit the shallow cross underneath.
IF the shallow is jumped, THEN climb to the dig sitting behind it.
IF coverage sinks deep, THEN take the cross on the move for yards.
usage_notes: Beats: man and soft zone. Coach's Key: the cross is the rhythm throw, the digs are the high-low behind it.

name: snapfire_mtn_curls_texas

routes.note: Yellow shallow coming back across to the left
Yellow stems vertical then curls back
Yellow stems up then breaks back inside (Texas-style)
Red short vertical settling into a hitch
Cyan releases across underneath to the right

reads: IF the defender sits on the curl, THEN drive the back on the Texas underneath.
IF zone widens, THEN throw the curl in the open window.
IF nothing settles, THEN take the shallow drag for yards after catch.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with the curl-flat combo. Coach's Key: high-low the curl defender with the back underneath.

name: snapfire_y_stick-2

routes.note: Yellow stems then breaks out to the left sideline
Yellow stems vertical then breaks in across the middle
Red short vertical stick settling at the sticks, primary read
Yellow streak up the right sideline, clearout
Yellow releases out to the right flat under the stick

reads: IF the flat defender widens to the back, THEN hit the stick at the sticks.
IF the flat defender sits on the stick, THEN throw the back in the flat.
IF both are covered, THEN climb to the dig over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage on third and short. Coach's Key: classic stick-flat read off the flat defender.

name: snapfire_45_quick_base-3

routes.note: Line fires out on the quick base run scheme
Red arrow from the back into the interior gap, the ball-carrier

reads: IF the playside gap is open, THEN hit it fast downhill.
IF the front collapses inside, THEN bounce off the base block.
IF the second level fills, THEN get north and take what is there.
usage_notes: Beats: light, spread-out boxes. Coach's Key: quick-hitting base run, take the gap that opens and go.

name: snapfire_slot_stick

routes.note: Yellow stems vertical then breaks in across the middle
Yellow stems up then breaks in across the field
Red short vertical stick settling at the sticks, primary read
Yellow streak up the right sideline, clearout
Yellow releases out to the right flat under the stick

reads: IF the flat defender chases the back, THEN hit the stick at the sticks.
IF the flat defender squats on the stick, THEN throw the back in the flat.
IF both are covered, THEN work the dig over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage on third and medium. Coach's Key: stick-flat high-low off the flat defender, take what he gives.

name: snapfire_wide_zone_read

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path pressing flat to the right on the zone track
Green pull arrow showing the QB read/keep option back to the left

reads: Open up and run the wide zone, eye the backside read defender
If the read defender stays home or chases flat, hand it to the back on the zone path
If he crashes down on the back, pull it and keep on the green path
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing fronts and crashing backside ends. Coach's Key: read the backside, the green keep punishes over-pursuit.

name: snapfire_qb_blast-2

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow driving straight downhill behind the line
White block paths opening the interior gap for the QB

reads: Snap and get downhill behind the white blocks up the middle
Press the interior gap and read the lead block
Cut off the block and finish vertical for the tough yards
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft interior fronts. Coach's Key: QB run game, hit the inside gap fast and finish forward.

name: snapfire_hb_angle_screen

routes.note: Red primary read / ball-carrier: releases shallow and angles back inside to catch the screen behind the line
White: linemen release downfield to set up the screen blocks for the back
Yellow: runs up the left side to clear the defenders out of the screen area
Yellow: works out toward the right then up to clear and occupy the flat defender
Yellow: runs up the right side to clear coverage

reads: This is a screen to the back: let the rush come, then deliver to the red HB behind the linemen.
Wait for the white blockers to get out in front before throwing the angle screen.
If the screen is read and jumped, the clear-out verticals occupy the deep defenders.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive pass rush and blitzes. Coach's Key: invite the rush, throw the angle screen, then follow your blockers downfield.

name: snapfire_strong_flood-2

routes.note: Yellow: stems up the left then breaks hard out toward the sideline
Yellow: works inside then breaks back out and up toward the corner
Yellow: releases from the backfield to the flat, the low man in the flood
Red primary read: stems up then breaks across toward the right at intermediate depth
Red: pushes vertically then breaks back toward the right sideline
Yellow: runs straight up the right sideline as the high stretch

reads: This is a flood to the strong side: read the three levels (vertical high, intermediate middle, flat low) on one defender.
If the flat defender sinks, throw the flat; if he widens, hit the intermediate route behind him.
The vertical clears the top and holds the deep defender off the intermediate window.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with the three-level sideline stretch. Coach's Key: flood one side, put the flat defender in conflict and throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_y_corner-4

routes.note: Yellow: breaks out toward the left sideline at a shallow depth
Yellow: stems up then breaks out and up toward the left corner
Yellow: runs vertically up the middle to clear
Yellow: works inside across the middle from the right
Red primary read: stems up then breaks out and up to the corner (the Y corner)
Yellow: releases out shallow toward the right sideline

reads: The Y corner is the primary: read the red corner route breaking away from the underneath defender.
Use the flat route underneath to high-low the flat defender on the right side.
If the corner is bracketed deep, work the dig or the crossing routes over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 2 and zone with the corner-flat high-low on the flat defender. Coach's Key: corner over flat, throw whichever the flat defender does not take.

name: snapfire_y_drag

routes.note: Yellow: stems up the left then breaks inside across the middle
Red primary read: shallow crosser working left to right just behind the line
Yellow: shallow inside route working across underneath
Yellow: stems up then bends toward the deep middle
Yellow: stems up the right then breaks out and up toward the corner

reads: Read the red Y drag first as he crosses the formation underneath; it is the rhythm throw.
If the underneath is jammed, climb to the dig settling in behind the linebackers.
Backside post or corner is the shot when the coverage rolls to the crossers.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage (the drag wins on the move) and zone (drag finds the underneath hole). Coach's Key: throw the Y drag on the move, lead him across into space.

name: snapfire_speed_option-4

routes.note: Red: QB attacks the edge, reads the force defender and either keeps or pitches
Yellow: trails the QB in pitch relationship to receive the option pitch on the perimeter
White: receivers stalk block the perimeter defenders to spring the option

reads: This is a run: attack the edge and read the force/pitch-key defender.
If the key crashes the QB, pitch it to the back trailing in the pitch relationship.
If the key sits on the pitch, keep it and turn upfield off the receivers' blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive edge defenders who commit to one option. Coach's Key: option off the pitch key, make him wrong, take what he does not defend.

name: snapfire_y_sail-2

routes.note: Yellow: breaks out toward the left sideline at a shallow depth
Yellow: stems up then breaks inside across the middle
Yellow: works out and up toward the corner at intermediate depth (the sail)
Yellow: releases out shallow as the low man on the right
Red primary read: angles out then turns up the right sideline on the wheel
Yellow: runs straight up the right sideline as the clear-out

reads: The sail concept stretches the sideline: read the high-low on the flat defender to the right.
If the flat defender carries the wheel, drop it to the flat underneath him.
The backside dig and out give you the answer if the right side is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the sideline three-level stretch (vertical, sail/wheel, flat). Coach's Key: put the flat defender in conflict, throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_pa_slot_under

routes.note: Yellow: stems vertically up the left then breaks back toward the sideline
Yellow: works underneath across the middle from right to left (the slot under)
Yellow: short stem then breaks out toward the right underneath
Red primary read: stems up then breaks inside across the middle
Yellow: runs straight up the right side to clear

reads: Carry out the play-action fake, then read the red dig breaking in behind the linebackers.
If the middle is covered, take the slot-under drag working across underneath.
Backside comeback or the seam is the answer if the run fake does not hold the coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that bite on the play fake and lose the intermediate middle. Coach's Key: the fake holds the linebackers, the under route and dig come open behind them.

name: snapfire_y_cross_flood

routes.note: Blue motion-then-route: works underneath across to the left at a shallow depth
Blue: shallow inside route working across to the left
Red primary read: runs the deep crosser from right to left across the middle (the Y cross)
Yellow: releases out shallow toward the right as the flood low man
Yellow: stems up the right then breaks out and up toward the corner (flood high man)

reads: The Y cross is the primary: read the red deep crosser coming across the middle into the open window.
On the flood side, high-low the flat defender with the corner over the flat.
If the cross is covered, the underneath drags and flat give you the quick outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage (the deep cross wins on the move) and zone (flood stretches the flat defender). Coach's Key: pair the deep cross with the flood, throw the cross or work the high-low.

name: snapfire_reload_inside_zone

routes.note: White vertical blocking lines stepping playside in zone.
Cyan line, back comes across on motion then takes the inside zone path downhill behind the line.
White vertical lines, receivers blocking the perimeter on both sides.

reads: Take the zone track playside off the motion and read the first down lineman.
Press the playside A or B gap, bang it if the gap is open.
If the front spills it, cut back to the open backside lane.
usage_notes: Beats: flowing fronts. Coach's Key: the motion sets the back's track, read it on the run and bend or bang off the line's displacement.

name: snapfire_y_trail-2

routes.note: Yellow. Pushes vertical up the left numbers then bends back in across the top of the picture.
Yellow. Releases and runs a long crossing route bending all the way across the field toward the left.
Red primary read. Breaks in underneath the crosser and trails across the middle, the Y trail strike.
Yellow. Stems up the right sideline turning vertical to clear the deep defender.
Yellow. Releases out of the backfield underneath to the right flat as the checkdown.

reads: First look is the red trail route coming in underneath the deep cross over the middle.
If the trail is covered, the deep crosser running across the field is the next window.
The HB flat to the right is the quick outlet if the in-breakers are carried.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and zone over the middle. Coach's Key: the deep cross clears the grass, then the Y trails into the vacated window underneath.

name: snapfire_reload_flood_x_choice

routes.note: Yellow. Pushes up off the numbers then breaks flat out toward the left sideline.
Cyan. Releases out of the backfield underneath and works to the left flat, the checkdown.
Red primary read. Stems up then settles or breaks back, the option route that gives the X his choice off leverage.
Yellow. Runs vertical up the right sideline as the deep clear-out of the flood.
Yellow. Breaks out underneath toward the right sideline, the low man of the flood stretch.

reads: Read the X-choice settling route on the bunch side first off the defender's leverage.
On the flood side, high-low the flat defender with the vertical over the out underneath.
Back side, the isolated WR out and the HB swing give the quick outlet if the flood is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the three-level flood and man with the X-choice option. Coach's Key: take the choice route, then stretch the right flat defender high-low.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_bubble-5

routes.note: Red read. Bubbles back toward the left sideline as the alert throw. Drawn in red as the primary read.
Yellow. Takes the give and runs up through the interior gap.
White short stem settling near the line inside the bubble.

reads: IF the alert defender sits in the box, THEN throw the red bubble to the leverage outside.
IF the box stays light, THEN give on the inside run.
IF the flat defender widens to the bubble, THEN hand it off and run inside.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that don't account for the bubble flat. Coach's Key: read the alert defender, bubble it if he stays inside, give it if he widens. Art verified: RPO label, red bubble breaks back to the left sideline, yellow HB inside run.

name: snapfire_pa_te_out

routes.note: Red read. Runs flat across to the left underneath. Drawn in red as the primary.
Yellow. Short stem then turns back at a shallow depth on the left.
Yellow. Climbs and bends across to the deep middle from the left.
Yellow. Pushes up then breaks out toward the right sideline at intermediate depth.
Yellow. Short break toward the right sideline near the line.

reads: IF the play-action holds the second level, THEN the TE out comes open in the right window.
IF the flat defender jumps the out, THEN climb to the deep cross behind it.
IF coverage drops deep, THEN check the red drag underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: zones that bite on the run fake. Coach's Key: sell the fake, then work the TE out off the play-action. Art verified: PASS label, red drag runs left underneath, yellow TE out breaks to the right sideline, deep cross climbs from the left.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_flat

routes.note: Red read. Runs flat across to the right at the line as the read throw. Drawn in red as the primary.
Yellow. Releases out to the left flat underneath.
Yellow. Vertical stems then turn back at a shallow depth on the left.
Yellow. Climbs straight up the right sideline clearing deep.

reads: IF the flat defender stays inside or in the box, THEN throw the red flat to the leverage.
IF the flat defender expands, THEN check to the inside run or the back in the left flat.
IF coverage squeezes the flat, THEN take the vertical clearing up the right side.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that don't honor the quick flat. Coach's Key: read the flat defender, throw the red flat now if he sits inside. Art verified: RPO label, red flat runs across to the right, yellow back swings to the left flat, vertical clears up the right.

name: snapfire_pa_jet_cross

routes.note: Red read. Climbs and breaks across to the left at intermediate depth. Drawn in red as the primary.
Red. Pushes up from the right and crosses to the left. Second red crossing element.
Yellow. Bends up the left then curls back, looping high over the top.
Cyan motion running across the formation from left to right and out to the right flat.
Yellow. Works back toward the right sideline at intermediate depth.

reads: IF the jet motion pulls the flat defender, THEN the red crosser opens behind it.
IF the underneath defenders carry the crossers, THEN check the jet swing in the right flat.
IF the coverage stays deep, THEN take the comeback to the open sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage stressed by the jet action and crossing routes. Coach's Key: the jet holds the flat, the red crossers come open across the middle. Art verified: PASS label, cyan jet motion runs across to the right flat, red crossers break to the left, yellow curls and comebacks fill the field.

name: snapfire_read_option-5

routes.note: Yellow. Takes the mesh path and runs up through the interior gap on the give read.
Red ball-carrier arrow showing the QB keep path bending up out of the mesh.
White vertical stems clearing out downfield on the left.

reads: IF the read defender crashes the back, THEN the QB pulls and keeps it.
IF the read defender stays home on the QB, THEN give it to the back inside.
IF the second level overflows, THEN cut north behind the down blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: edge defenders that commit early. Coach's Key: read the unblocked end, give or keep off his leverage. Art verified: RUN label, yellow back give path and red QB keep arrow form the mesh point in the right-center gap, white backside verticals clear.

name: snapfire_z_under

routes.note: Yellow. Breaks flat to the left sideline underneath.
Yellow. Pushes vertical then breaks back across to the left.
Yellow. Stems up and breaks out to the right sideline.
Yellow. Releases and works underneath across the field.
Red primary read. The Z works underneath across the middle to the left.

reads: Pre-snap: identify man or zone to know how the under route will come open.
IF the under (red) clears the wash and finds grass, THEN deliver it on the move.
IF the underneath is covered, THEN climb to the deep crosser from the left stack.
IF both crossers are bracketed, THEN check the out routes to either sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and zone holes underneath. Coach's Key: the Z under is the answer, the deep in and the sideline outs stretch coverage off it.

name: snapfire_stick_n_nod

routes.note: Yellow. Breaks flat to the left sideline underneath.
Yellow. Releases straight up the left sideline.
Red primary read. Shows the stick then climbs vertically up the seam to the deep middle.
Yellow. Works underneath back across to the left.
Yellow. Runs straight up the right sideline.

reads: Pre-snap: read the flat and hook defender over the right trey.
IF the defender bites the stick fake, THEN throw the nod up the seam (red) for the explosive shot.
IF the defender stays deep, THEN settle for the underneath drag working across.
IF the middle is plugged, THEN check the left-side out or the outside verticals.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive underneath defenders who jump the stick. Coach's Key: sell the stick to bait the squat, then deliver the nod up the seam behind him.

name: snapfire_rpo_zone_alert_omaha-2

routes.note: White run path shows the back's zone aiming point through the interior
Yellow short stem breaking inside as the quick alert read
Red alert read shows the receiver's break to the right side as the pass option
White blocking stems flowing on the zone

reads: Read the box, if the numbers favor the run, hand off the inside zone.
If the alert defender crashes, pull and throw the quick alert route.
Take the easy completion outside rather than force the run into a loaded box.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that overload the box. Coach's Key: the zone read plus the quick alert punishes whichever choice the conflict defender makes.

name: snapfire_smash_dagger

routes.note: Yellow short break out toward the left, smash underneath
Red primary read shows a vertical stem then break to the corner (smash top)
Yellow vertical up the field to clear the dagger window
Yellow deep stem then breaks in across the middle on the dig

reads: Read the smash side first, high-low the flat defender with the out and the corner.
If the corner is bracketed, work the dagger dig over the middle.
Let the vertical clear the middle before throwing the dig behind it.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 2 and zone over the middle. Coach's Key: smash high-lows the corner, dagger clears and digs the middle.

name: snapfire_smash_hammer

routes.note: Red primary read stems up then breaks to the corner (smash top)
Yellow short out underneath the corner on the left
Yellow stem turning back over the middle right
Yellow vertical stem up the field on the right
Blue checkdown swinging out to the left flat

reads: Read the smash high-low, the corner over the flat against the cornerback.
If the corner squats, throw the corner route over the top.
If the corner bails, drop it to the flat or the swing underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: cover 2 and soft corners. Coach's Key: the corner and the flat put the cornerback in a bind, throw off his leverage.

name: snapfire_rpo_zone_alert_glance

routes.note: White run path shows the back's zone aiming point through the interior
Yellow short stem breaking inside on the glance as the quick alert read
Red alert read shows the receiver's deeper break inside as the pass option
White blocking stems flowing on the zone

reads: Read the box, if light, hand off the inside zone.
If the alert defender triggers down, pull and throw the glance.
Take the glance window quick before the safety can rally.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that drop a safety into the box. Coach's Key: the glance attacks the space the run-fit defender vacates.

name: snapfire_dig_hb_option

routes.note: Yellow flat route working out to the left
Red primary read stems up then breaks in across the middle on the dig
Yellow short stem with an option break sitting in the underneath zone
Yellow shallow crosser working back underneath to the left
Yellow vertical stem then break out to the right sideline

reads: Work the dig over the middle as the primary read.
If the dig is covered, take the HB option sitting in the soft spot.
Underneath drag and out routes are the outlets versus zone.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: the dig and the option route stress the hook defender at two levels.

name: snapfire_pivot_return

routes.note: Yellow stem up then breaks out to the left sideline
Red primary read line works flat across to the right toward the pivot
Yellow short stem that breaks in then pivots back out (return) near the right
Yellow stem up then breaks out to the right sideline

reads: Read the pivot/return as the primary, throw it as the man defender trails.
If the pivot is squeezed, work the quick outs on either sideline.
Take what the leverage gives, pivot in against outside leverage, out against inside.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: the pivot return shakes a trailing defender, throw to the open hip on the break.

name: snapfire_hammer_dagger

routes.note: Yellow flat route working out to the left
Red primary read line works flat across to the right
Yellow vertical stem up the field to clear the dagger window
Yellow short stem turning back over the middle right
Yellow deep stem then breaks in across the middle on the dig

reads: Let the vertical clear the middle, then throw the dig in behind it.
If the dig is bracketed, work the underneath curl or the flat.
Take the quick hammer underneath against soft coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: dagger clears the hook with the seam, the dig settles in the vacated window.

name: snapfire_corner_strike-4

routes.note: Yellow stem up then breaks in across the middle
Yellow short stem turning back over the middle
Red primary read stems up then breaks to the right corner (the strike)
Yellow flat route working out under the corner to the right

reads: Read the corner route as the primary strike against single coverage.
If the corner is bracketed, work the dig over the middle.
Take the underneath out as the outlet if the deep shot is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high looks on the corner. Coach's Key: drop the strike to the back-corner shoulder away from the safety.

name: snapfire_mtn_flood_h_choice

routes.note: red, releases vertically to depth then breaks hard left and runs across the deep middle, this is the drawn primary

routes.route: Jet motion across to a flat / drag left
routes.note: cyan into yellow, motions from the right across the formation and runs flat to the far left, cyan marks the pre-snap motion
yellow, stems straight up the field to the deep left
yellow, vertical stem then an option break at intermediate depth, the choice route the play is named for

routes.route: Crosser / over to the far right sideline
routes.note: yellow, runs horizontally across at intermediate depth toward the right sideline, the deep level of the flood
yellow, intermediate break working back to the inside-left, fills the short level of the flood

routes.route: Release up into the right-side concept
routes.note: yellow, the back starts below the line then releases up to join the right-side routes

reads: First look the red dig coming open across the deep middle.
If the dig is covered, work the H-Choice option route to whatever window the defender gives.
Use the flood routes to the right to high-low the intermediate level.
Late, check the motion man on the flat underneath against man.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the flood stretch, plus a man-beater in the H-Choice option route. Coach's Key: the red dig is the primary, the H-Choice and the right-side flood are your built-in answers underneath.

name: snapfire_z_spot_dig

routes.note: yellow, shallow break working to the left
yellow, vertical stem then breaks toward the left deep corner
yellow, vertical then deep in-break across the middle
yellow, breaks to the right sideline
red, short release then hooks back into the right hook window, the primary

reads: Read the red Z spot sitting in the right hook window first.
Climb to the dig over the middle if the spot is covered.
Work the corner or the out to the right as the field stretches.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the spot-dig high-low. Coach's Key: the red spot is the trigger, the dig works behind it over the middle.

name: snapfire_orbit_whip_flood

routes.note: yellow, short break to the left
yellow, short vertical then breaks out to the left flat area
red, shallow in-breaker working across the middle, a primary read
yellow, breaks toward the right sideline deep
cyan, orbit motion across then turns up the right sideline on the flood

reads: Read the flood concept stretching the right sideline vertically.
Hit the orbit wheel up the sideline if the flat is cleared.
Work the red whip/drag underneath over the middle as the answer.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the flood and orbit stressing the sideline. Coach's Key: cyan orbit creates the flood, the red whip is the quick inside answer.

name: snapfire_orbit_z_spot

routes.note: yellow, short vertical then hooks back toward the ball
yellow, vertical stem then deep in-break across the middle
red, short release then hooks back into the right hook window, the primary
yellow, stems up then breaks toward the right deep corner
cyan, orbit motion across underneath then releases to the right flat

reads: Read the red Z spot sitting in the right hook window first.
Climb to the dig or corner if the spot is bracketed.
Take the orbit back to the flat as the outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the spot in the hook window. Coach's Key: cyan orbit sets the flat outlet, the red spot is the primary sit-down.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-15

routes.note: red, takes the handoff and runs the inside zone lane behind the line
white vertical stems, line zone-steps and drives in unison
white stems, receivers block the perimeter defenders

reads: Take the handoff and read the first down-lineman to the play side.
Press the inside zone lane and read the climbing blocks.
Cut back only if the front-side flow over-pursues.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts with zone blocking, hit the open lane. Coach's Key: standard inside zone, press and read the front-side block.

name: snapfire_redzone_hb_scissors

routes.note: yellow, stems up then breaks toward the left deep corner
yellow, shallow in-breaker working to the right underneath
red, breaks deep toward the right corner/post, the scissors over route, primary
yellow, breaks out toward the right sideline crossing under the red
yellow, releases underneath as the outlet

reads: In the red zone, read the scissors crossing routes on the right.
Throw the red deep breaker over the crossing out route against man.
Take the underneath drag or the back in the flat if both are covered.
usage_notes: Beats: man in the red zone with the scissors rub on the right. Coach's Key: the two right-side routes cross to free the red breaker, throw the man-beater.

name: snapfire_drive-2

routes.note: yellow, shallow break working to the left
yellow, vertical stem then breaks toward the left deep corner
red, deep in-break across the middle, the drive dig, the primary read
yellow, shallow crosser working to the left under the dig
blue, releases underneath to the right as the outlet

reads: Read the red drive dig coming open over the middle first.
Throw it in the window as it clears the underneath defenders.
Take the shallow drag underneath or the back in the flat if the dig is bracketed.
usage_notes: Beats: zone with the classic drive dig-and-shallow combo. Coach's Key: the red dig is the primary, the shallow drag is the man/zone outlet underneath.

name: snapfire_mesh_spot-3

routes.note: yellow, vertical up the left side
yellow, runs the mesh across the middle from the left
yellow, runs the mesh across from the right, the other half of the mesh
yellow, hooks back into the right hook window as the spot
red, releases and turns up the right sideline as the vertical, a key read

reads: Read the mesh crossers rubbing through the middle first.
Let the spot curl settle in the right hook window as the sit-down.
Climb to the red vertical up the right sideline against man.
usage_notes: Beats: man with the mesh rub and the spot sit-down underneath. Coach's Key: mesh crossers clear traffic, the spot is the zone answer, take the open level.

name: snapfire_hb_stretch

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path, takes the handoff and stretches toward the right edge.
White stems, line blocks zone reaching to the playside right.
White lines, perimeter receivers stalk block to seal the edge.

reads: IF the edge is sealed, THEN bounce the stretch outside.
IF the defense overflows wide, THEN cut up through the first open gap inside.
IF the backside crashes, THEN press playside and stay patient behind the zone blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: slower edge defenders and over-pursuing fronts. Coach's Key: stretch the front horizontally, then one cut downhill (183 calls, 10.3 avg, proven).

name: snapfire_y_trail-3

routes.note: Red primary, releases from the right and trails across the field on a deep crossing track.
Yellow, drives up and breaks out toward the left sideline.
Yellow, stems up then breaks in across the middle to the right.
Yellow, vertical stem then breaks out toward the right sideline.
Blue line, releases out underneath to the left as the dump.

reads: IF the trailing Y crosser comes open across the middle, THEN that is the primary throw.
IF the middle is congested, THEN work the dig and out breaks to either sideline.
IF coverage holds deep, THEN take the back in the flat underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the deep trail crosser, plus zone with the high-low sidelines. Coach's Key: let the Y trail develop across the field as the money read.

name: snapfire_te_option

routes.note: Yellow route stemming up then breaking back across toward the left
Yellow route working inside then back out, an option-style break
Yellow route up the field on the right
Red primary read working underneath across toward the right

reads: Read the option route off the defender's leverage.
If the defender sits inside, the route breaks out, and vice versa.
If the underneath is taken, climb to the vertical or crosser.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: let the option route win on leverage, throw away from the defender's hips.

name: snapfire_hb_stretch-2

routes.note: White blocks reaching playside toward the right
Red ball-carrier path stretching wide to the playside (right) before turning up

reads: Take the handoff and stretch the run to the playside.
If the edge sets hard, bend it up inside the reach block.
Press the perimeter, read the blocks, and get vertical.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-flowing fronts. Coach's Key: stretch the defense horizontally, one cut up and go.

name: snapfire_mtn_counter_solid

routes.note: White/cyan blocks with playside down blocks and pull action
Red ball-carrier path taking a counter step then cutting back through the gap
Cyan path showing the pulling blocker leading into the hole

reads: Take the counter step to hold the second-level defenders.
Follow the puller into the gap.
If the hole is muddy, press the block and cut off the lead.
usage_notes: Beats: fast-flowing linebackers. Coach's Key: counter footwork freezes pursuit, run off the puller in the solid front.

name: snapfire_hb_blunt_dive-2

routes.note: White blocks sealing the interior gaps
Red ball-carrier path hitting the interior gap straight downhill

reads: Take the handoff and hit the interior gap fast.
Stay tight to the down blocks.
Get downhill, no dancing, fall forward.
usage_notes: Beats: light interior boxes and short-yardage looks. Coach's Key: quick-hitting dive, attack the first daylight inside.

name: snapfire_mesh_spot-4

routes.note: Yellow route running across the field to the left flat underneath.
Red route (primary read) crossing from right to left underneath, meeting the other shallow.
Yellow route releasing vertically then breaking out toward the right sideline.
Yellow route working to a spot underneath in the right-side window.

reads: IF the underneath defenders widen, THEN hit the mesh crosser (red) on the rub.
IF a defender sits on the crosser, THEN climb to the corner/out breaking to the right sideline.
IF nothing opens, THEN check the spot sitting underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the mesh rub. Coach's Key: let the two shallow crossers clear traffic, then work crosser to corner to spot.

name: snapfire_speed_option-5

routes.note: Red path bends to the right toward the edge, QB attacks the pitch key.
Green arrow running parallel to the right, the pitch man stays in pitch relationship.

reads: IF the pitch key attacks the QB, THEN pitch it to the back running right (green path).
IF the pitch key widens to the back, THEN the QB keeps it and turns upfield (red path).
usage_notes: Beats: edge pressure and slow-flowing defenses. Coach's Key: attack the pitch key downhill, make a fast clean decision on the perimeter.

name: snapfire_wr_stick_n_out

routes.note: Yellow route breaks back inside across the field to the left.
Red route to the left flat, drawn red as the primary read on the stick-out combo.
Two yellow routes form a mesh/cross over the middle, one in, one out.

routes.route: Comeback / deep out up the right
routes.note: Yellow route stems vertical then works back toward the right sideline.
Yellow route releases from the backfield and bends up the right sideline.

reads: Read the stick-out: throw the out if the flat is open.
If the flat defender widens, hit the stick sitting inside him.
If both are covered, climb to the crossers over the middle or the back up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage, high-volume staple (83 calls, 6.1 avg). Coach's Key: classic stick-out flat read, take the easy completion and let runners work.

name: snapfire_hb_zone_wk

routes.note: Red line shows the back angling up into the weak-side gap at the line of scrimmage.
White blocking marks across the front; line steps in zone to the weak side.

reads: Press the weak-side gap behind the zone steps.
If the front overflows, cut back behind the block.
Stay downhill, one cut and go.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses overloading the strong side. Coach's Key: weak-side inside zone, attack the lighter box and stay vertical.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_flat_wheel-2

routes.note: Red line through the line of scrimmage marks the run path and the ball-carrier read.
Yellow route to the left flat, the quick RPO throw.
Yellow route runs vertically up the left sideline behind the flat.
Yellow route breaks back across to the inside.
Yellow route stems up and works to the right boundary.

reads: Read the conflict defender: give the inside run if he widens.
If he sits on the box, pull and throw the flat.
If the flat defender jumps it, look to the wheel climbing up the sideline behind.
usage_notes: Beats: defenders caught between run fit and the flat. Coach's Key: run-first RPO, flat is the easy pull throw and the wheel punishes flat-jumpers.

name: snapfire_read_option-6

routes.note: Red carry lane straight ahead off the mesh read
Green arrow give path to the right, the read key
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front

reads: IF the read key crashes the give, THEN pull and keep on the red lane.
IF the key stays home, THEN give the zone run to the HB.
Eyes on the unblocked end the whole mesh.
usage_notes: Beats: ends that commit to the dive. Coach's Key: classic zone-read, the unblocked end decides give or keep.

name: snapfire_inside_zone_split-2

routes.note: Red carry path downhill behind the line with a split-zone angle
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front, backside slice

reads: IF the front flows playside, THEN press the zone and read the first down lineman.
IF the backside crashes, THEN the split block seals him and the cutback opens.
Be patient, let the double teams climb before you commit.
usage_notes: Beats: backside penetration, the split block answers the chase. Coach's Key: read it playside first, cutback off the slice.

name: snapfire_hb_stretch-3

routes.note: Red path angling out to the right edge, stretching the front horizontally
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White block tics across the front, reaching playside

reads: IF the edge gets reached, THEN bounce it outside.
IF the front spills, THEN plant and cut up into the seam.
Press the edge to stretch the defense before you decide bounce or cut.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-flowing interior fronts. Coach's Key: stretch the front horizontally, then read bounce or bang it up.

name: snapfire_shock-2

routes.note: Red flat route running to the left, primary read
Yellow short stem squaring out to the left
Yellow climbing up and across the field
Yellow short stem settling back to the left
Yellow bending up the right sideline then back

reads: IF the underneath flat is open, THEN throw the red shallow now.
IF defenders jump the shallow, THEN hit the crosser climbing behind it.
IF coverage rotates, THEN work the right-side comeback as the outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the shallow-cross rub. Coach's Key: shallow first, crosser second, let the routes pick the defenders.

name: snapfire_quick_slants

routes.note: Red slant breaking inside to the right, the primary read
Yellow slant breaking inside up and to the right
Yellow breaking flat to the left sideline
Short yellow stem
Yellow running flat across to the right

reads: IF the leverage is outside, THEN throw the red slant breaking inside.
IF the slant is covered, THEN check the flat underneath.
Throw it on rhythm, this is a quick-game timing concept.
usage_notes: Beats: off and soft coverage, quick rhythm throws. Coach's Key: hit the slant on time, take the easy completion.

name: snapfire_jet_touch_pass-9

routes.note: Cyan flat path crossing the formation, receives the quick touch pass on the move
White-gray stem releasing up and out to the right edge
White block tics across the front
White vertical stem on the far left

reads: IF the perimeter is clear, THEN deliver the quick touch pass to the jet man on the move.
IF the edge sets the wall, THEN let the jet man press it and cut up.
Get the ball out fast, this is a glorified outside-run timing throw.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses slow to widen with jet motion. Coach's Key: touch pass replaces the handoff, hit him in stride on the perimeter.

name: shinobi_edge_blitz_3

routes.note: Red arrows show the down linemen and both inside players firing across the line, with pressure flaring to the edges
Two purple zones drop into the short middle and the flats
Three blue zones split the deep field into thirds behind the rush

reads: Pre-snap: count the box. Five red rushers means the offense has to block everyone, so confirm your protection is outnumbered before you trust it.
If the QB holds the ball, the edge pressure gets home off the outside.
If the QB throws hot, the two underneath purple defenders are sitting on the quick hook and flat windows.
Deep balls are covered three-deep, so keep everything in front.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-developing dropback passes. Coach's Key: five-man edge pressure with a three-deep, two-under shell behind it.

name: shinobi_cover_3_sky

routes.note: Red arrows show only the three down linemen attacking the line
Two pale zones drop and pattern-match the intermediate middle
Three blue zones cover the deep thirds

reads: Pre-snap: only three rushers (red), so expect everyone else to bail into coverage.
If the offense runs vertical, the three blue deep zones cap each third of the field.
If the QB checks down, the underneath defenders are squeezing the intermediate windows.
Be patient: with a three-man rush you are trading pressure for coverage, so keep the play in front of you.
usage_notes: Beats: deep shots and four-vertical concepts. Coach's Key: three-man rush, three-deep zone with underneath help, keep it all in front.

name: shinobi_mike_blitz_0

routes.note: Five red arrows fan out from the line, every front defender attacking a gap
Defenders marked only with X's, no zone bubbles drawn, consistent with man/Cover 0 behind the blitz

reads: Pre-snap: five red rushers and no zone bubbles drawn means there is no deep safety help. This is an all-out pressure.
If the protection holds, the Mike and the front overwhelm the gaps for a quick sack.
Because there is no deep help, win up front fast or the offense can take a shot over the top.
Time it: this is a get-home-now call, so the pressure has to arrive before the ball does.
usage_notes: Beats: max-protect and slow-developing plays. Coach's Key: all-out gap pressure with no deep safety, all risk, all reward.

name: shinobi_3_double_cloud

routes.note: Two short red arrows show the down linemen pressing the line
Two teal zones sink down toward the sidelines on each side
Pale and green zones drop over the middle, white lines show them settling
Three blue zones cap the deep field in thirds

reads: Pre-snap: only three red rushers, so this is a coverage call with the flats doubled by the teal cloud defenders.
