IF the offense leaves a free rusher, THEN that LB is your unblocked hitter at the mesh.
IF the QB drops to pass, THEN bring the heat, the all-out rush has to get home quickly with thin coverage behind.
usage_notes: Beats: short-yardage runs of any kind by flooding every gap. Coach's Key: linebackers join the rush, so this is a gamble for a stuffed run, do not call it when you fear a play-action shot.

name: shinobi_mlb_gap_a

routes.note: Six red squares on the line, red arrows pointing straight ahead into their assigned gaps.
The middle rusher's arrow converges toward the center, sending the MLB through the A gap right at the ball.
The rushers on either side fire straight down to keep their gaps filled while the MLB attacks the A.
White dots and X behind the line, no drawn drops, covering the goal line.

reads: IF the QB or back attacks the middle, THEN the A gap MLB should be unblocked in his lap.
IF the line accounts for the MLB, THEN a flanking gap rusher comes free instead.
IF it is a pass, THEN the A gap shot pressures the QB up the gut, get hands in the lane.
usage_notes: Beats: QB sneaks and inside dives. Coach's Key: the MLB is the dagger through the A gap, time the snap and meet the ball at the center.

name: shinobi_60_pinch_goal_line_blitz

routes.note: Red arrows all angle inward toward the center, the linemen slant down into the interior gaps
White dot stays in the box behind the pinch
Second white dot stays in the box behind the pinch
End mark also angles in toward the middle with the pinch

reads: If they run it up the gut, the pinch slants everyone into the interior gaps and walls off the middle.
If they try to bounce it outside, the linebackers and edge have to scrape over the top since the line is pinching in.
If it is a quick pass, the inside-angled rush still collapses the pocket from the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: interior dives and quarterback sneaks up the middle. Coach's Key: the pinch surrenders the edge to load the inside, so it is strongest against between-the-tackles runs.

name: shinobi_goal_line_6_2_base

routes.note: Leftmost white square, red arrow straight downhill into the gap
White square, red arrow firing forward off the line
White square, red arrow straight ahead
White square, red arrow straight ahead
White square, red arrow firing forward off the line
Rightmost white square, red arrow straight downhill into the gap
Left white circle stacked behind the front, no arrow drawn
Right white circle stacked behind the front, no arrow drawn
White X to the left at the line, no route drawn
White X on the right at the line, no route drawn
White X right side set slightly off the line, no route drawn

reads: Pre-snap: all six down linemen show red arrows, so everyone up front is firing off the ball at the snap.
If it is a run, the six rushers crash their gaps and the two linebackers fill the first crease that opens.
If it is a pass, get hands up and collapse the pocket fast since there is no deep help drawn.
Trust the two linebackers and the defensive backs to clean up anything that slips through the wall.
usage_notes: Beats: short-yardage and goal-line runs up the middle. Coach's Key: it is an all-out 6-man front, six down linemen blitz their gaps while the two linebackers and the defensive backs play behind the wall. No deep coverage is drawn, so this is a stop-the-run, stuff-it-at-the-line call.

name: shinobi_sam_will_blitz

routes.note: Six red arrows shown crossing at the line, heavy blitz with linebackers adding to the front
Linebackers fire downhill, the angled red arrows cross the gaps

reads: IF the protection slides one way, THEN one of the blitzers hits the opposite gap clean.
IF the offense keeps a back in, THEN you still bring more than they can block.
IF they release everyone, THEN get home fast because the coverage behind is thin.
usage_notes: Beats: empty and quick-snap looks. Coach's Key: all-out pressure, no deep help drawn, must get home.

name: shinobi_mike_will_blitz

routes.note: Multiple red arrows off the line, base front plus linebackers
Inside linebackers fire through the interior gaps, angled red arrows

reads: IF the center turns to one linebacker, THEN the other comes free up the middle.
IF the offense max protects, THEN make them hold the ball and rely on the rush.
IF the back leaks out, THEN the pressure should arrive before he is a real option.
usage_notes: Beats: interior protection and slow developing pass plays. Coach's Key: inside linebacker pressure up the gut.

name: shinobi_cover_3_sky-8

routes.note: Four red arrows at the line, base four-man rush
Blue deep zones with white coverage lines, three over the top
Purple underneath droppers with a strong-side flat defender
Cyan line shows a defender shifting to match the formation

reads: IF a receiver attacks the seam, THEN the deep zone caps it and the flat defender sinks under.
IF the ball goes to the flat, THEN the strong-side underneath defender drives on it.
IF the route combo floods one side, THEN pass off and keep three over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: flat and out concepts with the safety rolled down. Coach's Key: three deep, strong safety to the flat.

name: shinobi_tampa_2-11

routes.note: red arrow
red arrow
red arrow
red arrow
cyan flat, jam and sink
cyan flat, jam and sink
green vertical, carries the deep hole
white underneath zone
white underneath zone
blue deep half
blue deep half

reads: IF the seam route shows up the middle, THEN the Mike runs to the deep hole and takes it away.
IF the offense attacks the flats, THEN the corners sink and squeeze underneath while the safeties stay on top.
IF nothing crosses your face, THEN drop to your landmark and rally to the throw.
usage_notes: Beats: deep middle seams and four verticals (the Mike caps the hole). Coach's Key: Mike must carry the pole, two-deep safeties split the field.

name: shinobi_strong_slant_3

routes.note: red arrow, line slants strong
red arrow
red arrow
red arrow
purple/blue deep, left
white/yellow short zone over the middle
purple/blue deep, right
blue deep, middle of field

reads: IF the line slants to the strong side, THEN the front gets movement and you bring pressure off that edge.
IF the back releases away from the slant, THEN your underneath dropper has to wall him.
IF the ball comes out quick, THEN trust three-deep behind the pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: predictable strong-side runs and quarterbacks who sit in the pocket. Coach's Key: it is a slanting-front blitz with three deep behind, set the slant to the run strength.

name: shinobi_cover_2_man-4

routes.note: red arrow
red arrow
red arrow
red arrow, slight inside flare
blue deep half
blue deep half
corners and linebackers locked man-to-man under the two-deep shell

reads: IF you get four-man pressure, THEN the two safeties cap the deep halves while everyone underneath plays man.
IF your man breaks deep, THEN funnel him to your safety help inside.
IF the offense runs mesh or crossers, THEN trail tight and lean on the safeties over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: deep one-on-one shots (you have two-deep help). Coach's Key: trail technique underneath, push every vertical to the safeties.

name: shinobi_sam_will_blitz-2

routes.note: six red arrows off the line, the front four plus the Sam and Will linebackers crashing
X markers sit downfield with no coverage lines drawn, art is rush-only

reads: IF the protection holds the front, THEN the Sam and Will arrive on the edges for pressure.
IF the back stays in to block, THEN one extra rusher should still come free.
IF the QB gets the ball out fast, THEN the rush has to collapse the pocket before the throw.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-developing pass concepts behind a six-man pressure. Coach's Key: this is a heavy rush look, the linebackers add to the front so the coverage behind is thin.

name: shinobi_cover_2_man-5

routes.note: four white box markers with red arrows rushing upfield
left blue deep zone with drop line into the half
right blue deep zone with drop line into the half
two-high shell over man underneath, MAN label bottom-left

reads: IF a receiver runs vertical, THEN the man defender trails inside and the deep half safety caps it over the top.
IF a route breaks to the sideline deep, THEN the near safety drives on it from his half.
IF a back releases, THEN his man defender carries him man to man.
usage_notes: Beats: short and intermediate man-beating routes with two-deep help. Coach's Key: two safeties split the deep field, everyone else locks man so the rush has to get home.

name: shinobi_cover_1_hole-5

routes.note: Single high blue deep zone over the top
Green short middle hole zone underneath the FS
White man-match markers spread across the field, each locked on a man
Four red rush arrows off the front

reads: IF the offense runs a vertical, THEN the single-high free safety helps over the top.
IF a crosser comes through the middle, THEN the green hole defender robs it.
IF your man breaks, THEN trail in man coverage and rely on the FS over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: man-beating in-breakers, the hole defender takes away the dig and shallow cross. Coach's Key: lock man across, one deep safety, one robber in the hole.

name: shinobi_casino_blitz

routes.note: Red arrows fire straight ahead through the line.
Two white circles trigger downhill and join the red rush.
Defenders stay over the top with no zone drawn, playing the receivers.

reads: IF protection slides away, THEN the extra blitzers come free.
IF the back checks the blitz, THEN take him man-to-man out of the backfield.
IF the throw goes hot, THEN the cover defenders break on it fast.
usage_notes: Beats: empty and spread looks that leave the middle light. Coach's Key: gamble pressure with two extra rushers, man behind.

name: shinobi_cover_4_quarters-7

routes.note: Four blue zones split the deep field into quarters.
Purple zones drop underneath to carry the inside routes.
Four red arrows rush the line at the snap.

reads: IF receivers push vertical, THEN each quarters defender carries his man deep.
IF only one receiver goes vertical, THEN the quarters defender robs the underneath.
IF they run it, THEN the safeties trigger downhill to fill.
usage_notes: Beats: deep shots and four verticals. Coach's Key: four over the top, soft underneath, safeties read run-pass.

name: shinobi_ws_blitz_1

routes.note: Five red arrows rush across the line at the snap.
One blue zone sits over the deep middle as the lone deep help.
Defenders lock the receivers with no underneath zones drawn.

reads: IF the offense keeps the back in, THEN the fifth rusher comes free.
IF a receiver wins inside, THEN the single-high safety leans to that side.
IF they go max protect, THEN the man defenders stay glued and the safety helps deep.
usage_notes: Beats: dropback passing with limited hot answers. Coach's Key: five-man weak-side pressure with single-high man behind.

name: shinobi_ss_lb_blitz

routes.note: Red arrows rush straight through the line gaps.
Two white circles trigger forward and join the red rush.
Defenders play the receivers man-to-man with no deep zone drawn.

reads: IF the protection cannot account for all rushers, THEN a blitzer comes clean.
IF the back releases, THEN a defender carries him man-to-man.
IF the throw is quick, THEN the man defenders break and rally.
usage_notes: Beats: heavy sets and slow developing plays. Coach's Key: bring the safety and backer downhill, lock man behind it (high risk).

name: shinobi_cover_1_hole-6

routes.note: One blue zone plays the deep middle over the top.
Yellow zone sits in the short middle as the rat in the hole.
Defenders lock the receivers man-to-man.
Four red arrows rush the line at the snap.

reads: IF a crosser comes through the middle, THEN the hole defender robs it.
IF a receiver runs vertical, THEN the man defender stays on top with single-high help.
IF they dump it underneath, THEN the man defender drives downhill to tackle.
usage_notes: Beats: crossing routes and mesh concepts. Coach's Key: man-free with a robber sitting in the hole.

name: shinobi_cover_3_buzz_match-2

routes.note: Three blue zones split the deep field into thirds and match.
A defender drops down into the curl / hook to buzz the middle.
Purple zones match the underneath inside routes.
Four red arrows rush the line at the snap.

reads: IF a receiver runs vertical, THEN the deep-third defender carries him.
IF a route works the seam, THEN the buzz defender walls it underneath.
IF the offense floods a third, THEN match and pass off the next threat.
usage_notes: Beats: vertical and seam concepts. Coach's Key: three deep with a safety buzzing into the hook, match underneath.

name: shinobi_will_go_fire_3

routes.note: Red arrow shooting forward from the second level
Row of red rush arrows at the line
Blue lines pulling the deep bubbles back
Purple lines into the mid hook bubbles

reads: Pre-snap: identify the Will linebacker firing through the gap.
If the back picks up the Will, the four-man rush still has to win.
If the QB throws hot behind the blitz, the three-deep zones cap the field.
usage_notes: Beats: interior protection that misses the Will. Coach's Key: BLITZ tagged with three-deep zone behind it, this is a fire-zone look.

name: shinobi_cover_1_hole-7

routes.note: Blue line up to the deep bubble
Light/green line sitting in the underneath middle bubble
Four short red arrows showing man leverage at the line

reads: Pre-snap: confirm one deep safety with a robber sitting in the hole.
If a crosser comes through the middle, the hole defender robs it.
If receivers run vertical, man defenders carry and the safety caps over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: crossing routes and dig concepts over the middle. Coach's Key: COVERAGE, man-under with a free safety on top and a robber in the hole.

name: shinobi_tampa_2-12

routes.note: Two blue lines into the deep-half bubbles
Light/green line carrying up the middle seam
Cyan lines into the two outside short bubbles
White lines into the inside hook bubbles
Short red arrows off the line

reads: Pre-snap: two-high shell, the Mike will run the deep middle.
If a seam route splits the safeties, the Tampa runner carries it.
If they attack the flats, the corners squeeze and the hooks wall the inside.
usage_notes: Beats: deep seam and four verticals by closing the middle of the field. Coach's Key: COVERAGE, two-deep with the Mike sinking to make it a three-deep look late.

name: shinobi_cover_2_invert-2

routes.note: Cyan lines from the high defenders dropping toward the flats
Blue lines bailing the corners to the deep-half bubbles
White lines into the inside hook bubbles
Short red arrows off the line

reads: Pre-snap: looks like two-high but the safeties invert down.
If receivers run vertical, the corners take the deep halves.
If they throw to the flats, the inverted safeties trigger downhill.
usage_notes: Beats: smash and flat-based concepts by inverting the leverage. Coach's Key: COVERAGE, the safeties and corners swap responsibilities, it shows two-high then rolls.

name: shinobi_safety_blitz

routes.route: Double safety blitz off the edges
routes.note: Two long red arrows from the wide safeties angling into the backfield
Four straight-down red arrows from the box

reads: Pre-snap: both safeties are creeping toward the line of scrimmage.
If the offense keeps the back in, one safety still arrives free off the edge.
If the QB throws hot, there is no deep help so the rush must finish fast.
usage_notes: Beats: protections that cannot account for both edges. Coach's Key: BLITZ, both safeties come, this is an all-out pressure with no deep safety.

name: shinobi_cover_3_sky_wk

routes.note: Blue/white lines feeding the three deep bubbles

routes.route: Sky down to the weak flat
routes.note: Light line rolling down to the underneath bubble on the weak side
Purple and white lines into the mid hook bubbles
Short red arrows off the line

reads: Pre-snap: three-deep shell with the weak safety rotating down (sky).
If verticals release, the deep thirds carry them.
If they flood the weak flat, the rolled-down safety has it.
usage_notes: Beats: weak-side flood and out-breaking routes. Coach's Key: COVERAGE, three-deep with the weak (Sky) safety as the underneath flat player.

name: shinobi_lb_blitz_3

routes.note: Three blue caps spread across the top, three-deep shell.
Red arrows fire off the line up the middle.
Purple zones drop to the curls and flats on both sides.
Green zone sits over the middle.

reads: IF the offense slides protection away, THEN your blitzing front can free a rusher.
IF they throw quick to a flat, THEN the purple flat defenders are there to drive on it.
IF they push the seams, THEN your three deep caps stay over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: dropbacks with a back held in to block. Coach's Key: pressure underneath a three-deep zone, contain plus coverage.

name: shinobi_cover_3_46_bear

routes.note: Three blue caps across the top, classic Cover 3 deep shell.
Two yellow zones drop underneath over the middle.
Purple zones to the flats on each side.
Four red arrows, standard four-man rush.

reads: IF they release verticals, THEN your three deep defenders match them by thirds.
IF they sit in the curl, THEN the underneath hook zones wall it off.
IF they dump to the flat, THEN the flat defenders rally and tackle.
usage_notes: Beats: deep shots and balanced pass attacks. Coach's Key: four rush, three deep, four underneath, sound zone out of the bear front.

name: shinobi_slant_weak_bear_blitz

routes.note: All front red arrows angle to the weak side, full line slant and pressure.
Multiple red arrows attack the weak-side gaps together.

reads: IF the offense runs weak, THEN your slanting front fills the gaps fast.
IF they pass, THEN the weak-side pressure forces the quarterback to move strong.
IF they have no hot answer weak, THEN a rusher comes free.
usage_notes: Beats: weak-side runs and slow weak-side protection. Coach's Key: everybody slants weak, this is all-out pressure with little zone help shown.

name: shinobi_sam_mike_1

routes.note: red arrows fire from the inside backers and front, including a looping rush
one blue deep zone over the top is the cover 1 free safety

reads: IF the protection misses a blitzer, THEN the Sam or Mike pressure gets home free.
IF the QB beats the rush, THEN it is man coverage underneath with one safety deep.
IF the ball goes deep middle, THEN the single high safety cleans it up.
usage_notes: Beats: slow developing pass concepts. Coach's Key: man-free pressure with the inside backers blitzing, only the deep middle safety and the rush are drawn.

name: shinobi_cover_4_palms-3

routes.note: purple ovals and lines on both edges play match quarters (palms)
olive oval and line over the middle
red arrows at the line show underneath coverage
four blue deep zones across the top

reads: IF the number two receiver goes out, THEN the corner jumps it (palms) and the safety caps number one.
IF number two goes vertical, THEN the corner stays on quarters and matches.
IF it is underneath, THEN the hook and flat droppers squeeze the windows.
usage_notes: Beats: smash and out-breaking concepts. Coach's Key: quarters with a palms (2-read) trap on the outside.

name: shinobi_cover_1_robber_press-3

routes.note: one blue deep zone over the top is the cover 1 free safety
olive oval and line sitting in the middle hole, robbing crossers and digs
four red arrows at the line show man coverage triggers

reads: IF a crosser or dig comes inside, THEN the robber undercuts it.
IF the matchups are man, THEN press and trail the receivers.
IF the ball goes deep middle, THEN the single high safety is over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: crossing routes and inside breakers. Coach's Key: man-free with a robber sitting in the hole, corners press at the line.

name: shinobi_pinch_0

routes.note: six red arrows fire across the entire front, an all-out pinch rush with no deep help

reads: IF the protection cannot block everyone, THEN a free rusher gets home immediately.
IF the QB gets the ball out, THEN it is pure man with zero deep safeties.
IF the offense has a hot answer, THEN the rush has to win first.
usage_notes: Beats: max protect and offenses that hold the ball. Coach's Key: cover 0 all-out pressure, only rush arrows are drawn, no coverage help behind it.

name: shinobi_cover_2_man-6

routes.note: two blue deep zones across the top split the deep field in half
four red arrows at the line show man coverage underneath

reads: IF the receiver runs vertical, THEN the two deep halves cap him.
IF it is an underneath route, THEN the man defenders trail it.
IF the QB scrambles, THEN stay attached in man and let the halves play deep.
usage_notes: Beats: shorter routes with man trail. Coach's Key: two deep zones over man coverage underneath.

name: shinobi_tampa_sim_pressure

routes.note: red arrows fire up from the line squares with one extra red arrow from a defender to the left, a simulated pressure
olive oval over the middle with a green line running up the seam, the Tampa middle hole runner
two grey/white ovals with white lines drop to the middle hook windows
teal ovals and cyan lines to both sidelines cover the flats
two blue deep zones across the top split the deep field in half

reads: IF the simulated pressure is not picked up, THEN a rusher comes free off the front.
IF the seam is attacked, THEN the olive middle runner walls the deep hole.
IF the throw is to the flat, THEN the teal flat defenders jump it.
IF the ball goes over the top, THEN the two deep halves stay capped.
usage_notes: Beats: quick game and deep crossers. Coach's Key: it is a Tampa 2 shell behind a simulated pressure, two deep, a runner up the middle, hooks and flats underneath.

name: shinobi_1_double_wr1_man_coverage-2

routes.note: four red arrows at the line, one angles outside to help double the number one receiver

reads: IF WR1 is the target, THEN two defenders bracket him.
IF the ball goes to another receiver, THEN the remaining defenders carry man.
IF the QB looks off the double, THEN stay disciplined on your man.
usage_notes: Beats: an offense that leans on its top outside receiver. Coach's Key: man coverage with a double on WR1, only the man drops are drawn.

name: shinobi_cover_3_cloud-5

routes.note: teal oval and line to one side, the corner sinks into the flat (cloud)
two olive ovals with white lines drop to the middle hooks
red arrows at the line show underneath coverage
blue deep zones across the top with the safety rotating to cover the deep third over the cloud corner

reads: IF a route attacks the flat on the cloud side, THEN the corner squats and jumps it.
IF it goes vertical outside the cloud, THEN the rotating safety has the deep third.
IF the middle is attacked, THEN the hook defenders wall it.
usage_notes: Beats: smash and flat-based concepts to the cloud side. Coach's Key: cover 3 with the corner cloud (flat) to one side.

name: shinobi_cover_9_match-2

routes.note: four red arrows fire up from the line squares
two olive ovals with green lines drop to the middle hook windows
teal oval and cyan line to the left covers the flat
purple oval to the right with a long purple line running back inside, matches the curl-flat
three blue deep zones across the top split the deep field into thirds

reads: IF the offense pushes verticals, THEN the three deep zones match the thirds.
IF a route works the curl-flat, THEN the purple match defender carries and drives on it.
IF the throw is to the near flat, THEN the teal flat defender squeezes it.
IF the middle is attacked, THEN the olive hook defenders wall it.
usage_notes: Beats: vertical and curl-flat combinations. Coach's Key: it is a MATCH call, three deep with a match curl-flat defender and pattern-read underneath.

name: shinobi_double_bracket_switch-2

routes.note: Four red rush arrows from the line
Defenders aligned for man with bracket help, no routes drawn

reads: IF the bracketed receiver runs vertical, THEN the inside and outside defenders sandwich him.
IF the route breaks in, THEN the inside bracket player takes it.
IF the route breaks out, THEN the outside bracket player takes it.
usage_notes: Art label: MAN. Beats: an offense leaning on one or two top targets. Coach's Key: double the studs, switch the bracket on releases, rush four.

name: shinobi_fs_will_blitz_0-2

routes.note: Six red rush arrows converging, FS and Will added
No deep zone drawn

reads: IF the free safety and Will come clean, THEN it is six on a five-man protection.
IF it is Cover 0, THEN coverage defenders are locked man with no help.
IF the ball comes out hot, THEN man defenders must win at the catch point.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Beats: protections that cannot pick up the safety and backer. Coach's Key: zero coverage, bring the FS and Will, get home now.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_3-2

routes.note: Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Green underneath hook zone, middle
Purple underneath zone
Purple underneath zone
Red rush arrows up front, pressure look

reads: IF the blitz comes, THEN the three-deep, three-under shell stays behind it.
IF a vertical shows, THEN the thirds cap it over the top.
IF the ball comes out quick underneath, THEN the hook and curl defenders rally.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Beats: protections slow to ID the extra rusher. Coach's Key: fire zone look, three deep three under, bring nickel pressure.

name: shinobi_1_double_wr1-2

routes.note: Four red rush arrows from the line
Man alignment, extra help on the number one receiver, no routes drawn

reads: IF WR1 releases, THEN two defenders bracket him over and under.
IF the other receivers win one-on-one, THEN man defenders must hold up.
IF it is a vertical, THEN the deep help caps the rest of the field.
usage_notes: Art label: MAN. Beats: an offense that force-feeds its top wideout. Coach's Key: Cover 1, double WR1, four rush.

name: shinobi_1_double_slot

routes.note: Four red rush arrows from the line
Man alignment, extra help on the slot receiver, no routes drawn

reads: IF the slot releases, THEN two defenders bracket him inside and out.
IF the slot runs an option route, THEN the bracket takes away both breaks.
IF a vertical threatens, THEN the deep help caps the top.
usage_notes: Art label: MAN. Beats: offenses that win from the slot on option and choice routes. Coach's Key: Cover 1, double the slot, rush four.

name: shinobi_overload_3_seam

routes.note: Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Green underneath hook zone, middle
Purple underneath zone
Purple underneath zone
Red rush arrows overloaded from the left

reads: IF the overload comes free, THEN the three-deep shell stays behind it.
IF a seam shot shows, THEN the seam defender carries it to the thirds.
IF it is a hot throw to the flat, THEN the curl/flat defenders drive.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Beats: protections that cannot fan the overloaded side. Coach's Key: overload one side, three deep behind it, wall the seam.

name: shinobi_cover_9_show_2-2

routes.note: Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Cyan underneath flat zone
Green underneath hook zone
Green underneath hook zone
Purple underneath zone
Four red rush arrows from the line

reads: IF the offense shows a two-high beater, THEN the disguise rotates to the deep coverage shown.
IF a vertical works a seam, THEN the deep defenders match it.
IF the underneath fills up, THEN the hook and flat defenders pass it off.
usage_notes: Art label: MATCH. Beats: route concepts keyed off the safety shell. Coach's Key: show two, play the quarters-match coverage, four rush.

name: shinobi_cover_3_sky-9

routes.note: Blue deep third
Blue deep middle third
Blue deep third
Green underneath hook zone
Green underneath hook zone
Purple underneath flat zone
Purple underneath flat zone
Four red rush arrows from the line

reads: IF a vertical shows, THEN the three deep defenders cap their thirds.
IF it is a flat or quick out, THEN the strong safety rolls down to the flat (sky).
IF a curl sits, THEN the hook defenders wall and squeeze it.
usage_notes: Art label: ZONE. Beats: vertical concepts and deep shots. Coach's Key: three deep, four under, safety to the flat, four rush.

name: shinobi_fire_zone_blitz

routes.note: Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Blue deep zone
Green underneath hook zone
Green underneath hook zone
Purple underneath flat zone
Purple underneath flat zone
Red rush arrows from the left, four-rush simulated pressure

reads: IF the sim pressure comes, THEN a coverage defender replaces the rusher and it stays four-man.
IF a vertical shows, THEN the three-deep shell caps it.
IF the ball comes out hot, THEN the hooks and flats rally underneath.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Art title reads Field Sim 3 (JSON display_name was Fire Zone Blitz); authored from the art. Beats: protections fooled by the simulated edge pressure. Coach's Key: simulate the blitz, drop into three-deep, stay sound underneath.

name: shinobi_show_ss_sim_2

routes.note: Blue deep half
Blue deep half
Cyan underneath flat zone
Cyan underneath flat zone
Grey/white underneath hook zone
Green underneath hook zone, middle
Red rush arrows from the right edge

reads: IF the strong safety shows blitz, THEN a defender replaces him and it stays four-rush.
IF a vertical threatens, THEN the two deep halves cap it.
IF the quick game shows, THEN the flats and hooks squeeze.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Beats: protections that bite on the safety show. Coach's Key: show the SS, simulate the pressure, drop into two-deep zone.

name: shinobi_buck_zone_blitz

routes.note: Five red arrows attacking across the LOS
Purple zone with purple line crossing down toward the left, added pressure
Purple zone with purple line crossing down toward the right, added pressure
Olive zone with green line dropping to the short middle hook
Blue zone, drops into deep coverage
Blue zone, deep middle area
Blue zone, drops into deep coverage

reads: IF the protection slides one way, THEN the backside purple blitzer comes free off the edge.
IF the quarterback gets the ball out hot, THEN the deep zone droppers rally and tackle short.
IF nobody picks up the pressure, THEN get home, this is built to bring more than they can block.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-developing dropbacks and soft protections. Coach's Key: five-plus rush with edge zone blitzers and three-deep behind it, so the soft spot is the quick game and the underneath flats.

name: shinobi_nickel_sim_2

routes.note: Red arrow attacking down toward the LOS from the left side, this is the simulated pressure
Red arrow driving into the line, part of the left-side rush package
Red arrow at the LOS, fills the gap as the sim pressure comes
White line peeling off the mug back into the underneath middle, bails instead of rushing
Green line bailing into the green middle zone bubble, robs the seam
White line dropping back to the right underneath, peels out of the mug look
Cyan line working out to the right flat, covers the teal underneath bubble
Teal underneath bubble on the left, sits on the short outside
Blue bubble capping the top left, plays over the top
Blue bubble capping the top right, plays over the top

reads: IF the back releases or the offense slides protection, THEN the left-side red rush comes free as the simulated pressure.
IF the mug shows but only some rush, THEN the dropping defenders sink into the green middle hole and the underneath hooks.
IF a vertical pushes downfield, THEN the deep blue zones cap it over the top.
IF the ball comes out quick, THEN the teal flat defenders rally up and tackle short.
usage_notes: Art label: BLITZ. Beats: protections that bite on the mug look and leave the left-side rush unblocked. Coach's Key: show pressure from the double mug, send the left side, drop the rest into a middle-hole and two-deep zone shell.

name: shinobi_cover_2_man-7

routes.note: Blue line up to the wide deep-left zone.
Blue line up to the wide deep-right zone. Two-high shell.
White X marks across the underneath. Each plays man on his assigned receiver beneath the two safeties.
Red arrows from the down linemen at the LOS rushing the QB.

reads: IF your man releases vertical, THEN trail him with inside leverage and let the deep safety cap the route over the top.
IF your man runs an underneath route, THEN stay glued in man, the two safeties have the deep halves.
IF you get a pick or rub from the offense, THEN switch or fight over the top to keep the deep-half help working.
usage_notes: Beats: short and intermediate man-beaters when the rush gets home. Coach's Key: MAN under, two-deep over. Vulnerable to deep crossers and the hole between the two safeties.

name: shinobi_cover_9_show_2-3

routes.note: Three blue deep zones playing match
Teal zones outside
Olive and purple underneath zones
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF a vertical releases THEN the match defender carries it deep.
IF a route breaks to the flat THEN the flat defender drives on it.
IF an inside route shows THEN the curl/hook defender sinks and reroutes.
usage_notes: Beats: vertical and out concepts under a three-deep match shell. Coach's Key: shows two-high but rotates to a three-deep match.

name: shinobi_double_safety_blitz

routes.note: Red arrows curling down from both edge X marks
Red arrows from the interior linemen
White X marks and two white dots in man

reads: IF the offense cannot block both safeties THEN one runs free off the edge.
IF the QB throws hot THEN the man defenders have to make the tackle.
IF a back checks the blitz THEN the second safety still gets home.
usage_notes: Beats: empty and quick-drop looks that leave the edges light. Coach's Key: bring both safeties, win with overload pressure.

name: shinobi_silver_shoot_pinch

routes.note: Red arrows converging inside through the gaps
X with a red arrow down off the left edge
White X marks and two white dots in man

reads: IF the line slides one way THEN the pinched interior rushers attack the soft gap.
IF the edge blitzer comes clean THEN he is the free runner.
IF the ball comes out fast THEN the man defenders must squeeze the throw.
usage_notes: Beats: interior protection that cannot handle gap exchange. Coach's Key: pinch the inside gaps and add an edge runner.

name: shinobi_edge_blitz_3-2

routes.note: Three blue deep zones
Purple zones on the edges
Olive hook zone in the middle
Crossing red arrows at the line

reads: IF the tackles cannot handle the crossing rush THEN an edge runner comes free.
IF the QB throws hot THEN the three-deep shell keeps it in front.
IF the protection holds THEN a dropper sinks to the hook.
usage_notes: Beats: a slow dropback behind a three-deep shell. Coach's Key: cross the edge rushers to confuse the protection.

name: shinobi_cover_6_willie-3

routes.note: Blue deep zones, two quarters and a half
Purple zones on the left
Olive hook zone in the middle
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF the quarters side goes vertical THEN match it like man.
IF the half side runs a smash THEN the deep-half defender caps the corner.
IF an underneath route shows THEN the curl/flat defender drives on it.
usage_notes: Beats: formation-into-boundary flood and smash. Coach's Key: quarters to the field, half to the boundary.

name: shinobi_over_storm_brave

routes.note: White square on the LOS, red arrow straight downhill
White square inside on the LOS, red arrow straight downhill
White square inside on the LOS, red arrow straight downhill
White square on the LOS, red arrow straight downhill
Off-ball circle, red arrow curling down through the A/B gap to the LOS
Off-ball circle, red arrow curling down through the A/B gap to the LOS
White X at the second level, no rush arrow drawn
White X at the second level, no rush arrow drawn
White X deep on the left, no rush arrow drawn
White X deep on the right, no rush arrow drawn

reads: Pre-snap: confirm the four down linemen are pointed downhill and both off-ball backers are tagged to blitz, that is six in the rush.
At the snap: send the front and let the two storm backers fill the inside gaps off the linemen.
If the line picks it up, the inside pressure from the backers should arrive clean, get home.
Behind it: the drop defenders and the two deep X marks have to carry everyone, so the rush has to win fast.
usage_notes: Beats: a quarterback who sits in the pocket. Coach's Key: heavy six-man storm out of the over front, the off-ball backers are the difference makers so time them up.

name: shinobi_1_double_wr2_man_coverage-2

routes.note: White X marks on receivers
Two white dots set to double the number two receiver
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF the number two receiver releases THEN bracket him with both defenders.
IF a single-covered route wins THEN the four-man rush has to close.
IF the offense motions WR2 THEN carry the bracket across.
usage_notes: Beats: an offense feeding the slot or number two receiver. Coach's Key: take away WR2 and make the rest beat you.

name: shinobi_cover_3_buzz_match-3

routes.note: Three blue deep zones playing match
Olive hook zones, a defender buzzing down to the middle
Purple zones on the edges
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF a seam route shows THEN the buzz defender walls it off in the hook.
IF two go vertical to a side THEN the match rules carry them.
IF the route stays short THEN sink and reroute the next threat.
usage_notes: Beats: seam and dig concepts under three-deep. Coach's Key: a safety buzzes down to the hook to wall the seam.

name: shinobi_sim_pressure_3-2

routes.note: Three blue deep zones
Purple zones on the edges
Two olive hook zones
Crossing and pinched red rush arrows, one dropper

reads: IF the protection turns the wrong way THEN the simulated pressure springs a free rusher.
IF a dropper bails THEN he fills the vacated hook window.
IF the QB throws hot THEN the three-deep shell keeps it in front.
usage_notes: Beats: a QB who holds the ball under a three-deep shell. Coach's Key: rush four out of a different spot to fool the protection.

name: shinobi_nickel_sim_2-2

routes.note: Two blue deep zones
Olive zone dropping over the middle
Teal zones outside
Gray zones underneath
Red rush arrows showing simulated pressure

reads: IF the protection slides away THEN the simulated rusher comes free.
IF a crosser works the middle THEN the robber jumps it.
IF the ball goes outside THEN the flat defender drives on it.
usage_notes: Beats: middle reads under a two-high pressure look. Coach's Key: two-high with a robber and a five-man simulated rush.

name: shinobi_cover_2_roll-3

routes.note: Two blue deep zones rolling coverage
Olive zone over the middle
Teal zones outside
Gray and olive zones underneath
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF the coverage rolls to a half THEN the deep defender caps that side.
IF a vertical shows THEN the deep-half defender takes it over the top.
IF an out or flat route releases THEN the flat defender drives on it.
usage_notes: Beats: a side-loaded passing concept. Coach's Key: roll the two-deep shell to the strength.

name: shinobi_cover_1_hole-8

routes.note: Single blue deep zone up top
Olive zone sitting over the middle short
White X marks on receivers, two dots underneath
Four red rush arrows

reads: IF a crosser or shallow works the middle THEN the hole defender robs it.
IF everyone is man-locked THEN the free safety stays over the top.
IF the back leaks out THEN pass him to the nearest man defender.
usage_notes: Beats: shallow crossers and drag routes. Coach's Key: single-high man with a hole player to rob the middle.

name: shinobi_cross_sim_2-2

routes.note: Two blue deep zones
Olive zone over the middle
Teal zones outside
Gray zones underneath, white droppers
Crossing red rush arrows in the interior

reads: IF the interior protection cannot pass off the cross THEN a rusher comes free.
IF a dropper bails THEN he sinks to the hook or flat.
IF the QB throws hot THEN the two-deep shell keeps it bracketed.
usage_notes: Beats: man protection that struggles with a twist. Coach's Key: cross the interior rush behind a two-deep shell.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_0-2

routes.note: Five red arrows all fire downhill from the mugged box with no deep help drawn, a true zero pressure
Remaining defenders shown only as pre-snap alignment dots/X marks with no zone drops, everyone left in coverage is man with no safety help

reads: IF you are matched on a receiver, THEN lock him man-to-man with no help anywhere
IF the back releases, THEN a free rusher is already home
IF the QB holds the ball, THEN the all-out pressure gets there
usage_notes: Beats: predictable max-protect dropbacks. Coach's Key: Cover 0 all-out pressure from the mug, no deep safety, win the matchup or the rush wins for you.

name: shinobi_cover_3_match-7

routes.note: Four red arrows rush from the line
Two yellow lines drop and match into the middle hook landmarks (the two tan bubbles)
Two purple/magenta lines drop to the outside underneath zones
Three blue lines bail to deep landmarks, pattern-matching the vertical releases

reads: IF the receiver runs vertical, THEN match and carry him in your deep third
IF he breaks off short, THEN pass him to the underneath defender and look for the next threat
IF nobody threatens deep, THEN rob the dig or crosser underneath
usage_notes: Beats: vertical and four-verts-style concepts. Coach's Key: Cover 3 that pattern-matches the routes instead of spot-dropping, stay square and read releases.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_2

routes.note: Red arrows fire from the interior mug, four-plus rushers crash the line
Two yellow lines drop to the intermediate underneath landmarks (the tan bubbles)
Cyan lines drop to the short flat zones outside (the teal bubbles)
Two blue lines bail to deep-half landmarks, a two-deep shell behind the pressure

reads: IF the protection holds, THEN the mug pressure collapses the pocket
IF the QB throws the flat, THEN the flat defender drives on it
IF the route goes deep, THEN the two-deep safeties cap the halves
usage_notes: Beats: quick game when the rush gets home. Coach's Key: blitz from the mug behind a two-deep zone shell, take away the middle and force outside.

name: shinobi_cover_6_match-2

routes.note: Four red arrows rush from the line
Purple/magenta line drops to the short flat on the Cover 2 (cloud) side
Two yellow lines drop and match into the middle hook landmarks (the tan bubbles)
Blue lines split the back end: two-deep look to one side (halves/cloud) and a quarters look to the other, the Cover 6 combination

reads: IF the receiver attacks the quarters side deep, THEN match him vertically in your quarter
IF the route hits the flat on the cloud side, THEN the squat defender jumps it
IF it crosses the middle, THEN the match hook dropper carries it across
usage_notes: Beats: field/boundary route combos by playing two coverages at once. Coach's Key: Cover 6, quarters to one side and Cover 2 to the other, set the strength to take away their best concept. Art label reads MATCH/Cover 6; card name says Cover 6 Match.

name: shinobi_tampa_2-13

routes.note: Four red arrows rush from the line
The green/olive line carries straight up the middle to the deep hole, the signature Tampa 2 middle runner (center olive bubble)
White lines drop to the underneath hook and flat landmarks (the gray bubbles and teal flats)
Two blue lines bail to the deep-half landmarks

reads: IF a vertical splits the safeties, THEN the middle runner carries it up the pipe
IF the QB works the flat, THEN the underneath flat defender drives
IF the route stays intermediate, THEN the hook dropper walls it off
IF nothing threatens deep, THEN the safeties stay over the top of the halves
usage_notes: Beats: deep middle seams and four verts by closing the Cover 2 hole. Coach's Key: Tampa 2, the middle hook runner sprints to the deep hole, two-deep over the top. Note: art front/drops read Tampa 2 even though label box shows BLITZ.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_3-3

routes.route: three deep blue zones across the top (3-deep behind the pressure)
routes.note: blue ovals spanning deep

routes.route: two purple underneath zones outside plus a yellow middle drop
routes.note: purple ovals both sides, green/yellow oval center with a drop line

routes.route: rush (cluster of red arrows from the front, one purple blitz path off the edge)
routes.note: fanned red arrows at the LOS, purple angled lines from outside defenders

reads: IF you bring the box pressure, THEN three deep and a middle dropper play behind it.
IF a hot route shows, THEN the underneath zone defender squeezes it.
IF protection slides, THEN the looping rusher hits the open gap.
usage_notes: Beats: long-developing dropbacks. Coach's Key: fire-zone style pressure with three deep and matched underneath drops.

name: shinobi_hot_blitz_3-4

routes.route: three deep blue zones across the top
routes.note: blue ovals spanning deep

routes.route: two yellow hook/curl drops over the middle
routes.note: two beige ovals with yellow lines

routes.route: rush (cluster of red arrows from the front)
routes.note: fanned red arrows at the LOS, one from the wide defender

reads: IF the pressure forces a quick throw, THEN the three-deep shell takes away the shot.
IF the back leaks out, THEN the hook dropper passes it off.
IF the QB hangs in, THEN the rush gets home.
usage_notes: Beats: hot answers and quick game. Coach's Key: bring heat but stay sound with three deep behind it.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_1-2

routes.route: single deep blue zone over the top (Cover 1 post-safety)
routes.note: one blue oval high middle with a blue line back to a defender

routes.route: rush (five red arrows fanned from the LOS)
routes.note: five red arrows from the mug front

routes.route: white X defenders in man with no drawn drops
routes.note: scattered white X's, no underneath ovals

reads: IF one deep safety is up, THEN play man underneath.
IF a back stays to protect, THEN a rusher comes free.
IF the QB cannot beat the rush, THEN man-free wins.
usage_notes: Beats: slow dropbacks. Coach's Key: man-free with five rushers, win with pressure.

name: shinobi_lb_blitz_0

routes.route: rush (five red arrows from the front, including an inside LB)
routes.note: fanned red arrows at the LOS, one from a stacked defender

routes.route: white X defenders in man, no deep zone drawn (Cover 0)
routes.note: no blue or zone ovals

reads: IF no deep safety is shown, THEN this is zero coverage with no help.
IF the line slides away, THEN the free LB hits home.
IF the QB holds it, THEN the zero blitz finishes.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses slow to ID the blitzing LB. Coach's Key: Cover 0 LB pressure, get there or give up the explosive.

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_2-2

routes.route: two deep blue zones split the field (2-deep behind the pressure)
routes.note: two blue ovals high

routes.route: teal flat zones outside and beige hook ovals inside, with a cyan motion/blitz path
routes.note: teal ovals at the numbers, beige ovals inside, cyan line from one defender

routes.route: rush (cluster of red arrows from the front, one yellow drop line)
routes.note: fanned red arrows at the LOS, a yellow line showing one dropper

reads: IF the pressure comes, THEN two deep and underneath zones play behind it.
IF a flat route shows, THEN the outside zone squeezes it.
IF the back protects, THEN the cyan-path rusher comes off the edge.
usage_notes: Beats: medium dropback concepts. Coach's Key: two-deep zone pressure with a motioned blitzer off the edge.

name: shinobi_blitz_loop_sim_3

routes.note: Three blue zones cap the field in thirds behind the pressure
Purple zones sink to the curl and widen to the flat on each side
Yellow lines: the mug defenders bail to the tan middle hooks rather than rush
Red lines loop and cross at the line of scrimmage, the interior rushers twist to win a new gap

reads: If the protection sets for the mug look, then the front loops (red cross) to free a rusher off the twist.
If the ball comes out quick over the middle, then the two mug hooks in the tan zones wall it.
If a vertical or out develops, then the three-deep thirds and the purple curl/flat defenders cover behind the loop.
usage_notes: Beats: slow dropbacks against a looping rush. Coach's Key: sim pressure, the front twists to beat protection while a three-deep zone sits behind it.

name: shinobi_tampa_2-14

routes.note: Blue deep zone covering the left half of the field
Blue deep zone covering the right half of the field
Green zone up the middle, drops to wall off the deep seam between the two halves
Cyan zone in the left corner / flat
Cyan zone in the right corner / flat
Four red arrows at the line of scrimmage showing the front rushing the passer

reads: IF the offense floods one deep half, THEN the safety to that side stays on top and the Mike walls the seam.
IF a vertical hits the deep middle, THEN the Mike carries it so the safeties can stay wide.
IF the throw goes underneath to the flat, THEN the corners squeeze and rally up to make the tackle.
IF the front cannot get pressure, THEN passing windows stay open longer, so the rush has to win one-on-one.
usage_notes: Beats: deep crossers and four-verticals when the Mike carries the middle. Coach's Key: classic Tampa 2, two deep halves with the Mike sinking to take the deep hole. Soft underneath in the flats, give up the short stuff and rally, do not let anything over the top.

name: shinobi_cover_1_lb_blitz-5

routes.note: Six red arrows, all the box defenders fire straight ahead in an all-out pressure
Blue line runs back to a single deep zone bubble in the middle of the field

reads: IF the offense keeps in extra blockers, THEN find the one-on-one with no help.
IF the QB has to get rid of it fast, THEN the six-man rush is the reason.
IF the ball goes deep middle, THEN the single high defender is the only one back there.
usage_notes: Beats: max-protect sets that leave a single matchup. Coach's Key: pure Cover 1 pressure, everyone in the box rushes with one defender capping the deep middle.

name: shinobi_1dt_lb_loop

routes.note: Red arrow rushes straight off the left edge
Two crossing red arrows in the middle: the DT and looping LB swap gaps on a twist
Red arrow rushes straight off the right edge
Blue line runs back to a single deep zone bubble in the middle

reads: IF the line tries to pass off the twist, THEN the crossing rushers should beat the exchange.
IF the protection holds, THEN the edges still have one-on-one rushes.
IF the ball goes deep middle, THEN the single high defender is the only help.
usage_notes: Beats: protections that struggle to pass off line games. Coach's Key: the DT and LB run a loop / twist inside to free an interior rusher, single high behind it.

name: shinobi_cover_1_contain

routes.note: Single blue deep zone with a blue drop line, single high.
Brown spy zone over a linebacker (white dot) in the middle.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.
MAN label; man across behind the spy.

reads: If the QB tries to scramble, the spy is there to contain him.
If they go deep, the single-high free safety caps it.
If everyone is covered in man, the spy mirrors the quarterback.
usage_notes: Beats: scrambling QBs and draws. Coach's Key: single-high man with a contain spy on the quarterback.

name: shinobi_nickel_2_trap_blitz

routes.note: White square at the line with a red arrow straight downfield into the backfield.
White square at the line with a red arrow straight ahead, attacking the gap.
White square at the line with a red arrow straight ahead into the pocket.
White square at the line with a red arrow straight ahead. Four red arrows total form the four-man rush.
Blue line dropping back to the large blue ellipse over the left deep half.
Blue line dropping back to the large blue ellipse over the right deep half. Two blue deep ellipses give a two-deep shell.
Cyan line off the left edge dropping into the teal underneath zone ellipse (left flat/hook area).
Cyan line dropping into the teal underneath zone ellipse on the right (right flat/hook area).
Yellow line running to a landmark dot inside the olive/yellow zone ellipse in the left-middle (curl/hook trap zone).
Yellow line running to a landmark dot inside the olive/yellow zone ellipse in the right-middle (curl/hook trap zone).

reads: Pre-snap: confirm the two-deep blue shell. If both safeties stay over the top, the deep halves are covered and you are taking away the deep ball.
At the snap: send the four red rushers straight ahead to pressure the pocket fast.
If the QB looks to a flat or curl, the underneath teal and yellow droppers trap and squeeze that throwing window.
If protection holds, keep the rush lanes tight so the QB cannot step up between the four rushers.
usage_notes: Beats: quick-game and flat throws against a two-deep shell. Coach's Key: four straight rushers with two deep halves and underneath droppers trapping the short zones.

name: shinobi_sim_pressure_3-3

routes.note: Three blue deep zones drawn across the top.
Magenta match zones to each side with magenta lines.
Gray hook zones in the middle with white drop lines.
Red stunting rush lines at the line of scrimmage with crossing rushers.

reads: If protection misses the twist, a rusher comes clean up the middle.
If they throw hot, the three-deep zone is already set behind it.
If they max protect, the sim still rushes a contained four or five.
usage_notes: Beats: half-slide protection and quick game. Coach's Key: simulate pressure with a stunt, fall into Cover 3 behind it.

name: shinobi_bracket_switch_willie

routes.note: Four white boxes on the line, each with a red downhill arrow rushing the QB.
Two white dots behind the front, no rush arrow drawn.
Blue motion-style line runs from an X near the top numbers up into a large blue zone bubble over the deep right, showing a defender bracketing into deep help.
Remaining X marks in man across the field, label reads MAN.

reads: Pre-snap: see the blue bubble up top, one defender is bracketing the receiver in that area with help over the top.
The rest of the front is a four-man rush shown by the red arrows.
If the bracketed receiver runs vertical, the deep blue help caps him while the under defender squeezes.
Everyone else is man, so they travel with their guy while the bracket doubles the key matchup.
usage_notes: Beats: a single dangerous deep target. Coach's Key: four rush, man everywhere except the bracketed receiver who gets doubled.

name: shinobi_cover_3_buzz_mable-3

routes.note: Three blue deep zones with blue drop lines.
Magenta match zones on the perimeter with magenta lines.
Gray hook zones in the middle with white lines, a safety dropping down to the hook.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.

reads: If the safety buzzes the hook, the middle window is closed.
If they go vertical, the three deep defenders stay over the top.
If they flood a curl, the match defender carries it.
usage_notes: Beats: hook and dig routes. Coach's Key: Cover 3 with a safety buzzing into the middle hook.

name: shinobi_edge_blitz_0-4

routes.note: Two outside defenders (X marks) with red arrows rushing off the edges.
Four interior defenders with red arrows converging on the QB.
One off-ball defender (white dot) holding in the middle.
No deep zones drawn; Cover 0 pressure look.

reads: If you bring the edges with no deep help, you must get home fast.
If they have a hot route, it will be open, so the rush has to win.
If the QB escapes, the lone middle defender has to contain.
usage_notes: Beats: empty and slow drops. Coach's Key: all-out Cover 0 edge pressure, win one-on-one or pay for it.

name: shinobi_field_sim_3_blitz

routes.note: Three blue deep zones across the top.
Magenta match zones to each side with magenta lines.
Gray hook zones in the middle with white drop lines.
Red stunting rush lines and arrows attacking the field side.

reads: If the protection cannot pick up the field-side twist, a rusher comes free.
If they throw hot away from it, the Cover 3 zone is set behind.
If they slide to the field, the boundary may open up late.
usage_notes: Beats: field-side quick game. Coach's Key: simulate pressure to the field, drop into Cover 3 behind it.

name: shinobi_cover_6_willie-4

routes.note: Blue deep half zone to the boundary with a blue drop line.
Two blue deep quarter zones to the field with blue drop lines.
Magenta match zone to the field side with a magenta line.
Green hook zone in the middle with a green line.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.

reads: If they attack the boundary, the half safety has it capped.
If they go vertical to the field, you are in quarters there.
If they sit in the hook, the linebacker is waiting.
usage_notes: Beats: half-field reads and field verticals. Coach's Key: quarter-quarter-half, quarters to the field, half to the boundary.

name: shinobi_1_double_slot_man_coverage

routes.note: Four white boxes on the line, each with a red downhill arrow attacking forward.
Two white dots behind the front, no rush arrow, in man assignments.
X marks spread across the field in man-to-man, label reads MAN.

reads: Pre-snap: four red arrows tell you it is a four-man rush from the Nickel 2-4.
It is a man call, so the back-end X defenders lock onto their receivers.
If the slot shows movement, the matched man defender stays glued through the release.
If coverage holds, the four rushers have to win and get home.
usage_notes: Beats: routes that need separation against sticky man. Coach's Key: four rush, man behind, no help drawn.

name: shinobi_cover_6_nickel_2_4

routes.note: Two blue deep quarter zones to the field with blue drop lines.
Blue deep half zone to the boundary.
Magenta match zone to the field with a magenta line.
Olive match zones across the middle with yellow lines.
Teal flat zone to the boundary with a cyan line.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.

reads: If they go vertical to the field, you are in quarters there.
If they work the boundary, the half safety has the top and the flat is covered.
If it stays underneath, the curl/hook defenders settle on it.
usage_notes: Beats: split-field reads. Coach's Key: quarter-quarter-half with a boundary flat defender.

name: shinobi_cover_2_roll-4

routes.note: Two blue deep zones with blue drop lines.
Teal flat zones on each side with cyan lines, corners sinking to the flats.
Olive and gray hook zones in the middle with white and green lines.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.

reads: If the coverage rolls to a two-shell, expect deep halves with hard corners.
If they go vertical, the safeties split the field deep.
If they hit the flat, the corner is sitting underneath it.
usage_notes: Beats: flat and curl routes. Coach's Key: roll to a two-deep shell, corners squat in the flats.

name: shinobi_cover_2_man-8

routes.note: Two blue deep zones with blue drop lines.
Four down defenders with red arrows rushing.
MAN label; man across underneath the two-deep shell.

reads: If they go deep, the two safeties cap each half.
If receivers break underneath, the man defenders trail them.
If the rush is slow, two-deep help limits the big play.
usage_notes: Beats: vertical man-beaters. Coach's Key: two-deep over man, trail technique with safety help on top.

name: shinobi_overload_3_seam-2

routes.note: Three blue deep zones across the top.
Magenta match zones to each side with magenta lines; olive seam zone in the middle with a yellow line.
Multiple red rush lines and arrows overloading one side of the line.

reads: If you overload one side, a rusher should come free against the protection.
If they throw hot, the three-deep seam zones are behind it.
If they slide to the overload, the backside may open late.
usage_notes: Beats: protections that cannot fan the overload. Coach's Key: overload pressure to one side, Cover 3 seam behind.

name: shinobi_dt_mike_loop_3

routes.note: Blue zone, left third
Blue zone, middle third
Blue zone, right third
Purple zone left
Purple zone right
Green zone underneath over the middle
Two red arrows pressing straight down off the edges
Red crossing arrows inside, the tackle and Mike exchange gaps

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN the inside tackle and the Mike loop and cross to confuse the protection.
IF the guard sets to the first rusher, THEN the looper comes clean behind the pick.
IF the offense maxes protection, THEN three deep and the underneath defenders keep you sound.
IF the QB steps up, THEN the looping rusher meets him in the pocket.
usage_notes: Beats: man-protection schemes that pass off straight rushers but get crossed up by a loop. Coach's Key: badge reads BLITZ. Behind it is a Cover 3 shell (three blue deep, purple curl drops).

name: shinobi_nickel_blitz_3-4

routes.note: Blue zone, left third
Blue zone, middle third
Blue zone, right third
Purple zone left
Purple zone right
Green zone underneath the middle
Red arrow added off the left edge
Red arrows off the down linemen

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN the nickel adds off the edge for an extra rusher.
IF the back releases instead of blocking, THEN the hook/spy defender picks him up underneath.
IF the offense slides away from the pressure, THEN the blitz comes free on the back side.
IF the rush stalls, THEN three deep keeps the top of the coverage capped.
usage_notes: Beats: protections that can't fan out to the nickel edge pressure. Coach's Key: badge reads BLITZ. Cover 3 behind it (three blue deep, purple curls, one green hook).

name: shinobi_nickel_2_trap-2

routes.note: Blue zone, left half
Blue zone, right half
Cyan flat zone, jumps the underneath out
Cyan flat zone, jumps the underneath out
Cream/olive zone over the left middle
Cream/olive zone over the right middle
Four red arrows off the line of scrimmage

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN two safeties split the deep halves and the corners trap the flat.
IF a receiver runs a quick out or hitch, THEN the trapping corner jumps it underneath.
IF the offense throws deep outside, THEN the safety over the top is there because the corner sank the flat.
IF routes flood the hook area, THEN the underneath droppers wall the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: quick-game smoke screens and flat throws that the corner can trap and jump. Coach's Key: badge reads BLITZ. Cover 2 shell with a flat trap technique.

name: shinobi_blitz_tex_3_sim_3

routes.note: Blue zone, left third
Blue zone, middle third
Blue zone, right third
White zone, a faked rusher dropping to the middle hook
White zone, a faked rusher dropping to the middle hook
Purple zone left
Purple zone right
Two red arrows angling inward and crossing

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN two inside rushers angle in on a Tex stunt while two would-be rushers bail to underneath hooks.
IF the line over-sets to the simulated pressure, THEN the actual rushers cross inside for the free lane.
IF the offense reads the bluff, THEN you still have three deep and underneath droppers to stay sound.
IF the QB throws hot, THEN the dropping defenders are sitting in those windows.
usage_notes: Beats: protections that key the wrong rusher on a simulated pressure. Coach's Key: badge reads BLITZ. Sim pressure means total rushers stay around four behind a Cover 3 match shell.

name: shinobi_nickel_sim_2_blitz

routes.note: white square on the LOS, red arrow straight downhill into the backfield
white square on the LOS, red arrow straight ahead
white square on the LOS, red arrow straight ahead

routes.route: simulated pressure off the left edge
routes.note: white X off the line with a red arrow angling down across the LOS, the fourth rusher
white-to-green line dropping into the olive middle hook zone
white X on a blue line bailing into the left deep blue zone
white X on a blue line bailing into the right deep blue zone
white X sitting in the left teal flat zone
white X dropping out to the right teal flat zone on the cyan line

reads: IF the offense holds protection, THEN the four rushers keep heat on while the shell stays clean.
IF the QB attacks deep, THEN the two safeties split the field in half and rob anything vertical.
IF the ball comes out hot, THEN the flat defenders in the teal zones drive and make the tackle short.
IF you need more coverage, THEN bail the sim blitzer and play a true 2-deep, 6-under out of Dime.
usage_notes: Beats: dink-and-dunk and deep shots in obvious passing downs. Coach's Key: same sim-pressure as the fire zone but from Dime 2-3-6 personnel, 4 rush behind a 2-deep zone with extra DBs underneath.

name: shinobi_cover_9_show_2-4

routes.note: Three blue deep zone bubbles across the top with lines back to the deep defenders
Teal underneath zone bubble on the left with a short match line
Two olive-yellow underneath bubbles over the middle, one with a vertical match line
Purple underneath bubble on the right with a long match line out toward the flat
Four white squares with red down-arrows releasing underneath

reads: If a vertical releases, the three deep defenders match and carry their thirds.
If a route works the underneath, the match droppers squeeze the curl and seam windows.
If the ball goes to the flat, the purple match defender drives out on it since the badge reads MATCH.
usage_notes: Beats: vertical and curl-flat concepts in passing downs. Coach's Key: badge reads MATCH, shows a two-high look and plays three-deep match with the underneath defenders matching out.

name: shinobi_edge_blitz_3-3

routes.note: Red arrows converging at the line into the middle
Green/yellow bubble dropping over the middle
Purple zone bubbles on each side with match lines
Three blue deep zone bubbles across the top

reads: If the edge pressure gets home, finish the rush.
If the ball comes out hot, the underneath curl/flat defenders squeeze it.
If a route runs vertical, the three deep zones cap their thirds.
usage_notes: Beats: slow-developing edge protections. Coach's Key: edge pressure into a three-deep, three-under shell.

name: shinobi_dbl_safety_blitz-2

routes.note: Two long red arrows curling down from the safety depth on each side into the backfield
Two red down-arrows from the interior linemen
Two short red arrows pinching toward the middle

reads: If the offense slides protection inside, the two safety blitzers come free off the edges.
If a hot route shows, get the ball out fast because coverage is thin behind the pressure.
If the back stays in to block, the extra rushers still flood one side.
usage_notes: Beats: empty or five-man protections. Coach's Key: bring both safeties on the edge, win before the throw.

name: shinobi_field_sim_3_blitz-2

routes.note: Red arrows angling in at the line
Two yellow short drops over the middle
Purple zone bubbles on each side with match lines
Three blue deep zone bubbles across the top

reads: If the sim pressure draws the protection, the unblocked rusher gets through.
If the ball comes out quick, the two hook droppers wall the middle.
If a vertical releases, the three deep zones cap their thirds.
usage_notes: Coach's Key: field-side simulated pressure into a three-deep zone behind it.

name: shinobi_over_storm_brave_blitz-2

routes.note: Square on the line, red arrow straight downhill.
Circle with square below, red arrow drives down and angles inside.
Square with red arrow that loops and crosses inward, part of an interior twist with the neighboring rusher.
Square with red arrow that crosses back the other way, completing the twist with rusher A.
Square with an X marker above it, red arrow straight downhill on the edge.

reads: Pre-snap: count the five red arrows, this is a five-man pressure out of the Dime 2-3-6.
If the interior twist hits clean, the looping rusher comes free through the gap the line vacated.
If the protection picks up the twist, the edge rusher with the straight downhill arrow should win one-on-one.
usage_notes: Beats: empty and spread dropback sets that leave the interior light. Coach's Key: the middle twist is the engine, the crossing arrows free a rusher up the middle.

name: shinobi_edge_blitz_0-5

routes.note: Five red arrows all converging toward the QB from the line
No deep help drawn; Blitz 0 means man across with no safety

reads: If the protection cannot account for all five, the edge rusher comes free.
If a hot route shows, the man defenders must squeeze it with no deep help.
If the QB hangs in the pocket, the converging rush finishes.
usage_notes: Beats: deep drop-back concepts. Coach's Key: five-man edge pressure with zero coverage, get home fast.

name: shinobi_cover_6_willie-5

routes.note: Three blue deep zone bubbles across the top, the Cover 6 split-field shell
Purple bubble with a match line to one side
Green/yellow bubble with a vertical line on the other side
Four red down-arrows releasing underneath

reads: If a vertical hits the quarters side, the deep defenders match it like Cover 4.
If the ball goes to the Cover 2 half, the dropper carries the seam and the corner sits the flat.
If the offense floods one side, lean on the split-field rules for that half.
usage_notes: Beats: formation-into-boundary concepts. Coach's Key: Cover 6 split-field, quarters to the field and Cover 2 to the boundary.

name: shinobi_cover_3_buzz_mable-4

routes.note: Three blue deep zone bubbles across the top
Yellow line from a deeper defender dropping down into the middle hook
Purple zone bubbles on each side with match lines
Four red down-arrows releasing underneath

reads: If a vertical shows, the three deep defenders carry their thirds.
If a route works the middle hook, the buzz defender fills it from depth.
If the flat shows, the outside curl/flat defender drives down.
usage_notes: Beats: crossers and middle hooks by buzzing a safety down. Coach's Key: three-deep with a buzz defender filling the middle underneath.

name: shinobi_bracket_switch_willie-2

routes.note: White square on the line with a red down-arrow attacking the backfield
White square on the line with a red down-arrow
White square on the line with a red down-arrow
White square on the line with a red down-arrow
Single blue deep zone bubble in the top right with a line down to a defender near the 20

routes.receiver: Coverage defenders (white X and dot markers)
routes.note: Static markers spread across the middle and second level, no drawn coverage lines (MAN label)

reads: If the offense drops to pass, the four squares rush the drawn arrows and pressure the pocket.
If you are a coverage defender, lock your man and trail through the route since the badge reads MAN.
If a receiver releases vertical to that side, the deep bracket defender caps it over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: dropback passing with a bracket on one side. Coach's Key: badge reads MAN. Only the four red rush arrows and the single deep bracket are drawn, the rest are static man markers.

name: shinobi_cover_4_palms-4

routes.note: Four blue deep zones, corners read #2 to trap or carry
Purple zone left
Olive zone over the middle
Purple zone right

reads: IF #2 goes to the flat, THEN the corner palms (traps) #1 underneath and the safety takes #1 deep.
IF #2 runs vertical, THEN the safety carries #2 and the corner stays on #1.
IF everything stays underneath, THEN the purple and olive zones squeeze the short windows.
usage_notes: Beats: smash and flat-vertical combos. Coach's Key: corners read #2, trap the flat, carry the vertical, never get out-leveraged.

name: shinobi_3_double_buzz-2

routes.note: Three blue deep zones splitting the field into thirds
Two defenders buzz down (blue drop lines into the gray hook zones over the middle)
Two purple zones dropping to the underneath flats

reads: IF the snap comes, THEN three defenders bail to deep thirds while two buzz the middle hooks.
IF a crosser or dig works the middle, THEN the double buzz defenders wall it from both sides.
IF the throw goes to the flat, THEN the purple flat defenders trigger and tackle.
usage_notes: Beats: crossers and dig routes over the middle. Coach's Key: two underneath defenders buzz to double up the middle hole.

name: shinobi_dbl_safety_blitz-3

routes.note: Two red arrows from the deep middle racing down off the edges to the QB
Additional red arrows from the box square markers attacking inside

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN both safeties trigger downhill on the all-out pressure.
IF the protection slides one way, THEN the unblocked safety should come free clean.
IF the QB tries to escape, THEN the edge rushers contain and the box squeezes the pocket.
usage_notes: Beats: empty backfields and max-protect that leaves a safety unblocked. Coach's Key: zero-coverage pressure, bring both safeties, get there now.

name: shinobi_db_blitz_0

routes.note: Two red arrows from the outside DB spots firing into the backfield
Four red arrows from the box square markers attacking the LOS

reads: IF the ball is snapped, THEN everyone in the box plus the edge DBs rush with no deep help.
IF the offense keeps in extra blockers, THEN someone still comes free on Cover 0.
IF the QB gets the ball out hot, THEN the man defenders behind it must win one on one.
usage_notes: Beats: long-developing routes and shaky protections. Coach's Key: Cover 0 all-out blitz, no safety help, win up front or it is a touchdown.

name: shinobi_cover_1_contain-2

routes.note: One blue deep zone over the top
Brown zone over the middle spying the QB and the run
White X defenders aligned in man across the formation

reads: IF a receiver releases, THEN man defenders travel with their assignment.
IF the QB tries to scramble, THEN the spy mirrors him and keeps him contained.
IF the ball goes deep, THEN the single-high safety helps over the top.
usage_notes: Beats: scrambling quarterbacks and designed QB runs. Coach's Key: man everywhere, one deep, keep a contain spy on the QB.

name: shinobi_cover_2_hard_flat-2

routes.note: Two blue deep zones split the deep field in half
Two gray hook zones over the middle
Olive zone carrying the middle
Two teal/cyan zones jumping the underneath flats aggressively

reads: IF a receiver releases to the flat, THEN the hard flat defenders sit on it and undercut.
IF a vertical threatens the sideline, THEN the deep-half safeties cap it over the top.
IF a dig or hook shows, THEN the middle zones wall it off.
usage_notes: Beats: quick outs and flat routes. Coach's Key: two deep, corners squat hard on the flat, jump the quick game.

name: shinobi_overload_3_seam-3

routes.note: three blue zones up top, three-deep shell
purple zones each side and an olive hook in the middle (yellow drop)
multiple red arrows stacked to one side overloading a gap

reads: IF the rush overloads one side, THEN the protection can be outnumbered there, attack away from the overload.
IF three deep plays behind it, THEN do not force the deep ball, work the soft underneath.
IF the seams are buzzed, THEN dump to the flat or check down quickly.
usage_notes: Beats: quick throws and hot routes to the side away from the overload. Coach's Key: three-deep seam coverage with a one-sided overload pressure.

name: shinobi_tampa_2-15

routes.note: two blue zones up top split the deep field in halves
olive zone runs up the middle seam (yellow drop), the Tampa 2 rat
teal zones outside, sink and protect the curl-flat
grey zones drop to the hooks
four red arrows rush the passer

reads: IF the Mike runs the deep middle, THEN the seam hole is capped, do not force the vertical up the pipe.
IF two safeties play halves, THEN attack the sideline behind the corners between the deep defenders.
IF four rush, THEN the underneath flats are open, take the easy throw if the seam is covered.
usage_notes: Beats: deep comebacks and sideline routes outside the safeties. Coach's Key: classic Tampa 2, Mike carries the deep middle seam.

name: shinobi_strong_corner_2_fire

routes.note: two blue zones up top split the deep field in halves
teal and olive zones across the middle, cyan drop lines into the curl-flat
red arrows rush, the strong-side corner fires off the edge

reads: IF the strong corner fires, THEN that flat is vacated late, hit it quick before the buzz arrives.
IF two deep halves stay back, THEN the middle hole sits under the safeties, work the seam.
IF the underneath buzzes, THEN take the quick out or check down away from the corner pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: the strong-side flat thrown fast, and the deep middle hole. Coach's Key: two-deep with a strong corner blitz, buzz coverage behind it.

name: shinobi_dbl_safety_go

routes.note: two red arrows from depth crash down off each edge (the double safety go)
four more red arrows from the front, six total pressure with no deep zones drawn

reads: IF both safeties come off the edges, THEN it is all-out pressure, the ball must come out now.
IF no deep zones are drawn, THEN coverage is man with no help, expect single coverage everywhere.
IF you can find the one-on-one, THEN take your best matchup before the rush gets home.
usage_notes: Beats: a quick hot throw beating the all-out blitz, or a back-shoulder on the isolated matchup. Coach's Key: zero-help double safety blitz, six rushers, get the ball out fast.

name: shinobi_okie_eagle_3

routes.note: three blue zones across the top, three-deep shell
purple zone left, tan hook zones in the middle (white drop lines), teal zone right
red arrows attack from the eagle alignment, edge and interior rushers

reads: IF the eagle front rushes, THEN the gaps are slanted, expect the pressure to angle to one side.
IF three deep plays behind it, THEN the deep ball is capped, work the underneath windows.
IF the hooks drop, THEN take the flat or check down off the pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: quick game and underneath throws against the eagle-front pressure. Coach's Key: three-deep zone behind an Okie eagle front.

name: shinobi_str_eagle_slant_3_blitz

routes.note: Red arrow driving forward from the left of the line of scrimmage
Red arrow angling forward toward the gap from the left-middle
Red arrow angling forward toward the gap from the right-middle
Red arrow driving straight forward on the right side
Blue drop line up to the left deep blue zone bubble
Blue drop line up to the middle deep blue zone bubble
Blue drop line up to the right deep blue zone bubble
Purple drop line dropping out to the left intermediate purple zone bubble
Purple drop line dropping out to the right intermediate purple zone bubble
Yellow drop line into the central green hook zone bubble

reads: Pre-snap: confirm the 4 red rushers and an eagle front slant before the snap.
IF you have a free rusher off the edge, THEN get home fast and force the ball out early.
IF the QB holds it, THEN the 3 deep zones and 3 underneath zones squeeze every window.
IF the back releases, THEN the nearest underneath flat defender carries him while the middle hook walls the inside.
usage_notes: Beats: dropback pass and slow-developing concepts. Coach's Key: this is a 4-man zone blitz behind a 3-deep, 3-under shell, the front slants and brings four while seven drop into Cover 3 zones.

name: shinobi_fs_zone_blitz-2

routes.note: three blue zones up top, three-deep shell as the free safety pressures
purple zones each side and an olive hook in the middle (yellow drop)
multiple red arrows, the free safety blitzes while a lineman drops

reads: IF the free safety blitzes, THEN a front defender drops out, watch for the dropped lineman in the throwing lane.
IF three deep rotates behind it, THEN do not force the vertical, attack the soft underneath.
IF the hook drops, THEN throw hot to the flat or check down off the safety pressure.
usage_notes: Beats: quick hot throws and flats against the safety pressure. Coach's Key: three-deep zone blitz with a free safety pressure and a dropping front defender.

name: shinobi_cover_6_willie-6

routes.note: Three blue deep zones across the back end (quarter-quarter-half split)
Purple curl-flat drop on the left
Interior defender carries the middle hook (yellow)
Short underneath zone drops shown in the box (red)
